ENTRY.name: TRIBE
ENTRY.STRING: {NATION}: Click here to select which nation you will play. In a single player game you can also select the nation for each computer player.

ENTRY.name: TRIBEX
ENTRY.STRING: {NATION}: Shows what nation this player will control.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOM
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts. The nationality will be hidden from enemies until actually encountered on the map during the game.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOMOFFENSIVE
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM OFFENSIVE NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts. The nationality will be hidden from enemies until actually encountered on the map during the game. The nation selected will have a good offensive capability.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOMDEFENSIVE
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM DEFENSIVE NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts. The nationality will be hidden from enemies until actually encountered on the map during the game. The nation selected will be suitable for defensive situations.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOMECONOMIC
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM ECONOMIC NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts. The nationality will be hidden from enemies until actually encountered on the map during the game. The nation selected will have strong economic potential.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOMXPACK
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM X-PACK NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts. The nationality will be hidden from enemies until actually encountered on the map during the game. One of the new X-Pack nations will be selected.

ENTRY.name: TRIBERANDOMCTW
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM NATION}: A nation will be randomly selected when the game starts.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE0
ENTRY.STRING: {The Aztecs have the Power of Sacrifice.} Gain $NUMBER0 bonus resources per Age when your Barracks, Stable, and Dock units slay an enemy (maximum $NUMBER3). Plunder from enemy buildings increased by $NUMBER1%. Start with one Military research at the Library. Receive free Light Infantry whenever you build a new Barracks (one at start, two with Classical Age plus a Military research, and three with Gunpowder Age plus 3 Military research).

ENTRY.name: TRIBE1
ENTRY.STRING: {The Maya have the Power of Architecture.} Cities, Towers, Forts, etc. fire extra arrows/bullets--including Cities without garrisons. Buildings $NUMBER2% tougher and all non-Wonders are $NUMBER3% faster to build. Buildings cost $NUMBER0% less Timber (except Wonders and military buildings).

ENTRY.name: TRIBE2
ENTRY.STRING: {The Inca have the Power of Gold.} Mines make Wealth as well as Metal. Wealth Commerce Limit +$NUMBER0%. When one of your units is killed by an enemy, you receive a $NUMBER1% refund.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE3
ENTRY.STRING: {The Bantu have the Power of Migration.} +$NUMBER1 City Limit (must research at least one Civic), and Cities $NUMBER5% cheaper. +$NUMBER2% Population Limit. May exceed maximum limit by $NUMBER3%. Barracks units and Citizens move $NUMBER4% faster. Unit upgrades do not require Military research.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE4
ENTRY.STRING: {The Nubians have the Power of Trade.} Merchants in your territory collect + $NUMBER0% base resources. You see rare resources in your territory. +$NUMBER1 Caravan Limit. You start with a Market and can always trade resources with a +$NUMBER4/-$NUMBER4 price bonus. Merchants, Caravans, and Markets $NUMBER2% cheaper and $NUMBER3% more hit points.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE5
ENTRY.STRING: {The Greeks have the Power of Philosophy.} Library research $NUMBER1% cheaper (except Knowledge) and $NUMBER0% faster. Can build Universities and collect Knowledge from the beginning of the game. Libraries and Universities $NUMBER4% cheaper.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE6
ENTRY.STRING: {The Romans have the Power of Caesar.} Forts $NUMBER3% cheaper and $NUMBER8% faster to build, exert +$NUMBER2 National Borders, and get free upgrades. Heavy Infantry created $NUMBER6% cheaper and $NUMBER7% faster. Start with one Military research at the Library. Receive free Heavy Infantry whenever you build a new Barracks (one with Classical Age, two with Gunpowder Age plus 3 Military research, and three with Industrial Age plus 5 Military research). Cities gather an extra +$NUMBER4 Wealth.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE7
ENTRY.STRING: {The Egyptians have the Power of the Nile.} Start with a Granary, and receive Granary upgrades for free. Food Commerce Limit +$NUMBER3%. May build $NUMBER2 Farms per City. Farms generate +$NUMBER4 Wealth. Wonders cost $NUMBER1% less, can be built an age early, and may build two per City.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE7CTW
ENTRY.STRING: {The Egyptians have the Power of the Nile.} Start with a Granary, and receive Granary upgrades for free. Food Commerce Limit +$NUMBER3%. May build $NUMBER2 Farms per City. Farms generate +$NUMBER4 Wealth. Start Conquer the World campaigns with Build Wonder bonus card and build wonders an age early on the strategic map.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE8
ENTRY.STRING: {The Turks have the Power of Siege.} All siege, artillery, and bombardment units have +$NUMBER0 range and +$NUMBER1 Line-of-Sight. Receive $NUMBER3 free Siege unit(s) whenever you build a new Siege Factory or Factory. Citizens $NUMBER5% cheaper. Conquered cities assimilated $NUMBER2% faster. Siege units upgrade for free. Military research at the Library $NUMBER4% cheaper.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE9
ENTRY.STRING: {The Spanish have the Power of Discovery.} The map begins revealed and you start with an extra Scout. (In "Revealed Map" games gain a bonus Scout). Scout line is upgraded for free and uses abilities faster. Until Industrial Age, receive a free Heavy Ship whenever you complete a Dock. Gain $NUMBER2 resources from Ruins, and +$NUMBER3 per Science level.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE10
ENTRY.STRING: {The French have the Power of Leadership.} Receive a free General each time you build a new Fort. Supply Wagons heal your troops. Receive a free Supply Wagon each time you build a new Siege Factory or Factory. Siege Factory and Factory units created $NUMBER3% cheaper and $NUMBER4% faster. They also move faster. Free Lumber Mill and upgrades. Woodcutters +$NUMBER5 Citizens. Timber Commerce Limit +$NUMBER7%.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE11
ENTRY.STRING: {The British have the Power of Empire.} +$NUMBER0% Commerce Cap. Receive double income from Taxation, and tax upgrades are 50% cheaper. Ships created $NUMBER1% faster. Get one free Fishing Boat whenever you build a new Dock. Receive Foot Archer upgrades for free. Receive free Archer(s) whenever you build a new Barracks (one at start, two with Medieval Age, and three with Gunpowder Age). +$NUMBER5 range to Forts and Towers. Anti-Aircraft units and buildings created $NUMBER3% cheaper and $NUMBER4% faster.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE12
ENTRY.STRING: {The Germans have the Power of Industry.} Granary, Lumber Mill, and Smelter production upgrades are $NUMBER7% cheaper and available sooner. Cities gather an extra +$NUMBER6 Food, Timber, and Metal. Building completion bonuses +$NUMBER5%. Submarines and Fireships built $NUMBER1% faster and $NUMBER2% cheaper. Air units created $NUMBER3% faster. Each new Airbase comes with $NUMBER4 free Fighters.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE13
ENTRY.STRING: {The Russians have the Power of the Motherland.} Attrition damage to enemy units in your territory increased by $NUMBER0%. Attrition upgrades are free. $NATIONALBORDERS0 Start with 1 free Civic research at the Library. Spies are half price. Cavalry units do +$NUMBER3% damage to enemy Supply and Artillery units. Plunder from Russian buildings goes to the Russians, not the enemy who plundered them. +$NUMBER1% Oil gathering.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE14
ENTRY.STRING: {The Chinese have the Power of Culture.} Science research at the Library $NUMBER4% cheaper. Receive Herbal Lore, Medicine, and Pharmaceuticals upgrades for free. Start with a Large City. New Cities you found become Large Cities. Citizens, Caravans, and Merchants created instantly.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE15
ENTRY.STRING: {The Japanese have the Power of Honor.} Barracks units created $NUMBER1% cheaper and $NUMBER2% faster, for each Age plus Military research. Barracks units do $NUMBER0% extra damage vs. buildings for each Age plus Military research. Ships are $NUMBER3% cheaper and Aircraft Carriers are created $NUMBER4% faster. Farms are $NUMBER5% cheaper. Farms and Fishermen produce +$NUMBER6% resources.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE16
ENTRY.STRING: {The Koreans have the Power of Tradition.} Start with a Temple and receive Religion upgrades for free. Start with an extra $NUMBER1 Citizen, receive $NUMBER3 when you build your next City, and $NUMBER5 for all future Cities. Repair is $NUMBER2% faster. Your Citizens can build and repair buildings that are under fire without penalty. Receive the Militia, Minuteman, and Partisan upgrades for free. Towers $NUMBER4% cheaper.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE17
ENTRY.STRING: {The Mongols have the Power of the Horde.} Receive free Ranged Cavalry whenever you build a new Stable or Auto Plant ($NUMBER5 at start, $NUMBER3 with two Military research, $NUMBER7 with three Military research). Mounted units created $NUMBER0% cheaper and $NUMBER1% faster. Receive +1 Food for each $NUMBER4% of world landmass controlled (times half the number of nations). Attrition to your units reduced by $NUMBER2%. Receive Forage/Supply/Logistics research free.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE18
ENTRY.STRING: {The Iroquois have the Power of the Nation.} Get one free Scout unit with each Barracks built; Scouts get free upgrades and can move through forests. First Senate built is free and builds instantly. Woodcutters also gather +2 Food each. Military units (including Scouts) in friendly territory are hidden when not attacking. All units heal when not moving or attacking in friendly territory. Barracks units have +10% hit points.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE19
ENTRY.STRING: {The Lakota have the Power of the Plains.} Receive +$NUMBER0 Food for every Citizen, Scout, and Cavalry unit (except when garrisoned) instead of building Farms and Granaries. Farms and Granaries in captured enemy cities are instantly destroyed. Borders are invisible to enemies. Can construct buildings anywhere in territory not belonging to an enemy nation. Ranged Cavalry upgrades are free.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE20
ENTRY.STRING: {The Americans have the Power of Innovation.} First Wonder is built instantly (except Space Program or Supercollider) if no other nation is also building it. Governments are free. Receive $NUMBER2 free Scholar(s) at each new University. Aircraft and Carriers are $NUMBER0% cheaper. Receive $NUMBER1 free Bombers at each Airbase from Modern Age. 25% cheaper military ground unit upgrades. Receive +3 Food, Timber, Metal, and Wealth for every non-Scout Barracks unit (except when garrisoned).

ENTRY.name: TRIBE20CTW
ENTRY.STRING:
{The Americans have the Power of Innovation.} Governments at the Senate are free. Receive $NUMBER2 free Scholar(s) at each new University. Aircraft and Carriers are $NUMBER0% cheaper. Receive $NUMBER1 free Bomber(s) at each Airbase starting in Modern Age (these don't count against ramp-up cost).
20% cheaper military unit upgrades. Receive +3 Food, Timber, Metal, and Wealth for every Barracks unit (except when garrisoned). Start Conquer the World campaigns with a Build Wonder Bonus Card.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE21
ENTRY.STRING: {The Indians have the Power of Majesty.} Buildings do not rise in cost except for Wonders, Towers, Lookouts, and Forts, which rise at half the normal rate, and Cities, which rise normally. +$NUMBER0 City economic radius.+$NUMBER1% Caravan income. War Elephant upgrades are free, and War Elephant units are $NUMBER2% cheaper.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE22
ENTRY.STRING: {The Dutch have the Power of Commerce.} Start with a free Commerce research at Library. Receive $NUMBER0% discount on all future Commerce research. Start with a Market and 2 free Merchants.For every 100 of a resource in your treasury beyond starting level, receive +$NUMBER1 income for that resource (except Knowledge); this income can exceed Commerce Cap by 50. Receive $NUMBER2 free Light Ships whenever you complete a Dock. Ship upgrades are 10% cheaper.

ENTRY.name: TRIBE23
ENTRY.STRING: {The Persians have the Power of Ceremony.} Start with $NUMBER0% bonus Food. Second City built acts as a second Capital. Both receive Capital border bonus, and both must be captured to eliminate you. Receive a $NUMBER1% discount on Civic research. Receive Taxation upgrades for free. You always have the maximum number of Caravans allowed.

ENTRY.name: AGETECH
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. To advance in Age, click the buttons with roman numerals in the top row of your Library.

ENTRY.name: MILITARY
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON24 {Military} research increases your #ICON20 {Population Limit}. Most military units and buildings require military research. It also decreases unit and unit upgrade costs.

ENTRY.name: SCIENCEV
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON27 {Science} research decreases the {Research Cost} for all technology by $NUMBER0% and speeds the research time for them by $NUMBER1%. Science research also increases the {Line-of-Sight} for all Scouts, Cities, and Lookouts. It also increases the bonus for exploring Ruins. Most economic upgrades require Science research.

ENTRY.name: SCIENCENV
ENTRY.STRING: {Science} research decreases your {Research Cost} for all technology. It also allows your Scouts, Cities, and Lookouts to {see farther}, and increases the bonus for exploring Ruins. Most economic upgrades require Science research.

ENTRY.name: COMMERCE
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON26 {Commerce} research increases your #ICON21 {Commerce Limit}, which controls your {Efficiency}--the maximum rate at which you are allowed to gather any resource. It also increases your #ICON9 {Caravan Limit}, the maximum number of trade routes you can have.

ENTRY.name: CIVIC
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON25 {Civic} research increases the #ICON22 {number of Cities} you are allowed to build and expands your {National Borders}.

ENTRY.name: AGE0
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. In most games your nation begins in the {Ancient Age} with little technology and must make its way forward by researching technologies at the Library.

ENTRY.name: AGE1
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON28 {Classical Age} makes new resources available (#ICON4 Knowledge and #ICON5 Metal). It also allows you to build a Senate for government powers, Forts for defense, and Stables and Siege Factories to create powerful new kinds of units.

ENTRY.name: AGE2
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON29 {Medieval Age} allows you to arm your troops with more powerful weapons. It also allows you to build Supply Wagons to support your troops and Spies to subvert your enemies.

ENTRY.name: AGE3
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON30 {Gunpowder Age} makes powerful gunpowder units available.

ENTRY.name: AGE4
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON31 {Enlightenment Age} makes powerful new musket and cannon units available.

ENTRY.name: AGE5
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON32 {Industrial Age} gives you access to a new resource #ICON12 (Oil) and makes Tanks and Aircraft available for the first time.

ENTRY.name: AGE6
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON33 {Modern Age} gives you access to powerful modern units and buildings, including the ability to research nuclear weapons.

ENTRY.name: AGE7
ENTRY.STRING: {Age} advances represent your nation's progress through history; they make new units and buildings available. Advancing to the #ICON34 {Information Age} brings you to the pinnacle of information technology--the game's most powerful units and buildings at last become available.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK0
ENTRY.STRING: {SAVE TO PROFILE}: Check this box to save your profile. The current settings will become the default the next time you play.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK1
ENTRY.STRING: {TURBO RESOURCES}: Check this box to increase the resource gather rate. This option is for players who want to de-emphasize resource collection and spend more time with other parts of the game.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK2
ENTRY.STRING: {NO NATION POWERS}: Check this box to turn off the special national abilities (such as the Romans receiving the Power of Caesar).

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK3
ENTRY.STRING: {NO UNIQUE UNITS}: Check this box to use only the standard set of units. No nations receive unique units (such as the Japanese Samurai unit).

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK4
ENTRY.STRING: {COOPERATIVE AI}: A Computer player will share control of your nation with you.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK5
ENTRY.STRING: {ALLOW OBSERVER CHAT}: When checked, allows observers to chat to all players by pressing Shift+ENTER; when unchecked observers cannot chat to players during the game.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK6
ENTRY.STRING: {FREE PAUSE}: Check this box to allow players to scroll their screens and give orders when game is paused. This option is always active during solo games, but must be selected here to be used in multiplayer games.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK7
ENTRY.STRING: {TOURNAMENT PLAY}: Check this box to hide players' nation picks before the game and hide players' scores during the game. Players who resign early are not shown the entire map until the game is over.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK8
ENTRY.STRING: {LOCK GAME SPEED}: If the game speed is locked, players cannot change the speed once the game starts. This option is required for {Cannon Time}.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK9
ENTRY.STRING: {REQUIRE RANDOM TEAMS}: Requiring Random Teams prevents players from choosing their teammates--they must pick {"?"}.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK10
ENTRY.STRING: {REQUIRE RANDOM NATIONS}: Requiring Random Nations prevents players from choosing a specific nation to play--they must pick {Random}.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK11
ENTRY.STRING: {SHARE CONTROL OF TEAM}: Allows all players on a team to control the units of any of the team's nations.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK12
ENTRY.STRING: {ONLY RANKED}: When checked, only Ranked games will appear in the lobby browser.

ENTRY.name: SETUPCHECK13
ENTRY.STRING: {ONLY UNRANKED}: When checked, only Unranked games will appear in the lobby browser.

ENTRY.name: NAME
ENTRY.STRING: {PLAYER}: This shows who will control each nation. In a multiplayer game, the host can select whether each slot is Open, Closed, or Computer.

ENTRY.name: NAMEX
ENTRY.STRING: {PLAYER}: This shows who will control each nation. In a multiplayer game, the host can select whether each slot is Open, Closed, or Computer.

ENTRY.name: NAME0
ENTRY.STRING: {HUMAN}: A human player will control this nation. If you set multiple player slots to {Human} in a solo game, you will be able to switch between controlling two or more nations--just click on a unit or building of one of the other {Human} nations to take control of it.

ENTRY.name: NAME1
ENTRY.STRING: {COMPUTER}: A computer player will control this nation.

ENTRY.name: NAME2
ENTRY.STRING: {COMPUTER (Rush)}: A computer player will control this nation. It will build quickly and aggressively, and will try to attack early.

ENTRY.name: NAME3
ENTRY.STRING: {COMPUTER (Boom)}: A computer player will control this nation. It will prefer to focus on building a strong economy before attacking in force.

ENTRY.name: NAME4
ENTRY.STRING: {OPEN}: Open means this slot is open for players to join the game.

ENTRY.name: NAME5
ENTRY.STRING: {CLOSED}: Closed means this slot is not available for players to join the game.

ENTRY.name: NAME6
ENTRY.STRING: {NONE}: Removes this player from the game--you'll play with one less opponent or ally. If you want to add {more} nations to the game, increase the {Other Nations} game setting.

ENTRY.name: NAME7
ENTRY.STRING: {BOOT PLAYER}: Ejects this human player from the game.

ENTRY.name: NAME8
ENTRY.STRING: {HUMAN CONTROL ALL}: Sets all nations to human control. You will be able to switch back and forth between nations by clicking on any unit or building of the nation you want to control.

ENTRY.name: NAME9
ENTRY.STRING: {COMPUTER CONTROL ALL}: Sets all nations to computer control, so that you can watch the computer play against itself.

ENTRY.name: HANDICAP
ENTRY.STRING: {SKILL}: Click here to set the handicap for this player. In a multiplayer game, players who are more experienced can increase their handicap setting in order to balance teams. Higher skill settings increase the rate at which a player's OPPONENTS gather resources.

ENTRY.name: HANDICAPX
ENTRY.STRING: {SKILL}: In a multiplayer game, players who are more experienced can increase their handicap setting in order to balance teams. Higher skill settings increase the rate at which a player's OPPONENTS gather resources.

ENTRY.name: WHO
ENTRY.STRING: {COLOR}: Click here to select your player color. If two players take the same color, they will play a cooperative game sharing control of the same pieces.

ENTRY.name: WHOX
ENTRY.STRING: {COLOR}: Shows this player's color. If two players take the same color, they will play a cooperative game sharing control of the same pieces.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS AND ALLIES}: Click here to select whether and how nations will be organized into teams--and whether alliances can shift during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLEX
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS AND ALLIES}: Controls how nations are organized into teams and whether alliances can shift during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE0
ENTRY.STRING: {DIPLOMACY}: Nations begin alone but can forge treaties and alliances.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE1
ENTRY.STRING: {SURVIVAL OF THE FITTEST}: Every nation for itself; no alliances allowed.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE2
ENTRY.STRING: {ASSASSIN}: Our recommended "Free For All" format! No alliances allowed AND each nation is assigned a designated target nation and may only enter the territory of that enemy. Entering the territory of a nation which isn't your target results in severe attrition damage. When a nation is eliminated, the nation which was targeting it is assigned the eliminated nation's target.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE3
ENTRY.STRING: {BARBARIANS AT THE GATES}: Great for those who like to be ganged up on! A group of barbarian nations attempts to destroy a single defending nation before the time runs out. The barbarian nations can be given lower technology and resource levels than the defenders. In a solo game you play against all of the computer players; in a multiplayer game players can choose any sides (Team 1 defends, Team 2 attacks).

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE4
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS OF 2}: Preset alliances of 2 nations each. Players cannot change affiliation during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE5
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS OF 3}: Preset alliances of 3 nations each. Players cannot change affiliation during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE6
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS OF 4}: Preset alliances of 4 nations each. Players cannot change affiliation during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE7
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAM DIPLOMACY}: (Multiplayer Only)}: Preset teams with several neutral nations available for alliances. Only the neutral nations can change alliances during the game. Each team can attempt to woo neutral nations to its side through diplomacy.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE8
ENTRY.STRING: {COOPERATIVE DIPLOMACY}: (Multiplayer Only) Pairs of players share control of each nation. Nations begin alone but can forge alliances.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE9
ENTRY.STRING: {COOPERATIVE SURVIVAL}: (Multiplayer Only) Pairs of players share cooperative control of each nation, but no alliances are allowed between nations.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE10
ENTRY.STRING: {COOPERATIVE TEAMS}: (Multiplayer Only) Pairs of players share cooperative control of each nation, and nations begin the game allied in teams of 2 nations each. Players cannot change affiliations during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE11
ENTRY.STRING: {CUSTOM DIPLOMACY}: Individually configure each player's team and cooperative settings. This allows you to create custom situations, such as unbalanced teams. Players may freely make and break alliances during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE12
ENTRY.STRING: {CUSTOM TEAMS}: Individually configure each player's team and cooperative settings. This allows you to create custom situations, such as unbalanced teams. Players cannot change affiliation during the game.

ENTRY.name: GAMESTYLE13
ENTRY.STRING: {SAVED GAME}: Restore a previously saved game.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES
ENTRY.STRING: {RULES}: Click here to select whether the game will be played with standard or customized rules.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULESX
ENTRY.STRING: {RULES}: Controls whether the game is played with standard or customized rules.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES0
ENTRY.STRING: {STANDARD}: Standard rules are recommended for newcomers and players who wish to play the most common configuration of the game. In a standard game, a player or team can win by controlling 70% of the world's territory, building and holding 8 more Wonder points than their opponents, or conquering all enemies. Enemies can be eliminated by capturing and holding their capitals.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES1
ENTRY.STRING: {CUSTOM}: Custom rules activates new options, which allow the rules of the game to be customized.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES2
ENTRY.STRING: {NO RUSH}: Attacking is not allowed until at least one player reaches the Gunpowder Age.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES3
ENTRY.STRING: {SUDDEN DEATH}: Players lose instantly at the moment any of their cities are captured and win instantly by building enough wonders or controlling 70% of the world territory.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES4
ENTRY.STRING: {DEATHMATCH}: Players start with a huge supply of each resource.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES5
ENTRY.STRING: {NOMAD}: Players do not begin the game with a City or a Scout. Nation powers are not activated until first City is built.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES6
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACEFUL TECH RACE}: Players may not fight; the winner is the first player to reach the selected end Age or the Information Age.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES7
ENTRY.STRING: {ASSASSIN}: Our recommended "Free For All" format! No alliances allowed AND each nation is assigned a designated target nation and may only enter the territory of that enemy. Entering the territory of a nation that isn't your target results in severe attrition damage. When a nation is eliminated, the nation that was targeting it is assigned the eliminated nation's target.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES8
ENTRY.STRING: {BARBARIANS AT THE GATES}: Great for those who like to be ganged up on! A group of barbarian nations attempts to destroy a single defending nation before the time runs out. The barbarian nations can be given lower technology and resource levels than the defenders. In Multiplayer games, Team 1 defends and Team 2 attacks.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES9
ENTRY.STRING: {FAST GAME}: Technology can be researched quickly and resources are produced swiftly (as if Turbo Resources were selected).

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES10
ENTRY.STRING: {SCENARIO}: Select a custom multiplayer scenario. Each [scenarioname] listed corresponds to a parallel ./scenario/Custom/[scenarioname]/[scenarioname].scx file.

ENTRY.name: GAMERULES11
ENTRY.STRING: {INFO AGE DEATHMATCH}: Players start with a huge supply of each resource and all technologies researched. No future technology can be researched and no nuclear weapons can be built.

ENTRY.name: GAMESPEEDS0
ENTRY.STRING: {VERY SLOW}: Sets the game speed to very slow for longer games.

ENTRY.name: GAMESPEEDS1
ENTRY.STRING: {SLOW}: Sets the game speed to slow.

ENTRY.name: GAMESPEEDS3
ENTRY.STRING: {FAST}: Sets the game speed to fast.

ENTRY.name: GAMESPEEDS4
ENTRY.STRING: {HYPER FAST} Sets the game speed to hyper fast.

ENTRY.name: GAMESPEEDS2
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL}: Sets the game speed to normal.

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME
ENTRY.STRING: {CANNON TIME}: Click to allow each player to use "super slow motion" for brief periods during a multiplayer game.

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIMEX
ENTRY.STRING: {CANNON TIME}: Controls how often each player may use "super slow motion" for brief periods during a multiplayer game.

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME0
ENTRY.STRING: {NONE}: No "super slow motion" allowed.

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME1
ENTRY.STRING: {1 CANNON TIME}: Each player may use one 15 second burst of "super slow motion".

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME2
ENTRY.STRING: {2 CANNON TIMES}: Each player may use two 15 second bursts of "super slow motion".

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME3
ENTRY.STRING: {4 CANNON TIMES}: Each player may use four 15 second bursts of "super slow motion".

ENTRY.name: CANNONTIME4
ENTRY.STRING: {8 CANNON TIMES}: Each player may use eight 15 second bursts of "super slow motion".

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES
ENTRY.STRING: {RUSH RULES}: Click to determine whether and when "rushing" (early attacking) is allowed.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULESX
ENTRY.STRING: {RUSH RULES}: Controls whether and when "rushing" (early attacking) is allowed.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULESXS
ENTRY.STRING: {RUSH RULES}: Controls whether and when "rushing" (early attacking) is allowed. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES0
ENTRY.STRING: {STANDARD}: Standard rush rules mean players may attack each other at any time.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES1
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL CLASSICAL AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Classical Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES2
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL MEDIEVAL AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Medieval Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES3
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL GUNPOWDER AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Gunpowder Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES4
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL ENLIGHTENMENT AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Enlightenment Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES5
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL INDUSTRIAL AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Industrial Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES6
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL MODERN AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Modern Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES7
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE UNTIL INFORMATION AGE}: Players cannot attack one another until at least one player has reached the Information Age.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES8
ENTRY.STRING: {NON-VIOLENT}: Players cannot attack one another at any time.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES9
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 2 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 2 minutes.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES10
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 5 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 5 minutes.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES11
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 10 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 10 minutes.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES12
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 15 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 15 minutes.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES13
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 20 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 20 minutes.

ENTRY.name: RUSHRULES14
ENTRY.STRING: {PEACE FOR FIRST 30 MINUTES}: Players cannot attack one another for the first 30 minutes.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP STYLE}: Click here to select a style of map to play on. Each map style makes for a different kind of game and a differing strategic situation.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLEX
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP STYLE}: The style of map on which the game will be played. Each map style makes for a different kind of game and a differing strategic situation.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE0
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. The map style chosen will not be New World or Colonial Powers.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE1
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM LAND MAP}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. A "land map" will always be selected, so all players will begin the game on the same land mass and although there may be water on the map there will be no land masses separated by water.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE2
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM SEA MAP}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. A "sea map" will always be selected, so at least some land masses will be separated by sea (player starting locations may or may not be separated by sea). The map style chosen will not be Colonial Powers.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE3
ENTRY.STRING: {SECRET RANDOM}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. The map type will be concealed throughout the game and can only be discovered through exploration.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE4
ENTRY.STRING: {SECRET RANDOM LAND MAP}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. A "land map" will always be selected, so all players will begin the game on the same land mass. The map type will be concealed throughout the game and can only be discovered through exploration.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE5
ENTRY.STRING: {SECRET RANDOM SEA MAP}: Selects a map style randomly when the game starts. A "sea map" will always be selected, so at least some players will be separated by sea. The map type will be concealed throughout the game and can only be discovered through exploration.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE6
ENTRY.STRING: {OLD WORLD}: One huge continent with everyone on it. Rivers, forests, and mountains create tactical challenges.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE7
ENTRY.STRING: {GREAT SAHARA}: Desert land map. Only a few oases hold trees, but you'll find plenty of oil in the Industrial Age.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE8
ENTRY.STRING: {AMAZON RAINFOREST}: Plenty of resources and ruins hidden in a maze of tropical rivers and jungle clearings.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE9
ENTRY.STRING: {HIMALAYAS}: Land map with lots of mountains and mountain passes in the center and forests around the edge.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE10
ENTRY.STRING: {SOUTHWEST MESA}: Desert land map with lots of cliffs and mesas. Battle for the high ground!

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE11
ENTRY.STRING: {AFRICAN WATERING HOLE}: All the rare resources lie around the lone watering hole at the center of the map. Happy hunting!

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE12
ENTRY.STRING: {MEDITERRANEAN}: Land surrounding a central ocean with a small island.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE13
ENTRY.STRING: {AUSTRALIAN OUTBACK}: Harsh Desert or Lush Jungle--surrounded by ocean teeming with fish.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE14
ENTRY.STRING: {GREAT LAKES}: Fertile land map filled with large lakes useful for fishing, naval power, and infiltrating armies.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE15
ENTRY.STRING: {WARRING STATES}: One large continent with everyone, surrounded by an archipelago. Continent is long like Japan.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE16
ENTRY.STRING: {NEW WORLD}: Each nation gets a cramped island and must exploit the rich mainland.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE17
ENTRY.STRING: {COLONIAL POWERS}: Two large continents with all the players on one! Note}: in Team Diplomacy games all the neutral computer players go on the other continent.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE18
ENTRY.STRING: {EAST INDIES}: Lots of islands; lots of resources. Bring your navy.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE19
ENTRY.STRING: {EAST MEETS WEST}: Each team shares its own large continent.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE20
ENTRY.STRING: {ATLANTIC SEA POWER}: Each nation gets its own landmass; players are separated by a large ocean.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE21
ENTRY.STRING: {NILE DELTA}: Players face off across a major river.

ENTRY.name: MAPSTYLE22
ENTRY.STRING: {BRITISH ISLES}: Nations start on variably-sized islands which may or may not isolate them from their allies. When it's time to drill for Oil, you'll need to build offshore platforms.

ENTRY.name: TEAM
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAM}: Click here to join a team. If at least two players start on the same team, they will begin the game as allies. Players who select {?} will be assigned to a random team when the game starts.

ENTRY.name: TEAMX
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAM}: Shows what team this player will be on. If at least two players start on the same team, they will begin the game as allies. Players who select {?} will be assigned to a random team when the game starts.

ENTRY.name: SIZE
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP SIZE}: Click here to select the size of the map. Smaller maps make for quicker games; larger maps delay the initial contact between nations.

ENTRY.name: SIZEX
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP SIZE}: Controls the size of the map. Smaller maps make for quicker games; larger maps delay the initial contact between nations.

ENTRY.name: SIZE0
ENTRY.STRING: {TINY} maps are good for two-player games with rapid contact.

ENTRY.name: SIZE1
ENTRY.STRING: {ARENA} maps make for intense two-player competitions: the players aren't far apart, so conflict is fast and furious.

ENTRY.name: SIZE2
ENTRY.STRING: {SMALL} maps are good for two- and three-player games.

ENTRY.name: SIZE3
ENTRY.STRING: {STANDARD} maps are good for four-player games.

ENTRY.name: SIZE4
ENTRY.STRING: {LARGE} maps are good for five- and six-player games.

ENTRY.name: SIZE5
ENTRY.STRING: {HUGE} maps are good for seven and eight-player games.

ENTRY.name: SIZE6
ENTRY.STRING: {BIG HUGE} maps are good for large numbers of players and when you want initial contact to be slow. We also think they have the coolest name.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY
ENTRY.STRING: {DIFFICULTY}: Click here to select a difficulty level. This determines the efficiency and aggressive nature of the computer players.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTYX
ENTRY.STRING: {DIFFICULTY}: Determines the efficiency and aggressive nature of the computer players.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY0
ENTRY.STRING: {EASIEST} level means computer nations play slowly and inefficiently; they will only rarely attack. In a solo game you also gather resources much more quickly than the computer. This is a good level to play when you're first learning the game.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY1
ENTRY.STRING: {EASY} level means computer nations play slowly and inefficiently. They will occasionally attack, but rarely in force. In a solo game you also gather resources a bit more quickly than the computer. A good level if you're a new player but have finished learning the basics.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY2
ENTRY.STRING: {MODERATE} level means computer nations will sometimes behave aggressively and attack with small armies, but will not play with maximum efficiency. Neither you nor the computer receive any special resource bonus. A good level for the intermediate player or advanced beginner.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY3
ENTRY.STRING: {TOUGH} level means you play on an equal footing with the computer. Computer nations will play efficiently and act aggressively, attacking with sizeable armies. A good level for advanced intermediate players looking for a "fair game".

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY4
ENTRY.STRING: {TOUGHER} level means computer nations will play with brutal efficiency and attack with massive armies. Computer nations don't "cheat" outright, but they receive a significant resource collection handicap, so watch out! A good level for advanced players looking for a real challenge.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY5
ENTRY.STRING: {TOUGHEST} level means computer nations will not only play their best game, they'll also receive a whopping resource collection handicap. A sparring partner for the tournament level player, or perhaps the mentally deranged.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWN
ENTRY.STRING: {START WITH}: Click here to set how many buildings each nation starts with at the beginning of the game.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWNX
ENTRY.STRING: {START WITH}: Controls how many buildings each nation starts with at the beginning of the game.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWNXS
ENTRY.STRING: {START WITH}: Controls how many buildings each nation starts with at the beginning of the game. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWN0
ENTRY.STRING: {NOMAD}: Nations begin with only a few citizens and must build their first cities themselves.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWN1
ENTRY.STRING: {CITY CENTER ONLY}: Nations begin with a bare city, a few citizens, and a scout.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWN2
ENTRY.STRING: {SMALL TOWN}: Nations begin with a city and several buildings in addition to citizens and a scout.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTOWN3
ENTRY.STRING: {LARGE TOWN}: Nations begin with a well-developed city in addition to citizens and a scout.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: {RESOURCES}: Click here to select the amount of each basic resource you begin the game with (and the amount you receive when each new resource type is discovered).

ENTRY.name: RESOURCESX
ENTRY.STRING: {RESOURCES}: Controls the amount of each basic resources players begin the game with (and the amount they receive when each new resource type is discovered).

ENTRY.name: RESOURCESXS
ENTRY.STRING: {RESOURCES}: Controls the amount of each basic resources players begin the game with (and the amount they receive when each new resource type is discovered). [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES0
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with very low resources (half the standard amounts).

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES1
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with standard resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES2
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with double resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES3
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with triple resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES4
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with x5 resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES5
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with x10 resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES6
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with x20 resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES7
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with x100 resources.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES8
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with infinite resources, and your resources never run out!

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES9
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with low but variable resources. Resources may begin at different levels (e.g., everyone begins with more Timber than Food), every player will begin with the same amount.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES10
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with moderate variable resources. Resources may begin at different levels (e.g., everyone begins with more Wealth than Food), but every player will begin with the same amount.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES11
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with high variable resources. Resources may begin at different levels (e.g., everyone begins with more Wealth than Food), but every player will begin with the same amount.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES12
ENTRY.STRING: Nations begin the game with a random resource setting! In some games resources may begin at different levels (e.g., everyone begins with more Wealth than Food), but every player will begin with the same amount.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER RESOURCE BONUS}: Click here to set the bonus resources given to the defending nation.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCESX
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER RESOURCE BONUS}: Controls the bonus resources given to the defending nation.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCESXS
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER RESOURCE BONUS}: Controls the bonus resources given to the defending nation. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES0
ENTRY.STRING: {SAME}: The defending nation receives the same resources as barbarian nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES1
ENTRY.STRING: {DOUBLE}: The defending nation receives twice the amount of resources as the barbarian nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES2
ENTRY.STRING: {TRIPLE}: The defending nation receives three times the amount of resources as the barbarian nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES3
ENTRY.STRING: {QUADRUPLE}: The defending nation receives four times the amount of resources as the barbarian nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERRESOURCES4
ENTRY.STRING: {QUINTUPLE}: The defending nation receives five times the amount of resources as the barbarian nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER AGE}: Click here to set the technology bonus given to the defending nation. Notwithstanding this setting, the defending nation never begins later than the "End Age".

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGYX
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER AGE}: Controls the technology bonus given to the defending nation. Notwithstanding this setting, the defending nation never begins later than the "End Age".

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGYXS
ENTRY.STRING: {DEFENDER AGE}: Controls the technology bonus given to the defending nation. Notwithstanding this setting, the defending nation never begins later than the "End Age". [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY0
ENTRY.STRING: {SAME AGE}: The defending nation begins in the same age as all other nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY1
ENTRY.STRING: {+1 AGE}: The defending nation begins one age higher than all other nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY2
ENTRY.STRING: {+2 AGE}: The defending nation begins two ages higher than all other nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY3
ENTRY.STRING: {+3 AGE}: The defending nation begins three ages higher than all other nations.

ENTRY.name: DEFENDERTECHNOLOGY4
ENTRY.STRING: {+4 AGE}: The defending nation begins four ages higher than all other nations.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST
ENTRY.STRING: {TECHNOLOGY}: Click here to adjust the resource cost of technology and the speed of research. Cheaper costs and faster speeds make it easier to advance and can result in a shorter game. Higher costs make it more difficult for nations to advance and can result in a longer game.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOSTX
ENTRY.STRING: {TECHNOLOGY}: Controls the resource cost of technology and the speed of research. Cheaper costs and faster speeds make it easier to advance and can result in a shorter game. Higher costs and slower speeds make it more difficult for nations to advance and can result in a longer game.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOSTXS
ENTRY.STRING: {TECHNOLOGY}: Controls the resource cost of technology and the speed of research. Cheaper costs and faster speeds make it easier to advance and can result in a shorter game. Higher costs and slower speeds make it more difficult for nations to advance and can result in a longer game. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST0
ENTRY.STRING: {CHEAP AND FAST} technology allows nations to advance quickly and cheaply.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST1
ENTRY.STRING: {CHEAP} technology allows nations to advance cheaply.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST2
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL AND FAST} technology allows nations to research quickly but at the normal cost.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST3
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL} technology allows nations to advance at the standard cost and rate.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST4
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL AND SLOW} technology allows nations to buy research for the normal cost, but research takes place more slowly.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST5
ENTRY.STRING: {EXPENSIVE} technology makes it costly for nations to advance.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST6
ENTRY.STRING: {EXPENSIVE AND SLOW} technology makes it costly and time-consuming for nations to advance.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST7
ENTRY.STRING: {VERY EXPENSIVE} technology makes it extremely costly for nations to advance.

ENTRY.name: TECHCOST8
ENTRY.STRING: {VERY EXPENSIVE AND SLOW} technology makes it time-consuming and extremely costly for nations to advance.

ENTRY.name: SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: {GAME SPEED}: Click here to set the game speed. More experienced players may want time to pass more quickly in the game.

ENTRY.name: SPEEDX
ENTRY.STRING: {GAME SPEED}: Controls the game speed. More experienced players may want time to pass more quickly in the game.

ENTRY.name: SPEED0
ENTRY.STRING: {VERY SLOW}: Game plays at 1/3 normal speed.

ENTRY.name: SPEED1
ENTRY.STRING: {SLOW}: Game plays at 1/2 normal speed.

ENTRY.name: SPEED2
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL}: Game plays at normal speed.

ENTRY.name: SPEED3
ENTRY.STRING: {FAST}: Game plays at 4/3 normal speed.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATION
ENTRY.STRING: {ELIMINATION}: Click here to set the way players can be eliminated early from the game.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATIONX
ENTRY.STRING: {ELIMINATION}: Controls the way players can be eliminated early from the game.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATIONXS
ENTRY.STRING: {ELIMINATION}: Controls the way players can be eliminated early from the game. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATION0
ENTRY.STRING: {CONQUEST}: Players are eliminated early only when their nation is completely conquered.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATION1
ENTRY.STRING: {CAPITAL}: Players are eliminated early if their nation's capital city is captured and not quickly retaken.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATION2
ENTRY.STRING: {SUDDEN DEATH CAPITAL}: Players are eliminated instantly at the moment their nation's capital city is captured. No opportunity to recapture the city is provided.

ENTRY.name: ELIMINATION3
ENTRY.STRING: {SUDDEN DEATH}: Players are eliminated instantly at the moment any of their cities are captured. No opportunity to recapture the city is provided. The recapture of an unassimilated city doesn't count.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS
ENTRY.STRING: {MUSICAL CHAIRS}: Click here to set how often the player (or team member in a team game) with the lowest score is eliminated from the game.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRSX
ENTRY.STRING: {MUSICAL CHAIRS}: Controls how often the player (or team member in a team game) with the lowest score is eliminated from the game.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRSXS
ENTRY.STRING: {MUSICAL CHAIRS}: Controls how often the player (or team member in a team game) with the lowest score is eliminated from the game.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS0
ENTRY.STRING: {2 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 2 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS1
ENTRY.STRING: {5 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 5 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS2
ENTRY.STRING: {8 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 8 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS3
ENTRY.STRING: {10 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 10 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS4
ENTRY.STRING: {15 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 15 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS5
ENTRY.STRING: {20 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 20 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS6
ENTRY.STRING: {25 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 25 minutes.

ENTRY.name: CHAIRS7
ENTRY.STRING: {30 MINUTES}: The lowest scoring player or team member is eliminated every 30 minutes.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY
ENTRY.STRING: {VICTORY}: Click here to set the victory conditions for this game.

ENTRY.name: VICTORYX
ENTRY.STRING: {VICTORY}: Controls the victory conditions for the game.

ENTRY.name: VICTORYXS
ENTRY.STRING: {VICTORY}: Controls the victory conditions for the game. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: VICTORY0
ENTRY.STRING: {STANDARD}: Win by conquest, territory, or by building wonders. If you accumulate enough wonders or territory, a timer will start. If you still have enough wonders/territory when the timer expires, you win.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY1
ENTRY.STRING: {SUDDEN DEATH}: Win by conquest, territory, or by building wonders. If you meet the requirements for any victory condition you win instantly--no timers!

ENTRY.name: VICTORY2
ENTRY.STRING: {CONQUEST}: Win by eliminating all opposing nations. The method of elimination is determined by the {Elimination} box.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY3
ENTRY.STRING: {SCORE}: Win by being the first player or team to reach a pre-set score. The score to be reached can be set in the {Score} listbox.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY4
ENTRY.STRING: {TIME LIMIT}: When the time limit is reached, the player with the highest score wins (in a Barbarians at the Gates game, the defending team wins). The time limit can be set in the {Time} listbox.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY5
ENTRY.STRING: {MUSICAL CHAIRS}: Each time the {Musical Chairs} timer expires, the player with the lowest score is immediately eliminated from the game. In a team game one member of each team is eliminated whenever the timer expires until at least one team has only one member, after which the single lowest scoring player is eliminated.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY6
ENTRY.STRING: {WONDER}: Win by building wonders. The first player to accumulate enough wonder points wins instantly--no timers!

ENTRY.name: VICTORY7
ENTRY.STRING: {TERRITORY}: The first player/team to achieve a set percentage of world territory wins instantly--no timers!

ENTRY.name: VICTORY8
ENTRY.STRING: {ECONOMIC}: The first player/team whose average income of all resources (except Knowledge) exceeds the given rate wins. Resources which haven't been discovered yet (e.g., Oil) still count against the average.

ENTRY.name: VICTORY9
ENTRY.STRING: {TECH RACE}: The first player/team to reach the End Age wins.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT
ENTRY.STRING: {TIME LIMIT}: Click here to set the time limit for this game. The player or team with the highest score when the time limit expires wins (in a Barbarians at the Gates game, the defending team wins).

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMITX
ENTRY.STRING: {TIME LIMIT}: Controls the time limit for this game. The player or team with the highest score when the time limit expires wins (in a Barbarians at the Gates game, the defending team wins).

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT0
ENTRY.STRING: {15 MINUTES}: The game ends after 15 minutes of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT1
ENTRY.STRING: {30 MINUTES}: The game ends after 30 minutes of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT2
ENTRY.STRING: {45 MINUTES}: The game ends after 45 minutes of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT3
ENTRY.STRING: {60 MINUTES}: The game ends after 60 minutes of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT4
ENTRY.STRING: {90 MINUTES}: The game ends after 90 minutes of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT5
ENTRY.STRING: {2 HOURS}: The game ends after two hours of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT6
ENTRY.STRING: {3 HOURS}: The game ends after three hours of play.

ENTRY.name: TIMELIMIT7
ENTRY.STRING: {4 HOURS}: The game ends after four hours of play.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL
ENTRY.STRING: {SCORE GOAL}: Click here to set the score goal for this game. The first player or team to reach this score wins. In a team game the average score of all the team members much reach the goal.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOALX
ENTRY.STRING: {SCORE GOAL}: Controls the score goal for this game. The first player or team to reach this score wins. In a team game the average score of all the team members much reach the goal.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL0
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 1000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL1
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 2000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL2
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 3000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL3
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 4000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL4
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 5000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL5
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 6000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL6
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 7000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL7
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 8000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL8
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 9000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL9
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 10000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL10
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 15000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: SCOREGOAL11
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to achieve a score of 20000 points wins.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN
ENTRY.STRING: {INCOME GOAL}: Click here to set the average income rate of all resources (except Knowledge) required to win this game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWINX
ENTRY.STRING: {INCOME GOAL}: Controls the average income rate of all resources (except Knowledge) required to win this game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN0
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +100 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN1
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +200 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN2
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +300 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN3
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +400 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN4
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +500 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN5
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +600 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN6
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +700 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN7
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +800 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: ECONWIN8
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team whose resource income averages +900 wins the game.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN
ENTRY.STRING: {WONDER VICTORY}: Click here to set the number of Wonder Points a player or team must be ahead of all opponents in order to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWINX
ENTRY.STRING: {WONDER VICTORY}: Controls the number of Wonder Points a player or team must be ahead of all opponents in order to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWINXS
ENTRY.STRING: {WONDER VICTORY}: Controls the number of Wonder Points a player or team must be ahead of all opponents in order to win. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN0
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 1 more wonder point than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN1
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 2 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN2
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 3 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN3
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 4 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN4
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 6 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN5
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 8 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN6
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 10 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN7
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 12 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN8
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 14 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN9
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 16 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN10
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 20 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN11
ENTRY.STRING: Control at least 24 more wonder points than any other player/team to win.

ENTRY.name: WONDERWIN12
ENTRY.STRING: No wonder victory is allowed.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN
ENTRY.STRING: {TERRITORY GOAL}: Click here to set the percentage of the world's territory a player or team must control to win.

ENTRY.name: POPWINX
ENTRY.STRING: {TERRITORY GOAL}: Controls the percentage of the world's territory a player or team must control to win.

ENTRY.name: POPWINXS
ENTRY.STRING: {TERRITORY GOAL}: Controls the percentage of the world's territory a player or team must control to win. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: POPWIN0
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 30% of the world's territory wins. This setting is only useful in games with more than two players/teams.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN1
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 35% of the world's territory wins. This setting is only useful in games with more than two players/teams.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN2
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 40% of the world's territory wins. This setting is only useful in games with more than two players/teams.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN3
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 45% of the world's territory wins. This setting is only useful in games with more than two players/teams.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN4
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 50% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN5
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 55% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN6
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 60% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN7
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 65% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN8
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 70% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN9
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 75% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN10
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 80% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN11
ENTRY.STRING: The first player or team to control 90% of the world's territory wins.

ENTRY.name: POPWIN12
ENTRY.STRING: No territory victory is allowed in this game.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Click here to set the age for the beginning of the game. Depending on your selection, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGYX
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Controls the starting age of the nations at the beginning of the game. Depending on this setting, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGYXS
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Controls the starting age of the nations at the beginning of the game. Depending on this setting, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY0
ENTRY.STRING: {ANCIENT AGE}: Players begin with no technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY1
ENTRY.STRING: {CLASSICAL AGE}: Players begin with Classical Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY2
ENTRY.STRING: {MEDIEVAL AGE}: Players begin with Medieval Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY3
ENTRY.STRING: {GUNPOWDER AGE}: Players begin with Gunpowder Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY4
ENTRY.STRING: {ENLIGHTENMENT AGE}: Players begin with Enlightenment Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY5
ENTRY.STRING: {INDUSTRIAL AGE}: Players begin with Industrial Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY6
ENTRY.STRING: {MODERN AGE}: Players begin with Modern Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY7
ENTRY.STRING: {INFORMATION AGE}: Players begin with Information Age technology.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY8
ENTRY.STRING: {ALL}: The players begin with all technologies researched.

ENTRY.name: STARTINGTECHNOLOGY9
ENTRY.STRING: {RANDOM AGE}: The players begin in a random age--but no higher than the Ending Age.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Click here to set the starting age of the Barbarian nations. Depending on your selection, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGYX
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Controls the age in which Barbarian nations start at the beginning of the game. Depending on this setting, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGYXS
ENTRY.STRING: {START AGE}: Controls the age in which Barbarian nations start at the beginning of the game. Depending on this setting, players may begin the game with some or all of the technologies already researched. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY0
ENTRY.STRING: {ANCIENT AGE}: Barbarians begin with no technologies already researched.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY1
ENTRY.STRING: {CLASSICAL AGE}: Barbarians begin with Classical Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY2
ENTRY.STRING: {MEDIEVAL AGE}: Barbarians begin with Medieval Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY3
ENTRY.STRING: {GUNPOWDER AGE}: Barbarians begin with Gunpowder Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY4
ENTRY.STRING: {ENLIGHTENMENT AGE}: Barbarians begin with Enlightenment Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY5
ENTRY.STRING: {INDUSTRIAL AGE}: Barbarians begin with Industrial Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY6
ENTRY.STRING: {MODERN AGE}: Barbarians begin with Modern Age technology.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANTECHNOLOGY7
ENTRY.STRING: {INFORMATION AGE}: Barbarians begin with full Information Age technology--all techs have been researched.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY
ENTRY.STRING: {END AGE}: Click here to set the final age for the game--players can progress no higher than this technology level. You can use this option to create a shorter game or a game that concentrates on particular eras.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGYX
ENTRY.STRING: {END AGE}: Controls the final Age for the game--players can progress no higher than this technology level.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGYXS
ENTRY.STRING: {END AGE}: Controls the final Age for the game--players can progress no higher than this technology level. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY0
ENTRY.STRING: {CLASSICAL AGE}: Players can progress no further than Classical Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY1
ENTRY.STRING: {MEDIEVAL AGE}: Players can progress no further than Medieval Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY2
ENTRY.STRING: {GUNPOWDER AGE}: Players can progress no further than Gunpowder Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY3
ENTRY.STRING: {ENLIGHTENMENT AGE}: Players can progress no further than Enlightenment Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY4
ENTRY.STRING: {INDUSTRIAL AGE}: Players can progress no further than Industrial Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY5
ENTRY.STRING: {MODERN AGE}: Players can progress through Modern Age technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY6
ENTRY.STRING: {INFORMATION AGE}: Players can research all available technologies.

ENTRY.name: ENDINGTECHNOLOGY7
ENTRY.STRING: {EARLY INFO AGE}: Players can progress through Information Age technologies. Future technologies are not available.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAP
ENTRY.STRING: {REVEAL MAP}: Click here to determine how much of the map is visible at the start of the game and whether enemy activity can be seen when outside friendly Line-of-Sight.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAPX
ENTRY.STRING: {REVEAL MAP}: Controls how much of the map is visible at the start of the game and whether enemy activity can be seen when outside friendly Line-of-Sight.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAPXS
ENTRY.STRING: {REVEAL MAP}: Controls how much of the map is visible at the start of the game and whether enemy activity can be seen when outside friendly Line-of-Sight. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAP0
ENTRY.STRING: {NO ALLY LOS}: The map starts unexplored and must be revealed through scouting, and Science Level 2 must be researched at the Library in order to benefit from allied Line-of-Sight. Changes to the map outside the Line-of-Sight of any friendly unit are not seen.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAP1
ENTRY.STRING: {NORMAL}: The map starts unexplored and must be revealed through scouting, but you can see any of your allies' maps. Changes to the map outside the Line-of-Sight of friendly and allied units are not seen.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAP2
ENTRY.STRING: {EXPLORED}: The map starts fully explored so you can see the terrain layout (except for rare resources or ruins), but changes to the map outside the Line-of-Sight of any friendly unit are not seen.

ENTRY.name: REVEALMAP3
ENTRY.STRING: {ALL VISIBLE}: The map begins fully explored, and all enemy units and buildings are fully visible to all players.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT
ENTRY.STRING: {POPULATION}: Click here to set the maximum population limit for the game. A smaller population limit will result in smaller armies and engagements.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMITX
ENTRY.STRING: {POPULATION}: Controls the maximum population limit for the game. A smaller population limit will result in smaller armies and engagements.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMITXS
ENTRY.STRING: {POPULATION}: Controls the maximum population limit for the game. A smaller population limit will result in smaller armies and engagements. [Cannot be changed while Game Rules set to Standard.]

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT0
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 50.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT1
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 75.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT2
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 100.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT3
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 125.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT4
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 150.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT5
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 200.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT6
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 250. The last two levels of Military research provide an extra increase to your Population Limit.

ENTRY.name: POPLIMIT7
ENTRY.STRING: Nations are limited to a population of 300. The last four levels of Military research provide an extra increase to your Population Limit.

ENTRY.name: READYBOX
ENTRY.STRING: {READY BOX}: In a multiplayer game, click the {Ready} box next to your name in order to let the other players know when you are ready to begin. The host cannot launch the game until all of the other players have turned on their ready flags.

ENTRY.name: PLAYERS
ENTRY.STRING: {OTHER NATIONS}: Click here to select the number of other nations in the game. If you select zero (0), you will play a "sandbox" game without competition from other nations.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to abandon this game and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: START
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the game. In a multiplayer game, the host can only start the game once all of the other players have clicked their ready boxes.

ENTRY.name: OPTIONS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to modify your player profile and options.

ENTRY.name: RESET
ENTRY.STRING: Click to reset all settings to the defaults.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load a saved game.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message (which you have typed in the space to the left) to the other players.

ENTRY.name: TAB_SWITCH_LOBBY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to switch to the lobby.

ENTRY.name: TAB_SWITCH_STAGING
ENTRY.STRING: Click to switch to the Staging area.

ENTRY.name: INVITE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to invite your friends.

ENTRY.name: OPTIONS_SOLO
ENTRY.STRING: Click to modify your player profile and options.

ENTRY.name: RESET_SOLO
ENTRY.STRING: Click to reset all settings to the defaults.

ENTRY.name: APPLY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to apply pending changes to the game and players settings. Pending changes are marked with a transparent background. Button is disabled, if there are no pending changes.

ENTRY.name: CHAT_SEND
ENTRY.STRING: You can type a chat message in this space and then send it to the other players in the game.

ENTRY.name: PING
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the network latency (or "ping") times to other players, in milliseconds. The lower the latency, the better and faster the connection. Higher ping times will result in more "lag".

ENTRY.name: CHAT_RECEIVE
ENTRY.STRING: System messages, along with chat messages from other players, will appear here.

ENTRY.name: SCRIPT
ENTRY.STRING: Select a custom script. The scripts listed correspond to .bhs files found in the ./scenario/Scripts/ folder.

ENTRY.name: SCRIPTSX
ENTRY.STRING: Select a custom script. The scripts listed correspond to .bhs files found in the ./scenario/Scripts/ folder.

ENTRY.name: SCRIPTXS
ENTRY.STRING: Select a custom script. The scripts listed correspond to .bhs files found in the ./scenario/Scripts/ folder.

ENTRY.name: SCRIPTX
ENTRY.STRING: Select a custom script. The scripts listed correspond to .bhs files found in the ./scenario/Scripts/ folder.

ENTRY.name: MODS
ENTRY.STRING: {GAME MODS}: Click here to select whether the game will be played with standard or customized units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: MODSX
ENTRY.STRING: {GAME MODS}: Click here to select whether the game will be played with standard or customized units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: MODS0
ENTRY.STRING: {NO MOD}: The game will use the standard units and buildings

ENTRY.name: MODS1
ENTRY.STRING: {Draxsath's Population and Technology Mod TAP}: Doubles research times and resource requirements to allow for more time in lower ages. Population increases exponentially rather than in a linear fashion. (748KB)

ENTRY.name: MODS2
ENTRY.STRING: {World In Conflict Beta}: An exciting new World War 3 Mod (309040KB)

ENTRY.name: CREATE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create and host a new multiplayer game. Players can join your game through your Local Area Network (LAN), or through a Direct Internet connection if they know your IP address.

ENTRY.name: JOIN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join a game on your Local Area Network. If several games are listed in the window above, click on the one you wish to join before clicking here.

ENTRY.name: JOINIP
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join a game through a Direct Internet connection. You will need to know the IP address of the host's computer.

ENTRY.name: OBSERVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to observe a game on your Local Area Network. Observers can watch the game but do not control a nation.

ENTRY.name: OBSERVEIP
ENTRY.STRING: Click to observe a game through a Direct Internet connection. You will need to know the IP address of the host's computer. Observers can watch the game but do not control a nation.

ENTRY.name: BACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: VILLAGE
ENTRY.STRING:
ขยาย {National Borders} ของุคณ
ส้รางอาคารเศรษฐิกจในเขตนี้ไ้ด
ใ้ช {Citizens} ่กอส้รางและรวบรวมทัรพยากร
ีมรายไ้ด +$NUMBER1 #ICON2 และ +$NUMBER0 #ICON1

ENTRY.name: VILLAGEBUILD
ENTRY.STRING: หากุ่ปมส้รางเืมองเ็ปนีสเทา ุคณจะ้ตอง้คนค้วาเิ่พมเิตมี่ท้หองสุมด

ENTRY.name: CITY
ENTRY.STRING:
แ็ขงแก่รงและเปราะบาง้นอยก่วาเืมองเ็ลกๆ
ขยาย {National Borders} ของุคณ
ส้รางอาคารเศรษฐิกจในเขตนี้ไ้ด
ใ้ช {Citizens} ่กอส้รางและรวบรวมทัรพยากร
ีมรายไ้ด +$NUMBER1 #ICON2 และ +$NUMBER0 #ICON1 เิ่พมพัลงีชิวตใ้หักบอาคารุทกหัลงในัรศีมเศรษฐิกจของเืมอง

ENTRY.name: CITYBUILD
ENTRY.STRING: เืมองเ็ลกๆ ของุคณจะัอปเกรดเ็ปนเืมองให่ญโดยัอตโนัมิตเื่มอุคณส้รางอาคารี่ทแตก่ตางักนอ่ยาง้นอย้หาแ่หงในเืมองั้นนและึถงุยคคลาสิสก

ENTRY.name: METROPOLIS
ENTRY.STRING:
แ็ขงแก่รงและเปราะบาง้นอยก่วาเืมองให่ญ
ขยาย {National Borders} ของุคณ
ส้รางอาคารเศรษฐิกจในเขตนี้ไ้ด
ใ้ช {Citizens} ่กอส้รางและรวบรวมทัรพยากร
ีมรายไ้ด +$NUMBER1 #ICON2 และ +$NUMBER0 #ICON1 เิ่พมพัลงีชิวตใ้หักบอาคารุทกหัลงในัรศีมเศรษฐิกจของเืมอง

ENTRY.name: METROPOLISBUILD
ENTRY.STRING: เืมองให่ญของุคณจะัอปเกรดเ็ปนเืมองให่ญโดยัอตโนัมิตเื่มอุคณส้รางอาคารี่ทแตก่ตางักนอ่ยาง้นอยเ้กาแ่หงในเืมองั้นนและึถงุยคตัรสู้ร

ENTRY.name: FARM
ENTRY.STRING: พลเืมองหึ่นงคน่ตอฟา์รมสามารถรวบรวม #ICON1 {Food}

ENTRY.name: FARMV
ENTRY.STRING:
อุนญาตใ้หีมฟา์รมไ้ดูสงุสด $NUMBER0 ่ตอเืมอง
#ICON1สามารถเิ่พมผลผิลตอาหารไ้ดโดยการส้ราง {Granary} ในเืมองเีดยวักน

ENTRY.name: WOODCUTTER
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางใก้ล {trees} เื่พอรวบรวม #ICON2 {Timber}
ิ่ยงพลเืมองทำงานใน่คายมากเ่ทาไห่ร ุคณ็กิ่ยงสะสมไ้มไ้ดเ็รวเ่ทาั้นน

ENTRY.name: WOODCUTTERV
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่ยงีม้ตนไ้มอู่ยใก้ลๆ มากเ่ทาใด ประชาชน็กสามารถทำงานใน่คายไ้ดมากึ้ขนเ่ทาั้นน
#ICON2 ผลผิลตไ้มสามารถปัรบปุรงไ้ดโดยการส้ราง {Lumber Mills} ในเืมองของุคณ

ENTRY.name: MINE
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางเหืมองใก้ล {mountains} และ {cliffs} เื่พอรวบรวม #ICON5 {Metal}
ิ่ยงพลเืมองทำงานในเหืมองมากเ่ทาไห่ร ุคณ็กิ่ยงสะสมโลหะไ้ดเ็รวเ่ทาั้นน

ENTRY.name: MINEV
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่ยงูภเขาหืรอห้นาผาให่ญ ประชาชน็กิ่ยงสามารถุขดเหืมองไ้ดมากึ้ขนเ่ทาั้นน
#ICON5 ผลผิลตโลหะสามารถปัรบปุรงไ้ดโดยการส้ราง {Smelters} ในเืมองของุคณ

ENTRY.name: GRANARY
ENTRY.STRING: เิ่พมผลผิลต #ICON1 {Food} ของั้ทงหมด {Farms} ในเืมองเีดยวักนึ้ขน $NUMBER0%

ENTRY.name: LUMBERMILL
ENTRY.STRING: เิ่พมผลผิลต #ICON2 {Timber} ของแคม์ปคนัตดไ้มั้ทงหมดในเืมองเีดยวักนึ้ขน $NUMBER0%

ENTRY.name: SMELTER
ENTRY.STRING: เิ่พมผลผิลต #ICON5 {Metal} ของุ่ทนระเิบดั้ทงหมดในเืมองเีดยวักนึ้ขน $NUMBER0%

ENTRY.name: POWERPLANT
ENTRY.STRING:
เิ่พมผลผิลต #ICON6 {Oil} ของ่บอ้นำัมนั้ทงหมดในประเทศของุคณีอก $NUMBER0%
โรงกั่ลนเิ่พมเิตมภายในประเทศของุคณีมผลกระทบแบบ "้ซอนักน" สะสม ัดงั้นนโรงกั่ลนสองแ่หงจะเิ่พมผลผิลต้นำัมน #ICON6 ของุคณุสทิธ $NUMBER1%

ENTRY.name: BARRACKS
ENTRY.STRING: ส้รางทหารราบเื่พอโจมีตัศตูรของุคณ

ENTRY.name: BARRACKSV
ENTRY.STRING: ทหารกองทหารัรกษาการ์ณเ้ขาไป้ขางในเื่พอัรกษาพวกเขา

ENTRY.name: STABLE
ENTRY.STRING: ส้รางทหารี่ข้มาเื่พอโจมีตัศตูรของุคณ

ENTRY.name: STABLEV
ENTRY.STRING: กองทหารัรกษาการ์ณไ้ดนำห่นวยเ้ขาไปัรกษาพวกเขา

ENTRY.name: AUTOPLANT
ENTRY.STRING: ส้รางรถัถง รถุ้หมเกราะ และยานพาหนะื่อนๆ

ENTRY.name: AUTOPLANTV
ENTRY.STRING:
กองทหารัรกษาการ์ณภายในเื่พอ่ซอมแซม
ืพชัอตโนัมิตเ้ขามาแทนี่ทคอก้มา

ENTRY.name: SIEGEFACTORY
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางูยินต้ลอมเื่พอโจมีติ่สง่กอส้รางของัศตูร
ส้รางเสีบยงเกีวยนเื่พอสันบสุนนกองัทพของุคณ

ENTRY.name: SIEGEFACTORYV
ENTRY.STRING: ห่นวย Garrison Siege ี่ทอู่ย้ขางในเื่พอ่ซอมแซม

ENTRY.name: FACTORY
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางห่นวยืปนให่ญเื่พอโจมีตอาคารและกองกำัลงของัศตูร
ส้รางห่นวย่ตอ้ตานอากาศยานเื่พอิยงเคื่รองิบน้ขาึศกตก
ส้รางรถบรรุทกเื่พอสันบสุนนกองัทพของุคณ

ENTRY.name: FACTORYV
ENTRY.STRING:
ห่นวยทหารืปนให่ญภายในเื่พอ่ซอมแซม
โรงงานเ้ขามาแทนี่ทโรงงาน้ลอม

ENTRY.name: DOCK
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางเืรอประมงและเืรอทหารเืรอ
กองทหารัรกษาการ์ณัจด่สงมาี่ที่นเื่พอ่ซอมแซมและปก้ปองพวกเขา

ENTRY.name: ANCHORAGE
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางเืรอประมงและเืรอทหารเืรอ
กองทหารัรกษาการ์ณัจด่สงมาี่ที่นเื่พอ่ซอมแซมและปก้ปองพวกเขา

ENTRY.name: SHIPYARD
ENTRY.STRING:
ส้รางเืรอประมงและเืรอทหารเืรอ
กองทหารัรกษาการ์ณัจด่สงมาี่ที่นเื่พอ่ซอมแซมและปก้ปองพวกเขา

ENTRY.name: OILPLATFORM
ENTRY.STRING: รวบรวม#ICON6 {Oil}จากทะเล

ENTRY.name: OILWELL
LIBRARY
ENTRY.STRING: ดำเินนการิวัจยรวมึถงการ้กาวห้นาุยค

ENTRY.name: LIBRARYV
ENTRY.STRING: หมายเหุต: หากประเทศของุคณีม้หองสุมดหลายแ่หง ุคณจะสามารถทำการิวัจยหลายรายการพ้รอมักนไ้ด เวลา. ิ่สงี้นจะเิกดึ้ขนโดยัอตโนัมิต แ้ม่วาุคณจะัจดิควการิวัจยั้ทงหมดไ้วในี่ทเีดยว็กตาม ้หองสุมดของุคณ

ENTRY.name: UNIVERSITY
UNIVERSITYV
ENTRY.STRING: แ่ตละมหาิวทยาัลยสามารถรองัรบันกิวชาการไ้ดมากึถง 7 คน

ENTRY.name: MARKET
MARKETV
ENTRY.STRING: อุนญาตใ้หใ้ชทัรพยากรั่ทวไป 0 และ 1 (พ้รอมการ้คาระัดบ 2)

ENTRY.name: TEMPLE
ENTRY.STRING:
ขยายิอทิธพลของเืมอง่ตอ {National Borders} +$NUMBER0
เิ่พมพัลงีชิวตของเืมอง +$NUMBER1%
เิ่พมระยะการโจมีตของเืมอง +$NUMBER2

ENTRY.name: SENATE
ENTRY.STRING: หากส้รางี่ทเืมองื่อนนอกเหืนอจากเืมองหลวงของุคณ ใ้หเปี่ลยนเืมองั้นนเ็ปนเืมองหลวงให่มของุคณุวิฒสภาอุนญาตใ้หุคณ้คนค้วาระบบของัรฐบาลเืลอกระห่วางระบบการปกครองแบบเผ็ดจการและประชาิธปไตยเื่พอัรบผลประโยช์นี่ทแตก่ตางักน

ENTRY.name: SENATEV
ENTRY.STRING: ัยงไ้ดัรบนายพลู้ผัรกชาิตี่ทไ่มเหืมอนใครพ้รอมัรฐบาลแ่ตละูรปแบบู้ผัรกชาิตกัลบู่สุวิฒสภา (หัลงจาก่ลา้ชา) เื่มอูถกัสงหาร หากุวิฒสภาของุคณูถกัจบ Patriot จะกัลบมาี่ทเืมองใก้ลเีคยงุคณสามารถีมุวิฒสภาไ้ดคั้รงละหึ่นงคนเ่ทาั้นน ุคณ้ตองทำลายัอนเ่กา่กอน (ใ้ชเวลานาน!) ่กอนี่ทจะส้รางัอนให่มึ่ซงจะ้ยายเืมองหลวงของุคณไปัยงเืมองให่ม

ENTRY.name: COLOSSUS
