ENTRY.name: TERRACOTTAARMYCTW
ENTRY.STRING: ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย มอบกองัทพเสิรมิพเศษใ้หักบุคณในุทกการ่ตอู้ส Conquest หากีมอู่ยในการ่ตอู้ส ใ้หส้รางห่นวยทหารราบิอสระุทกๆ $NUMBER2 ิวนาีท (บวก $NUMBER3 ิวนาีทสำหัรบุทกห่นวยทหารราบี่ทุคณควบุคม)

ENTRY.name: FORBIDDENCITY
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย ันบเ็ปนโบันสเืมองให่ญในประเทศของุคณ สามารถส้รางไ้ดุทกี่ที่ทเืมองสามารถทำไ้ด และสถานี่ท่กอส้รางจะไ่มเิปดเผยใ้หัศตูรทราบจนก่วาจะส้รางเส็รจ
ใ้หอาหาร +50 #ICON1 และไ้ม แทนี่ทจะเ็ปน +10/+10 ปกิต
ัตวัจบเวลาุทนไ่ม่สงผลกระทบ่ตอุคณ

ENTRY.name: FORBIDDENCITYCTW
ENTRY.STRING: ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย เื่มออู่ยในการ่ตอู้ส Conquer the World การัจบเวลาเืมองหลวงและการกำัจดการเีสยีชิวตอ่ยางกะัทนัหนจะไ่ม่สงผล่ตอุคณ เื่มอูถกัจดึ้ขนในอาณาัจกรของุคณแ่ตไ่มปรากฏในการ่ตอู้สัปจุจับน เิ่พมัตวัจบเวลาเืมองหลวงของุคณเ็ปนสองเ่ทา และลดัตวัจบเวลาเืมองหลวงของัศตูรลงคึ่รงหึ่นง

ENTRY.name: PORCELAIN

ENTRY.name: REDFORT
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย
้ปอมขนาดให่ญี่ทีมระยะไกล, พัลงีชิวตมากมาย, ่คากองทหารเิ่พมเิตม, การัดนชายแดนี่ทเิ่พมึ้ขน และความ้ตานทาน่ตอการโจมีตทางอากาศ สามารถส้รางไ้ดุทกี่ที่ทีม้ปอม; ไ่มันบรวมักบีขดจำักด Wonder ของเืมอง ตำแห่นงการ่กอส้รางจะไ่มเิปดเผย่ตอัศตูรจนก่วาัมนจะเส็รจสมูบร์ณ
ัรบการัอพเกรด้ปอมฟีร และ้ปอมั้ทงหมดไ้ดัรบแ้ตมพัลงีชิวตเิ่พมึ้ขน 33% ห่นวยี่ทุคมัขงอู่ยใน้ปอมจะัรกษาี่ท +$NUMBER1% ัอตราปกิต

ENTRY.name: REDFORTCTW
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย ้ปอมขนาดให่ญี่ทีมระยะไกลและพัลงีชิวตมากมายีมูมล่คากองทหารเิ่พมึ้ขน เิ่พมการผัลกัดนชายแดน และ้ตานทานการโจมีตทางอากาศ สามารถส้รางไ้ดุทกี่ที่ทีม้ปอม; ไ่มันบรวมักบีขดจำักด Wonder ของเืมอง ตำแห่นงการ่กอส้รางจะไ่มเิปดเผย่ตอัศตูรจนก่วาัมนจะเส็รจสมูบร์ณ

ENTRY.name: VERSAILLES

ENTRY.name: TAJMAHAL

ENTRY.name: KREMLIN
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย
เิ่พมีขดจำักดการ้คา #ICON21 ของุคณสำหัรบ #ICON1 อาหาร #ICON2 ไ้ม #ICON5 โลหะ และ #ICON6 ้นำัมนึ้ขน $NUMBER1 เิ่พมความเีสยหายจากการัขดีสี่ทเิกดึ้ขนักบูยินตัศตูรี่ทไ่มไ้ดัรบเสีบยงในิดนแดนของุคณีอก 00% สายัลบูถกส้รางึ้ขนัทนีท ัรบการใ้ชูยินต Spy ิพเศษึ่ซงไ่มันบรวมักบจำนวนประชากรูสงุสดของุคณ และจะูถกส้รางึ้ขนให่มฟีรุทกคั้รงี่ทัมนูถกัสงหาร

ENTRY.name: STATUEOFLIBERTY
ENTRY.STRING: ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย ุคณไ้ดัรบการัอพเกรดห่นวยภาคื้พนิดนและฐานัทพอากาศั้ทงหมดฟีรลดความเีสยหายจากการัขดีสี่ทเิกดึ้ขนักบูยินตของุคณในอาณาเขตของัศตูรลง $NUMBER2% ลด้ตนุทนของเคื่รองิบนิ้ทงระเิบดและอาคาร้ปองักนทางอากาศลง $NUMBER3%

ENTRY.name: EIFFELTOWER
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย
เิ่พมรายไ้ด #ICON6 ้นำัมนของุคณ $NUMBER1%
เิ่พมีขดจำักดการ้คา #ICON21 ของุคณสำหัรบ้นำัมน #ICON6 ึ้ขน $NUMBER3
เิ่พมพรมแดนประเทศของุคณึ้ขน $NUMBER2

ENTRY.name: SUPERCOLLIDER
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย
้ตนุทนในการื้ซอิสน้คาในตลาดไ่มเคยูสงก่วา $NUMBER1 #ICON3 ความั่มงั่คง; ราคาขายไ่มเคย่ตำก่วา $NUMBER2 #ICON3 ความั่มงั่คง. เทคโนโลียสามารถ้คนค้วาไ้ดัทนีท (แ่ตไ่มใ่ชห่นวยเทคโนโลีย)

ENTRY.name: SPACEPROGRAM
ENTRY.STRING:
ิ่สงมัหศจรร์ยของโลก: $STRING0 ุจดมัหศจรร์ย
ุคณสามารถูดตำแห่นงของูยินต อาคาร และูภิมประเทศั้ทงหมดไ้ดบนแผนี่ท
ุคณส้รางเคื่รองิบนและีขปนาุวธไ้ดเ็รวก่วา 0% และูถกก่วา 1% ุคณีมูภิมุ้คมักน่ตอผลกระทบจากการค่วำบาตรินวเคีลย์ร

ENTRY.name: HVYELEPHANT
ENTRY.STRING: Persian and Indian unique {Elephant,} Classical Age #ICON28 - powerful, slow melee units. Good against close-order foot; vulnerable to light, scattered troops.

ENTRY.name: MAHOUT
ENTRY.STRING: Persian and Indian unique {Elephant,} Medieval Age #ICON29 - powerful, slow melee units. Good against close-order foot; vulnerable to light, scattered troops. Fires at range as well as in melee.

ENTRY.name: GUNMAHOUT
ENTRY.STRING: Persian and Indian unique {Elephant,} Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - powerful, slow melee units. Good against close-order foot; vulnerable to light, scattered troops. Fires at range as well as in melee.

ENTRY.name: CULMAHOUT
ENTRY.STRING: Persian and Indian unique {Elephant,} Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - powerful, slow melee units. Good against close-order foot; vulnerable to light, scattered troops. Fires as siege as well as in melee.

ENTRY.name: CITIZENS
ENTRY.STRING:
Can construct buildings such as Cities and Farms.
Can gather resources at Farms, Woodcutter's Camps, Mines, and Oil Wells.

ENTRY.name: SCHOLAR
ENTRY.STRING: The more Scholars you have in your Universities, the faster you will generate #ICON4 {Knowledge}.

ENTRY.name: SCHOLARV
ENTRY.STRING: Scholars cannot build and repair buildings or gather any other resource.

ENTRY.name: GENERAL
ENTRY.STRING:
Increases the armor of all your troops nearby by +$NUMBER0 (increases further per level General is upgraded).
Can {Force March} their armies to increase their move speeds.
Can {Entrench} foot troops to increase their defense.
Can hide units to prepare an {Ambush} or sneak attack.
Can {Create Decoy} units that look and behave like real units to your enemies, but cause no damage when they attack.

ENTRY.name: GENERALV
ENTRY.STRING: Having a General in a Tower (or Fort, City, etc.) adds +$NUMBER1 to the building's Attack Range and Attack Rating.

ENTRY.name: SPY
ENTRY.STRING:
Can {Bribe} enemy combat units to join your nation.
Can plant {Informers} to gain information on enemy buildings and units.
Can use {Counterintelligence} to destroy enemy Spies and to remove Informers.

ENTRY.name: CARAVAN
ENTRY.STRING:
Generate #ICON3 {Wealth} by making trade routes between pairs of your cities.
Only one Caravan can ply the route between a particular pair of cities.
You can create one additional Caravan for each #ICON26 Commerce research.
You specify one of the cities on a Caravan's route by selecting the Caravan and right clicking on the City. The Caravan will pick the most favorable second City.
You can specify both of the cities on a Caravan's route by right dragging between the two cities on either the main map or the minimap.

ENTRY.name: CARAVANV
ENTRY.STRING:
The larger (and further apart) the cities, the more #ICON3 Wealth will be generated.
With #ICON26 3 Commerce technologies you can also trade with allies' cities. Allies who trade with you generate wealth for you as well as for themselves.
Caravans are immune to bribery.

ENTRY.name: MERCHANT
ENTRY.STRING:
Merchants allow you to claim and gain the benefit from {rare resources} such as Wine, Diamonds, and Horses.
Deploy a merchant next to a rare resource to claim it.

ENTRY.name: BOWMEN
ENTRY.STRING: {Foot Archers}, ยุคโบราณ #ICON45 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง

ENTRY.name: ARCHERS
ENTRY.STRING: {Foot Archers}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON28 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง

ENTRY.name: CROSSBOWMEN
ENTRY.STRING: {Foot Archers}, ยุคกลาง #ICON29 and ยุคดินปืน #ICON30 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง

ENTRY.name: HEAVYARCHERS
ENTRY.STRING: {Foot Archers}, ยุคกลาง #ICON29 and ยุคดินปืน #ICON30 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง

ENTRY.name: KUSHARCHERS

ENTRY.name: HWARANG

ENTRY.name: HWARANGELITE
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเกาหลี {Foot Archers}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON28 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง; แข็งแกร่งและทนทานกว่าทหารธนูทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: KORYOHWARANG
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเกาหลี {Foot Archers}, ยุคกลาง #ICON29 - ทหารราบระยะกลาง เกราะบาง; แข็งแกร่งและทนทานกว่าหน้าไม้ทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: CHOSONHWARANG
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเกาหลี {Foot Archers}, ยุคดินปืน #ICON30 - ทหารราบระยะไกล เกราะบาง; แข็งแกร่งและทนทานกว่าหน่วยธนูทุกแบบ

ENTRY.name: MILITIA
ENTRY.STRING:
Citizens who have taken up weapons.
Once the Militia unit is researched, Citizens gain a {To Arms!} button, which allows them to become fighting Militia units.
Militia research also increases the hit points and Line-of-Sight of ordinary Citizens.
Militia research also increases Tower range and Line-of-Sight.

ENTRY.name: MINUTEMAN
ENTRY.STRING:
Upgraded Militia armed with muskets.
Citizens can become Minutemen using their {To Arms!} button.
Minuteman research also increases the hit points and Line-of-Sight of ordinary Citizens.
Minuteman research also increases Tower range and Line-of-Sight.

ENTRY.name: PARTISANS
ENTRY.STRING:
Upgraded Minuteman armed with rifles.
When Partisans are not moving or attacking they can't normally be seen by most enemies.
Citizens can become Partisans using their {To Arms!} button.
Partisan research also increases the hit points and Line-of-Sight of ordinary Citizens.
Partisan research also increases Tower range and Line-of-Sight.

ENTRY.name: HORSEARCHER
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Classical Age #ICON28 - fast raiders able to fire even while moving.

ENTRY.name: HEAVYCAVARCHER
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Medieval Age #ICON29 - fast raiders able to fire even while moving.

ENTRY.name: DRAGOON
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - fast gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving.

ENTRY.name: LIGHTHORSE
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Cavalry}, Classical Age #ICON28 - extremely fast and lightly armed melee troops; useful for catching enemy raiders.

ENTRY.name: LIGHTCAVALRY
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Cavalry}, Medieval Age #ICON29 - extremely fast and lightly armed melee troops; useful for catching enemy raiders.

ENTRY.name: LTCAVALRYELITE
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Cavalry}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - extremely fast and lightly armed melee troops; useful for catching enemy raiders.

ENTRY.name: HUSSAR
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Cavalry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - extremely fast and lightly armed melee troops, useful for catching enemy raiders.

ENTRY.name: AAGUN
ENTRY.STRING: {Anti-Aircraft}, Industrial Age #ICON32 - a slow and inaccurate defense against early aircraft.

ENTRY.name: FLAKGUN
ENTRY.STRING: {Anti-Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - a vehicle-mounted defense against enemy aircraft.

ENTRY.name: AVENGER
ENTRY.STRING: {Anti-Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - a powerful, accurate defense against enemy aircraft.

ENTRY.name: CARBINEER
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - fast, gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving.

ENTRY.name: CAMELARCHER

ENTRY.STRING: Scouts are cheap and expendable; they are good for exploring the map and scouting enemy positions.

เมื่อนักสำรวจไม่เคลื่อนที่ ศัตรูส่วนใหญ่มักมองไม่เห็น

ENTRY.name: LIGHTTANK

ENTRY.STRING: {Tanks}, Modern Age #ICON33 - armored and extremely powerful; difficult to destroy except with heavy weapons.

ENTRY.STRING: {Tanks}, Information Age #ICON34 - heavily armored and extremely powerful; difficult to destroy except with heavy weapons.

ENTRY.name: TIGERTANK
ENTRY.STRING: German Unique {Tanks}, Modern Age #ICON33 - armored and substantially more powerful than ordinary Tanks.

ENTRY.name: LEOPARDTANK
ENTRY.STRING: German Unique {Tanks}, Information Age #ICON34 - heavily armored and substantially more powerful than ordinary Tanks.

ENTRY.name: ARMOREDCAR
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Modern Age #ICON33 - lightly armored vehicles that can fire while moving.

ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Industrial Age #ICON32 - fast, lightly armored vehicles that can fire while moving.

ENTRY.name: ARMOREDCAVALRY
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Information Age #ICON34 - fast, moderately armored vehicles that can fire while moving.

ENTRY.name: BIPLANE

ENTRY.name: FIGHTER
ENTRY.STRING: {Fighter Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - powerful against enemy aircraft and useful for strafing enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: JETFIGHTER
ENTRY.STRING: {Fighter Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - powerful against enemy aircraft and useful for strafing enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: FIGHTERBOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Fighter Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - deployed on Aircraft Carriers; powerful against enemy aircraft and useful for strafing enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: JETFIGHTERBOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Fighter Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - powerful against enemy aircraft and useful for strafing enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: ADVFIGHTER
ENTRY.STRING: {Fighter Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - powerful against enemy aircraft and useful for strafing enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: ZEPPELIN
ENTRY.STRING: {Bomber Aircraft}, Industrial Age #ICON32 - slow and inaccurate; provides an early capability to bomb enemy buildings.

ENTRY.name: BOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Bomber Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - powerful against enemy cities and buildings; somewhat inaccurate.

ENTRY.name: STRATEGICBOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Bomber Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - extremely powerful against enemy cities and buildings; fast and relatively accurate.

ENTRY.name: STEALTHBOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Bomber Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - devastating against enemy cities and buildings; very fast and highly accurate. Cannot be targeted by ground-based defenses.

ENTRY.name: HELICOPTER
ENTRY.STRING: {Helicopters}, Modern Age #ICON33 - versatile aircraft that can hover in place. Useful for raiding and for hunting Submarines and Tanks.

ENTRY.name: ATTACKHELICOPTER
ENTRY.STRING: {Helicopters}, Information Age #ICON34 - versatile aircraft that can hover in place. Useful for raiding and for hunting Submarines and Tanks.

ENTRY.name: V2ROCKET
ENTRY.STRING: {Conventional Missiles}, Industrial Age #ICON32 - useful against enemy buildings and cities; somewhat inaccurate.

ENTRY.name: CRUISEMISSILE
ENTRY.STRING: {Conventional Missiles}, Modern Age #ICON33 - useful against enemy buildings, cities, and ships.

ENTRY.name: NUCLEARMISSILE
ENTRY.STRING: {Nuclear Missiles}, Modern Age #ICON33 - weapons of mass destruction capable of flattening several enemy buildings; there are political and economic consequences to using them.

ENTRY.name: NUCLEARICBM
ENTRY.STRING: {Nuclear Missiles}, Information Age #ICON34 - devastating weapons of mass destruction capable of levelling enemy cities; there are political and economic consequences to using them.

ENTRY.name: SUPPLYWAGON
ENTRY.STRING:
Prevent most attrition damage to your troops within their radius.
Allow your siege/artillery units to fire faster even in enemy territory.

ENTRY.name: ARMEDSUPPLYWAGON
ENTRY.STRING:
Dutch Unique civilian unit. Prevents most attrition damage to your troops within its radius. Allows your siege/artillery units to fire faster even in enemy territory.
Armed Supply Wagons have limited firepower for protection against raiding and improve with each Age.

ENTRY.name: FISHERMEN
ENTRY.STRING:
Can gather resources from Fish and Whale sites.
To fish, move near a Fish/Whale resource site in the sea.

ENTRY.name: TRANSPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Carries a ground unit across the sea.

ENTRY.name: XPORTFREIGHT
ENTRY.STRING: Carries a ground unit across the sea.

ENTRY.name: TRANSPORTHELICOPTER
ENTRY.STRING: Carries an Air Cavalry unit to its destination.

ENTRY.name: TRIREME
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Ships}, Ancient Age #ICON45 - slow but powerful; useful for massing naval power.

ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Ships}, Classical Age #ICON28 - slow but powerful; useful for massing naval power.

ENTRY.name: GALLEON
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Ships}, Medieval Age #ICON29 - slow but powerful; useful for massing naval power.

ENTRY.name: FRIGATE
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Ships}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - armed with cannons; useful for massing naval power. Limited range curtails their value for shore bombardment.

ENTRY.name: MANOWAR
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Ships}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - powerful cannon-armed warships; more than a match for any other wooden ship.

ENTRY.STRING: {Light Ships}, Classical Age #ICON28 - fast and lightly armed; useful for scouting and intercepting enemy Transports.

ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Bombardment Ships}, Modern Age #ICON33 - powerful armored naval vessels useful for both naval supremacy and shore bombardment.

ENTRY.name: HVYMACHINEGUNGER
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเยอรมัน {Machine Guns}, ยุคสมัยใหม่ #ICON33 - เคลื่อนที่ช้า; ทำลายทหารราบศัตรูได้รุนแรง

ENTRY.name: MORTAR
ENTRY.STRING: Inca Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - expensive and slow-moving; particularly effective at long range against enemy buildings and foot troops.

ENTRY.name: SIEGEMORTAR
ENTRY.STRING: Inca Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Industrial Age #ICON32 - expensive and slow-moving; particularly effective at long range against enemy buildings and foot troops.

ENTRY.name: RUSSHEAVYTANK
ENTRY.STRING: Russian Unique {Tanks}, Information Age #ICON34 - heavily armored, extremely powerful; hard to destroy except with heavy weapons. Cheaper than normal Main Battle Tank.

ENTRY.name: INFANTRYGERMAN
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเยอรมัน {Modern Infantry}, ยุคสมัยใหม่ #ICON33 - ทหารราบยิงถี่ แรง และเคลื่อนที่เร็ว; ทนทานและเร็วกว่าทหารราบทั่วไปเล็กน้อย

ENTRY.name: REDGUARD
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะรัสเซีย {Modern Infantry}, ยุคสมัยใหม่ #ICON33 - ทหารราบยิงถี่ แรง และเคลื่อนที่เร็ว; ถูกกว่าทหารราบทั่วไปเล็กน้อย

ENTRY.name: SHOCKINFANTRY
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะรัสเซีย {Modern Infantry}, ยุคสารสนเทศ #ICON34 - ทหารราบยิงถี่ แรง และเคลื่อนที่เร็ว; ถูกและแรงกว่าทหารจู่โจมทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: BASILICABOMBARD
ENTRY.STRING: Turkish Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - expensive and slow-moving; extremely effective at long range against enemy buildings and foot troops.

ENTRY.name: BASILICACANNON
ENTRY.STRING: Turkish Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - expensive and slow-moving; particularly effective at long range against enemy buildings and foot troops.

ENTRY.name: KATYUSHA
ENTRY.STRING: Russian Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Modern Age #ICON33 - powerful, long-range rocket; its wide explosive effect makes it very effective against enemy ground troops.

ENTRY.name: FLAMINGARROW
ENTRY.STRING: Korean Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Classical Age #ICON28 - expensive and slow; more effective against enemy buildings than Catapult.

ENTRY.name: HVYFLAMINGARROW
ENTRY.STRING: Korean Unique {Artillery Weapons}, Medieval Age #ICON29 - expensive and slow; more effective against enemy buildings than normal Trebuchet.

ENTRY.name: HAWKFB
ENTRY.STRING: Bantu Unique {Fighter Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - more powerful against enemy aircraft and ground troops than normal Fighter.

ENTRY.name: EAGLEFB
ENTRY.STRING: Bantu Unique {Fighter Aircraft}, Information Age #ICON34 - more powerful against enemy aircraft and ground troops than normal Jet Fighter.

ENTRY.name: LANCASTERBOMBER
ENTRY.STRING: British Unique {Bomber Aircraft}, Modern Age #ICON33 - more powerful against enemy cities and buildings than normal Bomber; somewhat inaccurate.

ENTRY.name: ADVANCEDBATTLESHIP
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Bombardment Ships}, Information Age #ICON34 - extremely powerful armored naval vessels useful for both naval supremacy and shore bombardment.

ENTRY.name: MERCHANTFLEET
ENTRY.STRING: {Caravans} - represents the seagoing portion of a Caravan trade route.

ENTRY.name: MODMERCHANTFLEET
ENTRY.STRING: {Caravans} - represents the seagoing portion of a Caravan trade route .

ENTRY.name: TRANSPORTGALLEON
ENTRY.STRING: Carries a ground unit over the sea.

ENTRY.name: ALEXANDER
ENTRY.STRING: +1 attack to all units in his radius. Extra +2 attack to all Heavy Infantry in his radius.Has double craft.Units in Alexander's Forced March move 50% faster than with a normal General.

ENTRY.name: NAPOLEON
ENTRY.STRING: +3 attack and +1 range to all siege units in his radius.Artillery units near Napoleon move and deploy faster.Has double craft.Units in Napoleon's Forced March move 50% faster than with a normal General.

ENTRY.name: PARMENIO
ENTRY.STRING: +1 attack to all Heavy Cavalry in his radius.Has a 50% larger radius than a normal General.Ambush lasts 3x longer.Starts battles with 5 extra Companions.

ENTRY.name: ANTIPATER
ENTRY.STRING: Heals units within his radius.When garrisoned in a building, the building has +3 attack.Can Entrench anywhere.Entrenches 2x faster than a normal General.Units entrenched by Antipater take 80% normal damage.

ENTRY.name: PTOLEMY
ENTRY.STRING: +1 range and +1 Line-of-Sight to all ranged and siege units in his radius.+1 attack to all siege units in his radius.+1 armor to all units in his radius.

ENTRY.name: DARIUS
ENTRY.STRING: +1 armor to all units in his radius. Prevents attrition damage to all units in his radius.

ENTRY.name: MEMNON
ENTRY.STRING: Always cloaked like a Spy.Regenerates craft 2x faster than a normal General.Gives large bonuses to Greek Mercenaries for attack and armor.

ENTRY.name: SPITAMENES
ENTRY.STRING: +1 attack to all Horse Archers in his radius. 20% speed bonus to all Cavalry units.When nearby, increases plunder gained from enemy buildings and gains plunder from enemy units.

ENTRY.name: CHANDRAGUPTA
ENTRY.STRING: Has 2x more craft than other unique Generals.Prevents attrition damage to all units in his radius.

ENTRY.name: PORUS
ENTRY.STRING: Detects cloaked units as a Scout does.Has 2x more hit points than other unique Generals.Creates 2x more decoys than a normal General.Enables creating War Elephants at the Stable.20% speed bonus to all War Elephant troops.

ENTRY.name: WELLINGTON
ENTRY.STRING: Double radius of effect.Heals units within his radius.+1 defense and +1 attack vs. siege and supply wagons for units in his radius.Disables enemy strategic resources during battles as long as he is alive.

ENTRY.name: CHARLES
ENTRY.STRING: Makes all units cost less while he is alive.Speeds up all units in his radius by 20%.

ENTRY.name: DJEZZAR
ENTRY.STRING: Gives +1 attack to all units in his radius. Starts CtW battles with an extra starting army.

ENTRY.name: FOUCHE
ENTRY.STRING: Reveals all enemies. Has triple the normal spy craft.

ENTRY.name: SCHWARZENBERG
ENTRY.STRING: Has double craft and hit points.Starts with a team of 4 Commandos.

ENTRY.name: BLUCHER
ENTRY.STRING: +2 attack, +2 armor, and 20% speed bonus to all Stable units in his radius. Starts with extra Cavalry.

ENTRY.name: KUTOSOV
ENTRY.STRING: Prevents attrition to units in his radius.Creates triple the normal amount of decoys.Has triple radius of effect.

ENTRY.name: PAOLI
ENTRY.STRING: Has double hit points.Can perform instant sniping and sabotage.

ENTRY.name: DARIUSCHARIOT
ENTRY.STRING: General Darius' Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Classical Age #ICON28 - fast, Ranged Cavalry that can fire even while moving; particularly effective against all other Ranged Cavalry.

ENTRY.name: THEDESPOT
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Acts like a Supply Wagon.Extra large Line-of-Sight.Doubles plunder from enemy buildings destroyed in his radius, and gives plunder from enemy units.

ENTRY.name: THESENATOR
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Buildings in his radius get +5 armor.Can bribe enemy units as though he were a Spy.Buildings and troops in his radius heal while in friendly territory.

ENTRY.name: THEMONARCH
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Acts like a Supply Wagon.Siege units in his radius move and deploy at 2x speed.Mounted units in his radius have an extra +2 attack, +2 armor.

ENTRY.name: THEPRESIDENT
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Unit-producing buildings in his radius produce at 2x speed.Buildings under construction in his radius build 33% faster.Buildings and troops in his radius heal while in friendly or neutral territory.

ENTRY.name: THECITIZEN
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Acts like a Supply Wagon.All friendly units in his radius have +2 attack, +2 armor.Enemy cities in his radius assimilate at 4x speed.

ENTRY.name: THECEO
ENTRY.STRING: Has all normal General powers (armor increase, special abilities) but is immune to sniping and bribery.Buildings and troops in his radius heal as long as they are not taking attrition damage.Friendly units in his radius are immune to bribery and sniping.All friendly units in his radius have +4 range and Line-of-Sight (except siege units).

ENTRY.name: IMMORTAL
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเปอร์เซีย {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคโบราณ #ICON45 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; อ่อนกว่าฮอพไลต์ทั่วไปเล็กน้อยแต่ยิงระยะได้

ENTRY.name: ANUSIYA
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเปอร์เซีย {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON28 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; อ่อนกว่าแถวทหารทั่วไปเล็กน้อยแต่ยิงระยะได้

ENTRY.name: ATHANATOI
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเปอร์เซีย {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON29 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; อ่อนกว่าทหารหอกทั่วไปเล็กน้อยแต่ยิงระยะได้

ENTRY.name: ARQIMMORTAL
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะเปอร์เซีย {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON30 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; อ่อนกว่าทหารหอกระดับสูงทั่วไปเล็กน้อยแต่ยิงระยะได้

ENTRY.name: MARINES
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอเมริกา {Gunpowder Infantry}, ยุคแห่งแสงสว่าง #ICON31 - ยิงรุนแรงเมื่อรวมกลุ่ม แต่ยิงช้าพอสมควร เรือขนส่งนาวิกโจมตีเร็วและทนทานกว่าทั่วไป และหน่วยนาวิกจะขุดสังเคราะห์อัตโนมัติหลังหยุดนิ่ง 5 วินาที

ENTRY.name: MARINERIFLEMEN
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอเมริกา {Gunpowder Infantry}, ยุคอุตสาหกรรม #ICON32 - ทหารราบแม่นและแรง เรือขนส่งนาวิกโจมตีเร็วและทนทานกว่าทั่วไป และหน่วยนาวิกจะขุดสังเคราะห์อัตโนมัติหลังหยุดนิ่ง 5 วินาที

ENTRY.name: MARINEINFANTRY
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอเมริกา {Modern Infantry}, ยุคสมัยใหม่ #ICON33 - ทหารราบยิงถี่ แรง และแม่นแม้ระยะไกล เรือขนส่งนาวิกโจมตีเร็วและทนทานกว่าทั่วไป และหน่วยนาวิกจะขุดสังเคราะห์อัตโนมัติหลังหยุดนิ่ง 5 วินาที

ENTRY.name: ASSAULTMARINES
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอเมริกา {Modern Infantry}, ยุคสารสนเทศ #ICON34 - ทหารราบยิงถี่ แรง และเคลื่อนที่เร็ว เรือขนส่งนาวิกโจมตีเร็วและทนทานกว่าทั่วไป และหน่วยนาวิกจะขุดสังเคราะห์อัตโนมัติหลังหยุดนิ่ง 5 วินาที

ENTRY.name: BRIG
ENTRY.STRING: Dutch Unique {Light Ship}, Medieval #ICON29 - fast and lightly armed; useful for scouting and intercepting enemy Transports. Stronger, faster, and tougher than normal Caravels.

ENTRY.name: FLUYT
ENTRY.STRING: Dutch Unique {Light Ships}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - swift and armed with light cannon; excellent for scouting and intercepting enemy Transports. Stronger, faster, and tougher than normal Corvettes.

ENTRY.name: CLIPPER
ENTRY.STRING: Dutch Unique {Light Ships}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - swift and armed with light cannon; excellent for scouting and intercepting enemy Transports. Stronger, faster, and tougher than normal Sloops.

ENTRY.name: MERCHANTDUTCH
ENTRY.STRING:
Dutch Unique civilian unit. Armed Merchants allow you to claim and gain the benefit from {rare resources} such as Wine, Diamonds, and Horses.
Deploy an Armed Merchant next to a rare resource to claim it. Armed Merchants have limited firepower for protection against raiding, and improve with each Age.

ENTRY.name: ARMEDCARAVAN
ENTRY.STRING:
Dutch Unique civilian unit. Generate #ICON3 {Wealth} by making trade routes between pairs of your Cities. Armed Caravans have limited firepower for protection against raiding, and improve with each Age.
Only one Armed Caravan can ply the route between a particular pair of Cities.
You can create one additional Caravan for each #ICON26 Commerce research.
Specify one of the Cities on an Armed Caravan's route by selecting the Armed Caravan and right clicking on the City. The Armed Caravan will pick the most favorable second City.
You can specify both of the Cities on an Armed Caravan's route by right dragging between the two Cities on either the main map or the minimap.

ENTRY.name: ARMEDCARAVANV
ENTRY.STRING:
The larger (and further apart) the Cities, the more #ICON3 Wealth will be generated.
With #ICON26 3 Commerce technologies you can also trade with allies' Cities. Allies who trade with you generate Wealth for you as well as for themselves.
Armed Caravans are immune to bribery.

ENTRY.name: CONQUISTADOR
ENTRY.STRING: Spanish Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - fast, gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving; greater attack than other Ranged Cavalry.

ENTRY.name: ECONQUISTADOR
ENTRY.STRING: Spanish Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - fast, gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving; greater attack than other Ranged Cavalry.

ENTRY.name: SUNKAWAKAN
ENTRY.STRING: Lakota Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Classical Age #ICON28 - fast raiders able to fire even while moving. Receive plunder each time they damage an enemy building used for gathering (e.g., Granary, Farm).

ENTRY.name: EAGLEFEATHER
ENTRY.STRING: Lakota Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Medieval Age #ICON29 - fast raiders able to fire even while moving. Receive plunder each time they damage an enemy building used for gathering (e.g., Granary, Farm).

ENTRY.name: IRQSCOUT
ENTRY.STRING:
เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Scouts}, ยุคโบราณ #ICON45 - เร็วแต่ไม่มีอาวุธ; เหมาะสำรวจแผนที่และค้นหาศัตรู เร็วกว่า ทนทานกว่า และมองเห็นไกลกว่าหน่วยสอดแนมทั่วไป
มองเห็นหน่วยซ่อนเร้น เช่น สายลับและคอมมานโด
ทำลายสายลับศัตรูได้

ENTRY.name: IRQSCOUTV
ENTRY.STRING: Takohs are cheap and expendable; they are good for exploring the map and scouting enemy positions.

ENTRY.name: IRQEXPLORER
ENTRY.STRING:
เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Scouts}, ยุคกลาง #ICON29 - เร็วแต่ไม่มีอาวุธ เร็วกว่า ทนทานกว่า และมองเห็นไกลกว่านักสำรวจทั่วไป
ใช้ {Counterintelligence} เพื่อทำลายสายลับและลบผู้ให้ข่าว
เมื่ออัควเคสไม่เคลื่อนที่หรือโจมตี ศัตรูส่วนใหญ่มักมองไม่เห็น
มองเห็นหน่วยซ่อนเร้น เช่น สายลับและคอมมานโด

ENTRY.name: IRQEXPLORERV
ENTRY.STRING: Akweks are cheap and expendable; they are good for exploring the map and scouting enemy positions.

ENTRY.name: IRQCOMMANDO
ENTRY.STRING:
เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Scouts}, ยุคแห่งแสงสว่าง #ICON31 - หน่วยสอดแนมเฉพาะทางที่มีความสามารถหลากหลาย เร็วกว่า ทนทานกว่า และมองเห็นไกลกว่าคอมมานโดทั่วไป ปลดล็อกเร็วกว่าหนึ่งยุค
ใช้ความสามารถ {Sniper} เพื่อทำลายหน่วยศัตรู
ใช้ {Counterintelligence} เพื่อทำลายสายลับและลบผู้ให้ข่าว
มองเห็นหน่วยซ่อนเร้น เช่น สายลับและคอมมานโด
เมื่อโอควารีไม่เคลื่อนที่หรือโจมตี ศัตรูส่วนใหญ่มักมองไม่เห็น

ENTRY.name: MUSKETHORSE
ENTRY.STRING: Lakota Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - fast, gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving. Receive plunder each time they damage an enemy building used for gathering (e.g., Granary, Farm).

ENTRY.name: RIFLEHORSE
ENTRY.STRING: Lakota Unique {Ranged Cavalry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - fast, gunpowder-armed raiders able to fire even while moving. Receive plunder each time they damage an enemy building used for gathering.

ENTRY.name: SPEARMEN
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคโบราณ #ICON45 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; ในเขตพันธมิตรจะเร็วกว่าฮอพไลต์ทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: MOHAWK
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคคลาสสิก #ICON28 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; ในเขตพันธมิตรจะเร็วกว่าแถวทหารทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: HEAVYMOHAWK
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคกลาง #ICON29 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; ในเขตพันธมิตรจะเร็วกว่าทหารหอกทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: ELITEMOHAWK
ENTRY.STRING: เฉพาะอิรอกัว {Heavy Infantry}, ยุคดินปืน #ICON30 - แข็งแกร่งแต่ช้า; ในเขตพันธมิตรจะเร็วกว่าทหารหอกระดับสูงทั่วไป

ENTRY.name: JUNKERS
ENTRY.STRING: Prussian Unique {Gunpowder Infantry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - powerful in quantity but somewhat slow-firing; greater attack than other Gunpowder Infantry.

ENTRY.name: BANDEIRANTES
ENTRY.STRING: Portuguese Unique {Heavy Infantry}, Gunpowder Age #ICON30 - slow, cloaked, powerful, short-ranged gunpowder units; extremely effective against mounted troops.

ENTRY.name: BANDEIRANTESELITE
ENTRY.STRING: Portuguese Unique {Heavy Infantry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - slow, cloaked, powerful, short-ranged gunpowder units; extremely effective against mounted troops.

ENTRY.name: PTBOAT
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Ships}, Modern Age #ICON33 - cheap, swift and able to detect and destroy enemy {Submarines} and transports; also armed with powerful {anti-aircraft} weapons.

ENTRY.name: HUMVEE
ENTRY.STRING: {Ranged Cavalry}, Information Age #ICON34 - fast, moderately armored vehicles that can fire while moving; better defense than other Ranged Cavalry.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANARCHER
ENTRY.STRING: Barbarian Unique {Foot Archers} - medium-ranged foot Infantry with little armor.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANJAVELINEERS
ENTRY.STRING: Barbarian Unique {Light Infantry} - fast, cheap, and short-ranged.

ENTRY.name: BARBARIANPHALANX
ENTRY.STRING: Barbarian Unique {Heavy Infantry} - powerful, slow, melee units.

ENTRY.name: ROYALGUARD
ENTRY.STRING: French Unique {Gunpowder Infantry}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - powerful in quantity but somewhat slow-firing; more health and defense than other Gunpowder Infantry.

ENTRY.name: GREEKMERCS
ENTRY.STRING: {Heavy Infantry}, Classical Age #ICON28 - cheap, slow, melee units.

ENTRY.name: IRONCLAD
ENTRY.STRING: {Light Ships}, Enlightenment Age #ICON31 - powerful, swift and armed with light cannon; excellent for scouting and intercepting enemy transports.

ENTRY.name: TYPHOON
ENTRY.STRING: {Submarines}, Information Age #ICON34 - difficult to detect except by Destroyers and Cruisers.

ENTRY.name: HIND
ENTRY.STRING: {Helicopters}, Information Age #ICON34 - versatile aircraft that can hover in place. Useful for raiding and to hunt subs and tanks.

ENTRY.name: CIVILIAN
ENTRY.STRING: Turns this unit back into a Citizen or Scholar.

ENTRY.name: TOARMS
ENTRY.STRING: Turns a normal Citizen or Scholar into an armed Militia, Minuteman, or Partisan.

ENTRY.name: GORADAR
ENTRY.STRING: Upgrades an Observation Post to an Air Defense Gun, Air Defense or SAM Installation, depending on your current level of technology.

ENTRY.name: ENTRENCH
ENTRY.STRING: Entrenches Barracks units near the General. Entrenched units are protected from much of the damage they receive from enemies in front of them (flanking damage is unaffected) and from splash damage regardless of direction.

ENTRY.name: BRIBE
ENTRY.STRING: Once you bribe an enemy unit it becomes yours! Spies must stand adjacent to enemy siege and artillery units to bribe them. Spies cannot bribe non-combat units (e.g., Citizens, Scholars, Merchants, Caravans).

ENTRY.name: BRIBEV
ENTRY.STRING: You can also bribe enemy buildings in your territory. You can bribe towns and buildings controlled by an ally who has resigned or otherwise left the game. Spies themselves are immune to bribes but are vulnerable to counterintelligence.

ENTRY.name: INFORMER
ENTRY.STRING:
When an enemy unit or building becomes your Informer:
You can see what is inside (e.g., what is being built at a barracks).
You can see everything that the unit or building can see.

ENTRY.name: INFORMERV
ENTRY.STRING:
An Informer at a Library lets you see your enemy's research progress.
An Informer at a Market lets you see your enemy's resource levels.
An Informer at a Barracks (or other unit creation building) shows you the number of each unit type your enemy has constructed.

ENTRY.name: PILFER
ENTRY.STRING:
To Pilfer Resources, target an enemy {City}.
Pilfering takes resources out of your enemy's treasury and adds it to yours.

ENTRY.name: PILFERV
ENTRY.STRING:
Pilfering is especially useful against large cities.
Repeated pilfering against the same target will achieve diminishing results.

ENTRY.name: COUNTERINTEL
ENTRY.STRING:
Destroys enemy Spies.
Removes Informers your enemy has placed among your buildings and units.

ENTRY.name: AMBUSH
ENTRY.STRING: Allows a General and all your units nearby to become temporarily invisible to enemy forces.

ENTRY.name: AMBUSHV
ENTRY.STRING: As long as units in ambush don't attack, only Scouts, Scout Vessels, Subs, Helicopters, and Lookout/Radar/SAM sites can see them.

ENTRY.name: FORCEDMARCH
ENTRY.STRING: Allows your units near the General to move faster than usual for a short period of time.

ENTRY.name: FORCEDMARCHV
ENTRY.STRING: The effect is strongest for foot units, weaker for mounted and vehicle units.

ENTRY.name: RALLY
ENTRY.STRING: Increases the speed at which friendly units near the General attack for a short period of time.

ENTRY.name: CREATEDECOY
ENTRY.STRING: Create phantom decoy units who look and behave just like real units to your enemies, but cause no damage when they attack. Use them to fool and distract your enemies.

ENTRY.name: SNIPER
ENTRY.STRING:
Kills an enemy unit or destroys an enemy vehicle.
Cannot be used to assassinate civilian units (e.g., Citizens, Scholars, Merchants, Caravans). Generals and Supply Wagons are considered fair game, however.

ENTRY.name: SNIPERV
ENTRY.STRING: Foot and horse units may be sniped at range, but enemy vehicles must be approached at close range ("for planting charges").

ENTRY.name: SABOTAGE
ENTRY.STRING: Severely damages an enemy building.

ENTRY.name: PARADROP
ENTRY.STRING:
Inserts units quickly behind enemy lines.
Can only be initiated from within friendly territory.

ENTRY.name: JAMRADAR
ENTRY.STRING: Temporarily disables enemy Anti-Air defenses nearby.

ENTRY.name: PACK
ENTRY.STRING: Pack your units when you want to move them--certain types of unit can only move while packed.

ENTRY.name: DEPLOY
ENTRY.STRING: Deploy your units when you are ready to use them, but remember they cannot move while deployed.

ENTRY.name: ASSIMILATE
ENTRY.STRING: Assimilating captured enemy cities fully converts them to your color; this takes a period of time after you capture the city.

ENTRY.name: ASSIMILATEV
ENTRY.STRING: Once you have assimilated a city you can freely create citizens; the land around the city will become part of your national territory.

ENTRY.name: RAZE
ENTRY.STRING: Razes a building to the ground.

ENTRY.name: TRANSPORT
ENTRY.STRING:
{Transporting} ground units {by sea} does not require you to build special transport ships.
Once you have the appropriate #ICON27 Science technology and at least one Dock, you can simply select the units and order them into the water.
When {Auto Transport} is on, ground units will automatically board transports to cross water.
When {Auto Transport} is off, unit will not board transports, and units already on transports must be manually disembarked.

ENTRY.name: TRANSPORTDOCK
ENTRY.STRING:
{Transporting} ground units {by sea} does not require you to build special transport ships.
Once you have at least one Dock and the appropriate level of #ICON27 Science technology, you can simply select the units and order them into the water.
To land transported troops, simply select their ships and order them back onto the land.

ENTRY.name: DOW
ENTRY.STRING: Declare War -- this carries a resource cost if you are currently at peace with your target.

ENTRY.name: DOW2
ENTRY.STRING: Cancel Alliance -- this carries a resource cost

ENTRY.name: DISBAND
ENTRY.STRING: Disband permanently eliminates a unit you don't want anymore.

ENTRY.name: PINGV
ENTRY.STRING:
Signals are useful in multiplayer games for communicating with other players.
In solo games, signals tell your computer allies where to concentrate their attacks.

ENTRY.name: BUY
ENTRY.STRING: Buying allows you to trade #ICON3 wealth for commodities you need. Click to buy 100, SHIFT + click to buy 500, or ALT + click to buy as much as you can afford.

ENTRY.name: BUYV
ENTRY.STRING: Buying a commodity will drive up the price for all players. Market prices fluctuate over time; if you watch the market carefully you may be able to get a better price.

ENTRY.name: SELL
ENTRY.STRING: Selling allows you to sell excess commodities to obtain #ICON3 wealth. Click to sell 100, SHIFT + click to sell 500, or ALT + click to sell everything you have.

ENTRY.name: SELLV
ENTRY.STRING: Selling a commodity will drive down the price for all players. Market prices fluctuate over time; if you watch the market carefully you may be able to get a better price.

ENTRY.name: STATATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: The higher a unit or building's attack strength, the more damage it will do to enemies.

ENTRY.name: STATANTIAIR
ENTRY.STRING: This unit/building can only attack aircraft. The higher its Anti-Air strength, the more damage it will do to enemy aircraft.

ENTRY.name: STATARMOR
ENTRY.STRING: Armor helps protect units and buildings from damage. Higher armor value prevents more damage; but there is no armor value that offers invincibility.

ENTRY.name: STATHITPOINTS
ENTRY.STRING: When a unit or building's hit points reach zero, it is destroyed.

ENTRY.name: STATSUPPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Units that are supported by friendly units nearby are stronger in combat; they cause more damage and receive less damage. The support display lights up when a unit is being supported.

ENTRY.name: STATRANGE
ENTRY.STRING: Range indicates how far a unit or building can fire its weapon. Hold the mouse over this icon, and see the main map for a visual indication of the Range of the selected unit(s) or building(s).

ENTRY.name: STATCLOAK
ENTRY.STRING: Hidden units can only be detected by enemy Scouts, Scout Vessels, Subs, Helicopters, and Lookout/Radar/SAM sites. Some units, such as Submarines, are usually hidden even while they are moving; others are hidden only when they aren't moving.

ENTRY.name: STATBLOWNCLOAK
ENTRY.STRING: Normally, hidden units are revealed when they perform certain activities such as attacking. Some units, such as Submarines, are usually hidden even while they are moving; others are hidden only when they aren't moving.

ENTRY.name: STATENTRENCHED
ENTRY.STRING: Entrenched units take only $NUMBER0% damage from splash weapons and frontal attacks (non-splash damage from the flanks is unaffected) as long as they remain in their current entrenched location.

ENTRY.name: STATGARRISON
ENTRY.STRING: Shows population value of units currently garrisoned and the maximum population value that can garrison in this building. Most units have a population value of 1, but some larger units such as siege units and heavy ships have a higher value and therefore take up more space.

ENTRY.name: STATGARRISONQ
ENTRY.STRING: An asterisk indicates that the building can garrison mounted and foot troops.

ENTRY.name: STATPOWER
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the garrison's current power compared to the maximum power. The higher the power, the more often the building is able to attack.

ENTRY.name: STATPOWERNV
ENTRY.STRING: Power can be increased by adding more foot units (especially ranged foot units) to the building.

ENTRY.name: STATPOWERV
ENTRY.STRING: Some buildings (e.g., Towers, Forts, etc.) have an intrinsic power level. Others (e.g., Cities) do not. The power level can be increased by adding more FOOT troops to the garrison. Additional power is computed by dividing the total strength of all foot units in the garrison (non-ranged combat units count half value) by the strength of the building.

ENTRY.name: STATCRAFT
ENTRY.STRING: Some units spend Craft to use special abilities. To replenish Craft, the unit must rest before using its abilities again.

ENTRY.name: STATSUPPLIES
ENTRY.STRING: Supply units carry limited supplies; each time they repair a hit point of damage their supplies deplete. When the supplies are completely depleted the unit is eliminated.

ENTRY.name: STATDECOY
ENTRY.STRING: Decoy units remain in existence for a limited period and then disappear.

ENTRY.name: STATAIRBASE
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the number of aircraft currently based here and the total number of aircraft able to be based here.

ENTRY.name: STATSPLASH
ENTRY.STRING: Units with splash damage deal some damage to all enemy units near their target; the target itself takes the most damage if it is hit dead-on.

ENTRY.name: STATBUILDINGS
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the number of unique buildings required for the next upgrade of this City.

ENTRY.name: STATINSUPPLY
ENTRY.STRING:
Artillery is In Supply if it is in friendly territory or within range of a Supply Wagon.
When Out of Supply, artillery reloads at half speed.

ENTRY.name: STATOUTOFSUPPLY
ENTRY.STRING:
Artillery is In Supply if it is in friendly territory or within range of a Supply Wagon.
When Out of Supply, gunpowder artillery reloads at $NUMBER0% normal speed.
When Out of Supply, pre-gunpowder siege units reload at $NUMBER1% normal speed.

ENTRY.name: STATATTRITION
ENTRY.STRING:
Units and buildings receiving attrition take damage gradually over time. The value indicates the rate attrition damage is being inflicted; higher values indicate more severe damage. Attrition damage is dependent on the number of technologies your enemy has researched, and on whether your enemy has reached a more advanced age than your nation. Attrition can be caused by:
Entering the territory of an enemy who has researched Allegiance at the Tower unless in range of one of your Supply Wagons.
Entering Russian territory unless in range of one of your Supply Wagons.
Entering the territory of a nation with whom you are at peace.
Entering the territory of a nation other than your assigned target during an Assassin game.
Buildings stranded in another (non-allied) nation's territory always receive attrition damage.

ENTRY.name: STATRADIUS
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the economic radius of this City.Buildings constructed within this radius contribute to (and benefit from) the City's economy.

ENTRY.name: INFORMER
ENTRY.STRING: An enemy Informer has infiltrated this unit or building. The enemy can see what is happening inside this building, and can see with this building's Line-of-Sight. Use the Counterintelligence ability of a Spy or Scout to remove this Informer.

ENTRY.name: INFORMERMINE
ENTRY.STRING: You have infiltrated this unit or building with an Informer, allowing you to use its Line-of-Sight and see what is going on inside.

ENTRY.name: WOODCUTTERHEADS
ENTRY.STRING: These heads indicate how many Citizens are working at this Woodcutter's camp, and the maximum number which are allowed to work here.The more trees nearby, the larger the number of workers allowed. Colored heads indicate workers already assigned here; grey heads indicate open slots for more Citizens to be assigned.

ENTRY.name: MINEHEADS
ENTRY.STRING: These heads indicate how many Citizens are working at this Mine, and the maximum number which are allowed to work here.The more mountains nearby, the larger the number of workers allowed.Colored heads indicate workers already assigned here; grey heads indicate open slots for more Citizens to be assigned.

ENTRY.name: UNIVERSITYHEADS
ENTRY.STRING: These heads indicate how many Scholars are studying at the University of the 7 which are allowed.Colored heads indicate Scholars already assigned here; grey heads indicate open slots for more Scholars to be assigned.

ENTRY.name: FARMHEADS
ENTRY.STRING: One Citizen can gather #ICON1 Food at each Farm.A colored head indicates a Citizen is already assigned here; a grey head indicates this Farm is fallow and will not produce any #ICON1 Food until a Citizen is assigned.

ENTRY.name: OILHEADS
ENTRY.STRING: One Citizen can gather Oil at each Oil Well. A colored head indicates a Citizen is already assigned here; a grey head indicates this Oil Well is idle and will not produce any Oil until a Citizen is assigned.

ENTRY.name: CAPITALTIMER
ENTRY.STRING: A player whose Capital has been captured must recapture it within the time alotted or be eliminated.

ENTRY.name: TERRITORYTIMER
ENTRY.STRING: Once a player or team controls the designated percentage of the world's territory, a Territory Victory Timer starts.When the timer expires, that player or team will win.To stop that player or team from winning, opponents must reduce the amount of territory controlled below the victory threshold for the game.

ENTRY.name: WONDERTIMER
ENTRY.STRING: Once a player or team's wonder points exceed all other players/teams by a designated amount, a Wonder Victory Timer starts.When the timer expires, that player or team will win.To stop that player or team from winning, opponents must either build, capture, or destroy enough wonders to reduce the difference in wonder points.

ENTRY.name: ARMAGEDDONCLOCK
ENTRY.STRING: Whenever a nuclear weapon detonates, the Armageddon Clock is lowered.If the Armageddon Clock reaches zero, the game ends in {Armageddon} with all players defeated.

ENTRY.name: IDLECITIZEN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select and center on an idle Citizen. When this button is lit, it means that at least one of your Citizens doesn't have anything to do.

ENTRY.name: IDLECITIZENV
ENTRY.STRING:
{SHIFT + click} to select ALL idle Citizens at once.
{CTRL + click} to select the next idle Citizen without centering. {ALT + click} to select and center on the next Citizen (whether idle or not).{ALT + SHIFT + click} to select ALL Citizens (whether idle or not). {ALT + CTRL + click} to select the next Citizen (whether idle or not) without centering.

ENTRY.name: NEXTMILITARY
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to select and center on the next military unit (or group of military units).

ENTRY.name: NEXTMILITARYV
ENTRY.STRING: Multiple clicks will cycle you through all of your military units. SHIFT + clicking will select all of your military units at once (or as many as will fit in the selection list). CTRL + click will select the next unit (or group) without centering on it.

ENTRY.name: NEXTGENERAL
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to select and center on one of your Generals.

ENTRY.name: NEXTGENERALV
ENTRY.STRING: Multiple clicks will cycle you through all of your Generals. SHIFT + clicking will select ALL your generals at once. CTRL + click will select the next General without centering on it.

ENTRY.name: NEXTBUILDING
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to select a building of this type. Double Click to select and zoom to the building. Triple Click (or SHIFT + click) to select all buildings of this type.

ENTRY.name: CYCLERESEARCH
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to find the next building that has a new technology available for research.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOMACY
ENTRY.STRING: Through Diplomacy you can negotiate peace treaties and alliances with other players. You can also send and receive extra resources.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOMACYNOTMET
ENTRY.STRING: Through Diplomacy you can negotiate peace treaties and alliances with other players, but you cannot negotiate with a nation until you have made contact with it on the map.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOCLOSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to close the diplomacy window and display the normal interface.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOBACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the main diplomacy screen showing the list of all nations in the game.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOGOOD
ENTRY.STRING: These are the number of resources you have available to offer to another nation. The amounts are equal to the amounts currently in your treasury minus the value currently being offered in the right column.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSWAR
ENTRY.STRING: You are at war with this nation and can now attack one another.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSINFOALLY
ENTRY.STRING: You are allied with this nation. When two nations are allied, their territories are combined, and they jointly win the game if they fulfill the victory conditions. With the sufficient number of #ICON27 Science technologies, allies can also see each other's Lines-of-Sight. Except in a {Team} game, you may not have more than one ally at a time.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSINFOPEACE
ENTRY.STRING: You are at peace with this nation.Nations at peace cannot attack one another without declaring war first.Military units which violate a peace treaty by entering a nation's territory suffer severe attrition damage.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSINFOWAR
ENTRY.STRING: You are at war with this nation.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSALLY
ENTRY.STRING:
You are allied with this nation; click here to break the alliance and change your status to peace.
When two nations are allied, their territories are combined, and they jointly win the game if they fulfill the victory conditions. With the sufficient number of #ICON27 Science technologies, allies can also see each other's Lines-of-Sight. Except in a {Team} game, you may not have more than one ally at a time.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOSTATUSPEACE
ENTRY.STRING: You are at peace with this nation; click here to declare war.Nations at peace cannot attack one another without declaring war first.Military units which violate a peace treaty by entering a nation's territory suffer severe attrition damage.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOALLY
ENTRY.STRING: Select this option to propose an alliance with this nation.When two nations are allied, their territories are combined, and they jointly win the game if they fulfill the victory conditions.With a sufficient number of #ICON27 Science technologies, allies can also see each other's Lines of Sight.Except in a {Team} game, you may not have more than one ally at a time.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOPEACE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to propose peace with this nation.Nations at peace cannot attack one another without declaring war first.Military units which violate a peace treaty by entering a nation's territory suffer severe attrition damage.

ENTRY.name: DIPLONOCHANGE
ENTRY.STRING: Select this option if you don't wish to change your current status with this nation.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOOFFER
ENTRY.STRING: Click to offer resources to this nation (or to reduce the amount you are demanding).Each click adds 100 to your offer (or lowers your demand by the same amount).Some resources are lost to corruption: the recipient gets $NUMBER0% of the resources plus $NUMBER1% for each #ICON26 Commerce technology you have researched.You can SHIFT click to add 500 to your offer, or ALT click to offer everything you have.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOCLEAR
ENTRY.STRING: Click to clear all diplomatic proposals.This zeroes out all offers/demands for resources and cancels any proposed treaty or mutual attack.

ENTRY.name: DIPLODEMAND
ENTRY.STRING:
Click to demand resources from this nation (or to reduce the amount you are offering)
Each click adds 100 to your demands (or lowers your offer by the same amount). Some resources are lost to corruption: the recipient gets $NUMBER0% of the resources plus $NUMBER1% for each #ICON26 Commerce technology the nation has researched. You can SHIFT click to increase your demands by 500 or ALT click to increase your demands by 1000.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to propose a mutual attack. If both nations agree, both declare war on the specified nation.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOPROPOSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to make this proposal to this nation. If the nation accepts, the deal will be completed.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOGIFT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send resources to this nation.Since you are not asking anything in return, the gift will be immediately and automatically accepted by the other nation.Some resources are lost to corruption: the recipient gets $NUMBER0% of the resources plus $NUMBER1% for each #ICON26 Commerce technology you have researched.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOTRIB
ENTRY.STRING: Click to demand resources from the other nation.The player controlling that nation will be informed of your demand and may decide whether or not to accede to it.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOPAY
ENTRY.STRING: This nation has demanded resources from you (and is offering nothing in return). Click here to meet these demands by sending the resources; they will be transferred immediately from your treasury.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOCANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel your offer to this nation.Unless you cancel or change it, the offer will remain on the table until this nation accepts or rejects it.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOCANCELTRIB
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel your demand for tribute.Unless you cancel or change it, the demand will remain on the table until this nation accepts or rejects it.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOACCEPT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to accept the proposal on the table. If you accept, the proposed deal will be completed immediately.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOREJECT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to reject the proposal on the table.

ENTRY.name: DIPLOREFUSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to refuse to pay the tribute demanded.

ENTRY.name: TOGGLEADVANCED
ENTRY.STRING: {Advanced Options} turns on additional buttons and displays, which are not necessary to play the game but may come in handy for veteran players.

ENTRY.name: TOGGLEHELP
ENTRY.STRING: {Help Text Detail} allows you to choose the level of rollover text shown on-screen. Clicking the button toggles the settings between LOW, MEDIUM, and HIGH.

ENTRY.name: OBJECTIVES
ENTRY.STRING: Click to see the objectives and victory conditions for the current game.

ENTRY.name: MENU
ENTRY.STRING: From the game menu you can {Save} and {Load} games, {Resign} or {Quit} the game, or adjust your preferences.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: Toggles the {Resource Interface}. When turned on, shows the number of resources you have of each type.

ENTRY.name: MARKET
ENTRY.STRING: Toggles the {Market Interface}. When turned on, you can see current market prices and buy/sell directly from the main display.

ENTRY.name: INFRA
ENTRY.STRING: Toggles the {Infrastructure Interface}, which shows additional economic information.

ENTRY.name: MAIN
ENTRY.STRING: Toggles the {Main Interface}. When turned off, you still see streamlined data when you select a friendly unit/building or when one of your Citizens is placing a building.

ENTRY.name: LIBRARY
ENTRY.STRING: Toggles the {Mini Library}, which shows a smaller library interface that you can use.

ENTRY.name: PAUSE
ENTRY.STRING: Pauses and unpauses the game. In solo games you can freely examine units and give orders while the game is paused.In multiplayer games you cannot give orders unless the {Free Pause} option was selected.In multiplayer games you are normally limited in the number of times you can pause the game.

ENTRY.name: MOVETO
ENTRY.STRING:
Units {Move} to a map location you select.
Use this command to flee from enemies; units will not engage enemies along the way.
Click a unit to {follow} that unit.

ENTRY.name: ATTACKTO
ENTRY.STRING:
{Attacks} a target you select.
Target on a map location for {Attack Move}; units will fight any enemy they encounter while moving to that location.
You can also CTRL + right-click on a location to order an Attack Move.
With siege/artillery units selected, ALT + right-click to order a {Siege Attack}. Your siege units will attack the target and your non-siege units will guard the siege units.

ENTRY.name: EXPLORETO
ENTRY.STRING: When you give an {Explore Move} order, units will stop to explore any Ruins they encounter on the way to their destination.

ENTRY.name: FLEETO
ENTRY.STRING: When you give a {Flee} order, units will flee in the direction you choose avoiding enemies along the way.

ENTRY.name: PATROL
ENTRY.STRING:
Patrolling units will walk back and forth between their current location and one or more points you specify.
Combat units on patrol will attack any enemies they encounter.

ENTRY.name: GUARD
ENTRY.STRING: Combat units assigned to Guard will stay close to the friendly unit you specify and attack anything that threatens it.

ENTRY.name: FOLLOW
ENTRY.STRING: Units assigned to {Follow} will follow the unit you specify.

ENTRY.name: FORWARD
ENTRY.STRING:
Units move forward a short distance, attacking any enemies they encounter.
Useful for advancing in formation.

ENTRY.name: AUTOEXPLORE
ENTRY.STRING: Units ordered to {Auto Explore} will automatically seek out and explore areas of the map you haven't visited yet.

ENTRY.name: FORM
ENTRY.STRING:
{Formation} tells your units what formation (if any) to keep when they line up. Possible settings include:
{Line} tells them to line up.
{Echelon Right/Left} protects/attacks a flank.
{Refused} tells them to protect both flanks.
{Envelop} is useful for surrounding enemies.

ENTRY.name: STANCECITIZEN
ENTRY.STRING: The {stance} tells your citizens what work they are allowed to look for when they are idle. You can choose from {Gather}, {Build and Gather}, {Build}, and {Idle}.

ENTRY.name: NEXTDATA
ENTRY.STRING: {Data Display} shows the information on the minimap and the text just above this button.

ENTRY.name: NEXTDATAV
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cycle through the different displays.

ENTRY.name: DATAOFF
ENTRY.STRING: When Data Display is {OFF}, no text information is shown in this section of the screen; the minimap shows the locations of all known units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATAPOLITICAL
ENTRY.STRING: The {POLITICAL} display shows each nation's current standing in the game and the percentage of the world's territory it controls. It also shows, if applicable, the net wonder score for the team or nation with the most wonders.

ENTRY.name: DATAMILITARY
ENTRY.STRING: {MILITARY} shows your military units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATAMILITARYV
ENTRY.STRING: Also shows strategic military buildings such as Cities, Forts and Towers.

ENTRY.name: DATAECONOMY
ENTRY.STRING: {ECONOMY} gives information on resource production and idle citizens.

ENTRY.name: DATAECONOMYV
ENTRY.STRING: Also shows the location of non-military units and all buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATAMARKET
ENTRY.STRING: {MARKET} shows current market prices for resources and the location of all known units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATACITIES
ENTRY.STRING: The {CITIES} display shows current building and infrastructure information.

ENTRY.name: DATACITIESV
ENTRY.STRING: In this mode the minimap shows the locations of all known buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATATECHNOLOGY
ENTRY.STRING: {UPGRADES} shows which buildings currently have technology researches available and the location of all known units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATAGROUPS
ENTRY.STRING: {GROUPS} shows your groups or armies, and the location of all known units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: DATAGROUPSV
ENTRY.STRING: Select units or buildings and right-click the display to create a new group. Left-click the display to select an existing group.

ENTRY.name: DATADIPLOMACY
ENTRY.STRING: {DIPLOMACY} shows your diplomatic status with other nations.

ENTRY.name: DATAWONDERS
ENTRY.STRING: {WONDERS} shows information on Wonders of the World.

ENTRY.name: STANCE
ENTRY.STRING: {Stance} automatically adjusts aggression of your combat units. Aggressive units attack and chase anything they see. Units set to {Defensive} attack anything nearby but don't give chase. Units told to {Raid} pefer to target civilian units. Units told to {Raze} prefer to target buildings. Units told to {Stand Ground} can attack but do not move from their positions.

ENTRY.name: BUILDANDGATHER
ENTRY.STRING: Idle Citizens set to {Build and Gather} automatically help build, then gather resources, then repair damaged structures.

ENTRY.name: BUILD
ENTRY.STRING: Idle Citizens set to {Build and Repair} automatically help build, then repair damaged structures.

ENTRY.name: GATHER
ENTRY.STRING: Idle Citizens set to {Gather} automatically look for open resource gathering buildings (Farms, Mines, etc.).

ENTRY.name: STAND
ENTRY.STRING: Citizens set to {Idle} will take no action unless explicitly ordered.

ENTRY.name: AGGRESSIVE
ENTRY.STRING: When not already performing another order, units set to {Aggressive} will automatically chase and attack any enemies they encounter.

ENTRY.name: RAID
ENTRY.STRING: When not already performing another order, units set to {Raid} will automatically chase and attack any enemies they encounter, but they will seek out economic targets (such as enemy Citizens and Caravans) in preference to military targets.

ENTRY.name: RAZE
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Raze} will chase and attack enemies, but prefer to target enemy buildings.

ENTRY.name: DEFENSIVE
ENTRY.STRING: When not already performing another order, units set to {Defensive} will automatically attack any enemies they encounter, but will only chase them for a limited distance before returning to the spot where you left them.

ENTRY.name: STANDGROUND
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Stand Ground} will attack enemies they encounter, but will not move to chase them.

ENTRY.name: HOLDFIRE
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Hold Fire} will not attack enemies, nor move to engage them, even if they are attacked.

ENTRY.name: AUTO
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Auto Craft} will automatically use their special abilities defensively.

ENTRY.name: NONE
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Manual Craft} will never use special abilities unless explicitly ordered.

ENTRY.name: AUTO
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Auto Deploy} will deploy themselves for action whenever they are left idle for more than five seconds (one second for machine guns).

ENTRY.name: NEVER
ENTRY.STRING: Units set to {Manual Deploy} will only deploy when explicitly ordered.

ENTRY.name: TIMBER
ENTRY.STRING: {Timber} is gathered by building {Woodcutter's Camps} near {trees} and manning them with Citizens.

ENTRY.name: TIMBERV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g., "$NUMBER0") indicates how much Timber you currently have; the second number (e.g., "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are collecting it.
Timber is useful for making buildings, ships, and siege engines; it is also required for #ICON26 Commerce advances.

ENTRY.name: WEALTH
ENTRY.STRING:
{Wealth} can be collected by building {Markets} and by using {Caravans} to form trade routes between your {Cities}.
Wealth can also be accumulated through {taxation}: that is, based on the percentage of the total world landmass you control. Taxation can be researched at the Temple.

ENTRY.name: WEALTHV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g., "$NUMBER0") indicates how much Wealth you currently have; the second number (e.g., "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are collecting it.
Many units and all #ICON27 Science technologies require Wealth.

ENTRY.name: KNOWLEDGE
ENTRY.STRING:
{Knowledge} is accumulated by creating {Scholars} at a {University}.
The more Scholars you have, the faster you will accumulate Knowledge.
Unlike other resources Knowledge can never be bought, sold, or exchanged.

ENTRY.name: KNOWLEDGEV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g. "$NUMBER0") indicates how much Knowledge you currently have; the second number (e.g. "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are accumulating it.
Most Library research (including Age advances) requires the use of Knowledge.

ENTRY.name: METAL
ENTRY.STRING: {Metal} is gathered by building {Mines} near {mountains} or {cliffs} and manning them with Citizens.

ENTRY.name: METALV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g., "$NUMBER0") indicates how much Metal you currently have; the second number (e.g., "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are gathering it.
#ICON24 Military research and most of the more powerful military units require Metal.

ENTRY.name: OIL
ENTRY.STRING: {Oil} is gathered by building {Oil Wells} on oil deposits and manning them with Citizens.

ENTRY.name: OILV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g., "$NUMBER0") indicates how much Oil you currently have; the second number (e.g., "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are gathering it.
Oil is required for most modern units.

ENTRY.name: FOOD
ENTRY.STRING: {Food} is gathered by building {Farms} and manning them with Citizens.

ENTRY.name: FOODV
ENTRY.STRING:
The first number (e.g., $NUMBER0) indicates how much Food you currently have; the second number (e.g., "+$STRING0") shows how fast you are gathering it.
Food is required to build citizens and cities, for Age advances, #ICON25 Civic research, and many types of unit.
Food can also be gathered by building Fishermen and placing them near Fish and Whale resources.

ENTRY.name: LAND4
ENTRY.STRING: Build a Woodcutter's Camp nearby to gather #ICON2 Timber.

ENTRY.name: LAND5
ENTRY.STRING: Build a Mine nearby to gather #ICON5 Metal.

ENTRY.name: LAND5A
ENTRY.STRING: Starting in #ICON28 Classical Age, build a Mine nearby to gather #ICON5 Metal.

ENTRY.name: LAND6
ENTRY.STRING:
Units can move through rocks but buildings cannot be placed here.
Starting in #ICON32 Industrial Age, #ICON6 Oil deposits can be discovered among rocks.
Light Infantry, Gunpowder Infantry, and Modern Infantry receive a 33% defensive benefit in rocks.

ENTRY.name: LAND7
ENTRY.STRING: Build an Oil Well here to gather #ICON6 Oil.

ENTRY.name: LAND7A
ENTRY.STRING: Build an Oil Platform here to gather #ICON6 Oil.

ENTRY.name: LAND8
ENTRY.STRING:
Units firing from the tops of cliffs gain a combat bonus.
Build a Mine nearby to gather #ICON5 Metal.

ENTRY.name: LAND8A
ENTRY.STRING:
Units firing from the tops of cliffs gain a combat bonus.
Starting in #ICON28 Classical Age, build a Mine nearby to gather #ICON5 Metal.

ENTRY.name: LAND9
ENTRY.STRING: Units move slowly when crossing a river. Units attempting to cross rivers receive additional damage if attacked.

ENTRY.name: GOODCAPPED
ENTRY.STRING: When {Flashing Yellow}, you need to increase your #ICON21 Commerce Cap, usually by researching #ICON26 Commerce at the Library. For more details, refer to the #ICON21 {Commerce Cap} shown at the upper left of the screen above your resource totals.

ENTRY.name: POPCAP
ENTRY.STRING:
Shows your population's ability to support units (e.g., Citizens, Scouts, Hoplites, Frigates, etc.)
To increase, research Military #ICON24 at the Library.
When {Flashing}, you have reached your #ICON20 Population Limit and are currently unable to produce additional units.

ENTRY.name: POPCAPV
ENTRY.STRING:
The number on the left (e.g., "$NUMBER0") shows your current unit level; the number on the right (e.g., "$NUMBER1") shows the maximum unit level you can currently support.
Larger units (e.g., horses, vehicles, and artillery) require more population to support them than smaller units (e.g., civilians and infantry).

ENTRY.name: RESCAPNV
ENTRY.STRING:
Shows the maximum rate at which you can collect any resource.
When {Flashing}, you have reached your #ICON21 Commerce Cap in one or more resources--you can't collect that resource any faster no matter how many more gatherers or gather buildings you create.
To increase this limit, you must research {Commerce} #ICON26 at the Library.

ENTRY.name: RESCAPV
ENTRY.STRING:
Shows the maximum rate you can collect any resource (except Knowledge, which is exempt).
If this rate is {Flashing}, then you have reached your #ICON21 Commerce Cap in one or more of your resources--you won't collect that resource any faster no matter how many more gatherers or gather buildings you create.
To increase this limit, you must research {Commerce} #ICON26 at the Library.
Some Wonders of the World (Pyramids and Kremlin) also raise your #ICON21 Commerce Cap, as does sending a Merchant to gather Diamonds.

ENTRY.name: CITYCAP
ENTRY.STRING:
Shows how many cities you have and how many you are allowed to have.
You can increase your City Limit by researching #ICON25 Civics at the Library.

ENTRY.name: CITYCAPV
ENTRY.STRING: Building new cities helps you claim new territory, gives you access to new resources, and allows you to form trade routes between your cities to accumulate Wealth.

ENTRY.name: CARAVANCAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many Caravan trade routes you currently have and how many you are allowed to have. You can increase your #ICON9 Caravan Limit by researching #ICON26 Commerce at the Library.

ENTRY.name: CARAVANCAPV
ENTRY.STRING: The more Caravans you have plying trade routes, the more Wealth you will accumulate.

ENTRY.name: FOODCAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many of your Citizens are working on Farms and the number of Farms you currently have.

ENTRY.name: WOODCAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many Citizens are gathering Timber and the maximum you can currently have based on the Woodcutter's Camps you have built. To increase the maximum build more Woodcutter's Camps.

ENTRY.name: METALCAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many Citizens are gathering Metal and the maximum you can currently have based on the Mines you have built. To increase the maximum build more Mines.

ENTRY.name: KNOWLEDGECAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many Scholars are at your Universities and how many spaces are currently available for additional Scholars. To create additional spaces build more Universities.

ENTRY.name: OILCAP
ENTRY.STRING: Shows how many Citizens are working on Oil Wells and the number of Oil Wells you currently have.

ENTRY.name: AIRSTRIKE
ENTRY.STRING: Orders your planes to attack a target or patrol an area.

ENTRY.name: AIRTRANSFER
ENTRY.STRING: Orders your planes to a new home base.

ENTRY.name: AIRPATROL
ENTRY.STRING: Orders your planes to fly to the point you indicate and attack any targets they find.

ENTRY.name: AIRFIGHTERS
ENTRY.STRING: Selects all of your fighters and biplanes.

ENTRY.name: AIRBOMBERS
ENTRY.STRING: Selects all of your bombers.

ENTRY.name: AIRHELIS
ENTRY.STRING: Selects all of your helicopters.

ENTRY.name: AIRALL
ENTRY.STRING: Selects all of your aircraft.

ENTRY.name: AIRREPEAT
ENTRY.STRING: While on, aircraft returning to base will automatically repeat their missions as soon as they refuel. While off, aircraft returning to base will remain there.

ENTRY.name: AIRRETURN
ENTRY.STRING: Orders this aircraft to abort its mission and return to base.

ENTRY.name: AIRRECALL
ENTRY.STRING: Orders all aircraft currently flying from this base to abort their missions and return.

ENTRY.name: AIRSCRAMBLE
ENTRY.STRING: Orders all aircraft into the air immediately. Once launched, aircraft will attack any nearby enemies they see.

ENTRY.name: AIRFUELING
ENTRY.STRING: Aircraft in this column are {refueling} after a mission.

ENTRY.name: AIRREADY
ENTRY.STRING: Aircraft in this column are {ready} and can be given new orders.

ENTRY.name: RESPAWN
ENTRY.STRING: Aircraft Carriers which lose aircraft are gradually resupplied with new planes over a period of time.

ENTRY.name: T0
T1
ENTRY.STRING: You can select a group of units by using the left mouse button to drag a box around them. You can move the selected units by left-clicking {Move} and then left-clicking a location.

ENTRY.name: T2
ENTRY.STRING: To gather the #ICON1 {Food} resource, Citizens must build {Farms} near your cities. One Citizen can work on each Farm. Since each city can have only five farms, you may need to build additional cities to increase your food production.

ENTRY.name: T3
ENTRY.STRING: Your {Library} is where your nation conducts research. Select your {Library} to research different technologies--{Military} #ICON24, {Civics} #ICON25, {Commerce} #ICON26, and {Science} #ICON27. Each technology you research will open up new opportunities for your nation. Once you have completed enough research the {Age} #ICON23 button will light up, and you will be able to advance to a new Age of history.

ENTRY.name: T4
ENTRY.STRING: Create additional {Citizens} by selecting your City and clicking the {Create Citizen} button. You can queue up several citizens to be created one at a time. If you change your mind, you can cancel unit creation by clicking on the Citizen icon in the queue on the right side of the screen.

ENTRY.name: T5
ENTRY.STRING: Move your {Scout} into the unexplored (dark) areas of the map to reveal hidden territory. Your scout can also explore {Ruins} to gain resource bonuses and discover rare resources such as Silk, Wine, and Horses, which Merchants from your Market can claim.

ENTRY.name: T6
ENTRY.STRING: To gather the #ICON2 {Timber} resource, build {Woodcutter's Camps} near forests. The more trees near a Woodcutter's Camp, the more citizens will be able to work there (and the faster you will gather Timber). Gathering Timber will never "exhaust" the trees in your forests, but once a Woodcutter's Camp is "full" of citizens you will need to build additional Woodcutter's Camps near other forests in order to gather Timber faster.

ENTRY.name: T7
ENTRY.STRING: Your {Capital} city is the political center of your nation and you must defend it at all costs. You can eliminate an enemy nation by conquering and holding its Capital. To increase the defenses of your Capital, consider building a Temple, Towers, and Forts. City defense also increases slightly with each additional building constructed.

ENTRY.name: T8
ENTRY.STRING: Your {National Borders} extend out from your cities based on the political strength of your nation. You can normally construct new buildings only within your national territory. To increase your national territory you must build new {Cities}, {Forts}, and {Temples}. Or, upgrade your nation's political strength by researching #ICON25 Civics.

ENTRY.name: T9
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON25 {Civic} research at the Library allows you to build additional cities; this strengthens your economy and extends your national borders.

ENTRY.name: T10
ENTRY.STRING: If you would like to adjust game options and preferences, click the {Menu} button at the upper right corner of the screen (or press {F10}) and select {Options}. You can also adjust music, sound effects, and video settings.

ENTRY.name: T11
ENTRY.STRING: You can use the {Auto Explore} button to have one or more of your units explore the map automatically, concentrating on revealing the unexplored areas of the map and finding Ruins.

ENTRY.name: T12
ENTRY.STRING: The #ICON3 {Wealth} resource is gathered by {Caravans} trading between your cities. To trade, you will need to build at least two cities, and then create Caravans at the {Market}. Wealth is also gathered by increasing the percentage of the world's territory you control; the more territory you have, the more taxes you receive. {Taxation} can be researched at the {Temple}.

ENTRY.name: T13
ENTRY.STRING: The level of #ICON24 {Military} technology you research at the Library determines your #ICON20 {Population Limit}, the maximum number of units your nation is allowed to control. If you reach your population limit, perform additional Military research at the Library. Military research also provides access to powerful Siege and Artillery units as well as better defensive fortifications. If you research more Military technology than you actually need for a particular unit or unit upgrade, the cost of the unit or upgrade is significantly reduced.

ENTRY.name: T14
ENTRY.STRING: Adjust the speed of the game by pressing the {+} and {-} keys.

ENTRY.name: T15
ENTRY.STRING: Create military units to defend your nation against enemies. Your citizens must first build a Barracks. Select a Citizen, click the {Military Buildings} button, click {Barracks}, and then click where you want the Barracks to be built.

ENTRY.name: T16
ENTRY.STRING: To see the Objectives and Victory Conditions for the current game at any time, click the {Objectives} button below the minimap at the bottom of the screen.

ENTRY.name: T17
ENTRY.STRING: Different military units have different strengths and weaknesses. For instance, Heavy Infantry units such as Hoplites and Phalanxes are very strong against cavalry and are useful when attacking enemy buildings, but are highly vulnerable to attack by Bowmen and Archers. Try to use your units in ways that take advantage of their strengths and minimize their weaknesses.

ENTRY.name: T18
ENTRY.STRING: To see the combat range of an Archer, a Tower, or another ranged unit or building, select the unit or building and then position your mouse over the {Range} icon in the lower right area of the screen. The combat range will be drawn on the main map.

ENTRY.name: T19
ENTRY.STRING: To take advantage of different levels of detail, roll your mouse wheel to zoom the view in and out. The view can also be zoomed with PAGE UP and PAGE DOWN.

ENTRY.name: T20
ENTRY.STRING: There's a maximum speed you can accumulate any resource no matter how many resource gathering buildings you construct. If one of your resources is blinking yellow, it means you are at your #ICON21 {Commerce Limit}. To increase your limit, research #ICON26 Commerce technologies at your Library.

ENTRY.name: T21
ENTRY.STRING: To lower the cost of research and make it faster, research #ICON27 {Science} technology at the Library. Science technology also allows your scouts to see farther and increases the value of the resource bonuses you gain from Ruins. Most economic upgrades require Science research.

ENTRY.name: T22
ENTRY.STRING: Attacking enemy forces from the flanks or rear causes extra damage. Send part of your force to engage the enemy from the front, and then another attachment to catch them in the flanks or rear. It is better to attack from the flank than from the rear--the disruption caused to the enemy is greater.

ENTRY.name: T23
ENTRY.STRING: Once you have researched four Library technologies (in a normal game) you can advance to the next {Age} by clicking the #ICON23 {Age Advance} button at the Library. Advancing in Age will allow you to access additional units, buildings, and technologies.

ENTRY.name: T24
