ENTRY.STRING: When you have reached at least the #ICON28 Classical Age, you can begin gathering #ICON5 {Metal}, a resource useful for building military units. To gather metal, build a {Mine} near a mountain or cliff and assign Citizens to work there. The bigger the mountain or cliff, the more Citizens you will be able to assign. To increase your Metal gathering, build Mines near additional mountains or cliffs.

ENTRY.name: T25
ENTRY.STRING: Horsemen, Knights, and other mounted units are built at the {Stable}. To build a {Stable} you must reach the #ICON28 Classical Age and research at least one #ICON24 Military technology at the Library.

ENTRY.name: T26
ENTRY.STRING: Build a {Granary} in a City to increase the #ICON1 {Food} output of all {Farms} in that city.

ENTRY.name: T27
ENTRY.STRING: Build a {Lumber Mill} in a City to increase the #ICON2 {Timber} output of all {Woodcutter's Camps} in that city.

ENTRY.name: T28
ENTRY.STRING: Build a {Smelter} in a City to increase the #ICON5 {Metal} output of all {Mines} in that city.

ENTRY.name: T29
ENTRY.STRING: To attack enemy buildings such as Towers and Cities, build {Siege} and {Artillery} units at a {Siege Factory}. In order to build a Siege Factory, you must reach the #ICON28 Classical Age and research two #ICON24 Military technologies.

ENTRY.name: T30
ENTRY.STRING: Press the SPACEBAR when you receive messages of significant events to center the screen on the most recent event. You can also use CTRL + PAGE UP and CTRL + PAGE DOWN to scroll back through previous messages.

ENTRY.name: T31
ENTRY.STRING: Clicking the {Idle Citizen} button (or pressing ".") will check to see if any of your Citizens are idle, and if so will select one of them. Citizens left idle for more than 10 seconds will also look for useful assignments on their own.

ENTRY.name: T32
ENTRY.STRING: Press SHIFT while dragging a box around units to add them to your current selection. Press CTRL while dragging a box around units to select Civilian as well as Military units. Press ALT while dragging to select only non-siege military units. Press CTRL + ALT to select only siege units. Press ALT + SHIFT to select only aircraft. Press CTRL + SHIFT to select only Citizens and Scholars. Press CTRL + ALT + SHIFT to select only Transports.

ENTRY.name: T33
ENTRY.STRING: Once you have researched two #ICON26 {Commerce} technologies, you can sell excess resources at your Market, or buy needed resources. Prices will fluctuate up and down over the course of the game, so pay attention to the trends and you can maximize the efficiency of your trading.

ENTRY.name: T34
ENTRY.STRING: To queue several consecutive move orders, press SHIFT while clicking the destinations. To tell a Citizen to build a series of buildings, press SHIFT while giving build orders. Units will execute each order, one at a time, in the sequence the orders were given. Press ALT instead of SHIFT if you want the Citizen to execute the new order before any previously given orders.

ENTRY.name: T35
ENTRY.STRING: Build a {University} to help you accumulate the #ICON4 {Knowledge} resource, which is useful for purchasing research at the Library. Create {Scholars} at your University to increase Knowledge collection. The more Scholars you have at your University, the faster you will accumulate Knowledge. Unlike other resources Knowledge can never be bought, sold, or exchanged.

ENTRY.name: T36
ENTRY.STRING: Logistics, bureaucracy, and food supply have presented significant challenges to nations throughout history. The more you build of a particular type of unit, the more each additional unit will cost and the longer it will take to be created.

ENTRY.name: T37
ENTRY.STRING: The {Advanced Options} button (or {SHIFT + Y}) turns on additional features, such as {Stance} to customize the behavior of your units.

ENTRY.name: T38
ENTRY.STRING: Use an {Attack Move} to have your troops move to a location while fighting any enemies they encounter along the way. With your troops selected, click the Attack button (or press A), and then click on a target map location. You can also simply CTRL + right-click on a map location to order an Attack Move.

ENTRY.name: T39
ENTRY.STRING: To take control of the seas, your nation will need a {Dock}. Once you have a Dock and one #ICON27 Science technology, you can transport your ground units overseas--simply order them to move into the ocean, and transport ships will automatically appear to pick them up. To land your troops simply move them back onto land. When you have two #ICON26 Commerce technologies, you can build Cities and Forts on other continents.

ENTRY.name: T40
ENTRY.STRING: Heavy ships such as Triremes are useful for achieving naval supremacy and destroying enemy shipping. Light ships, such as Dromons, can move quickly to find and destroy enemy transports. In later ages you can build Bombardment ships to attack land areas and Fire Ships and Submarines to counterattack against superior enemy navies.

ENTRY.name: T41
ENTRY.STRING: On sea maps with many islands and continents, you can build your first city on a new landmass even if your national borders do not yet extend there. The first city on a new continent or island must be placed near the coast.

ENTRY.name: T42
ENTRY.STRING: To overcome an enemy City, you need not raze it to the ground. Instead you may {capture} it by reducing its hit points to zero, eliminating any enemy Towers and Forts nearby, and achieving military superiority (i.e., having the most units, especially infantry units, in the vicinity). Once you have captured a city, a brief period is required to {assimilate} the new City into your nation.

ENTRY.name: T43
ENTRY.STRING: Building a {Temple} will increase the defensive strength of your City and will extend your National Borders. Upgrading the power of your religion at the Temple will increase these effects.

ENTRY.name: T44
ENTRY.STRING: Supply Wagons support your troops, especially when they are in enemy territory. In enemy territory, Artillery and Siege Units reload much more quickly if they are near one of your Supply Wagons. Supply Wagons also protect your nearby troops from most forms of Attrition.

ENTRY.name: T45
ENTRY.STRING: Position your forces on high ground to gain a combat advantage.

ENTRY.name: T46
ENTRY.STRING: Razing enemy buildings allows you to "plunder" resources. Attacking enemy caravans allows you to steal #ICON3 Wealth.

ENTRY.name: T47
ENTRY.STRING: {Forts} and {Castles} are strong defensive positions, which also influence National Borders. They can also garrison military units, and can create Generals and Spies.

ENTRY.name: T48
ENTRY.STRING: Garrisoning military units inside Forts, Castles, Towers, and Cities protects the units from damage and increases the attack value of the building. To garrison military units in a building, select the military units, left-click the {Garrison} button, then left-click the building. Units can also garrison inside of the kind of building that produced them (e.g., Barracks, Dock, Siege Factory). Units garrisoned in buildings are gradually healed or repaired.

ENTRY.name: T49
ENTRY.STRING: Spies can be used to {Bribe} enemy units to convert to your side. They can also recruit {Informers} among enemy buildings and troops, and employ {Counterintelligence} to destroy enemy Spies and remove enemy Informers.

ENTRY.name: T50
ENTRY.STRING: Generals can enhance the value of their troops in combat. Positioning them near their troops provides an increased combat value. Generals can order a {Forced March} temporarily increasing the speed of all of their troops. They can place their forces in {Ambush} hiding their forces from enemy units for a period of time. They can {Entrench} infantry to protect them from much enemy fire, and they can create {Decoy} units to distract and confuse the enemy.

ENTRY.name: T51
ENTRY.STRING: To select a previously selected unit and its group, hold down ALT while left-clicking on the unit.

ENTRY.name: T52
ENTRY.STRING: To group units together, select them and press CTRL+1. You can then reselect them at any time by pressing 1. Pressing 1 a second time will center on the group, and a third time will zoom in. You can create other groups by pressing CTRL+ 0-9 or F1-F8.

ENTRY.name: T53
ENTRY.STRING: To remember a camera view, press CTRL+ F1 with no units or buildings selected. Return to that screen view at any time by pressing F1. Different views can be saved by pressing CTRL+F1-F8, and with 0-9.

ENTRY.name: T54
ENTRY.STRING: Press the default hotkey {L} to select one of your Libraries. A quick double-tap will center on the Library, and a triple-tap will zoom. Repeatedly pressing {L} without double-tapping will cycle you through all of your libraries. Other building types have similar keys, such as {U} for University, {N} for Market, and {C} for City.

ENTRY.name: T55
ENTRY.STRING: To chat with other players, press ENTER and type your message. Your message will go to your allies by default (or everyone in a free for all game). You can also use SHIFT+ENTER to send your message to everyone, CTRL+ENTER to send to your allies, and ALT+ENTER to send to your enemies.

ENTRY.name: T56
ENTRY.STRING: To send a pre-programmed voiceover or "Taunt", press ENTER to chat and type a number from 1-100 in the message box. If you have computer-controlled allies, they will try to follow any instructions you give this way.

ENTRY.name: T57
ENTRY.STRING: To control the formation of your selected troops, right-click and hold where you want them to go, and then while still holding down the right mouse button left-click to choose a formation. You can also drag while holding down the right mouse button to change the facing. Roll the mouse wheel during the process to stretch/compress the formation; use the arrow keys to fine tune the position of the formation.

ENTRY.name: T58
ENTRY.STRING: You can use the Rally Point button to control where new units from your buildings (e.g., City, Barracks, Stable) go. With the building selected, click the Rally Point button (or press R), then click where you want new troops to go. You can hold down the SHIFT key while placing rally points to place multiple rally points for a single building (units will traverse them in the order you lay them).

ENTRY.name: T59
ENTRY.STRING: Set a building's rally point inside a building to have new units garrison inside the building.

ENTRY.name: T60
ENTRY.STRING: Set a building's rally point on a unit to have new units go to wherever the unit is at the time they are created. New units will also try to join the target unit's control groups, if any.

ENTRY.name: T61
ENTRY.STRING: The easiest way to launch planes from an Airbase is to select the base and then {right-click} where you want the planes to go. One plane (if available) will launch each time you right-click. You can also {SHIFT + right-click} to launch all available planes at the same time. With either method, planes will look for appropriate enemy targets to attack near the target location. Other options include {CTRL + right-click} to launch specifically a Bomber and {ALT + right-click} to launch specifically a Fighter; either can be combined with {SHIFT} to launch all of that type of aircraft.

ENTRY.name: T62
ENTRY.STRING: Build a missile at a Silo, then launch the missile by selecting the Silo and {right-clicking} the target.

ENTRY.name: T63
ENTRY.STRING: If your nation has reached the #ICON34 Information Age and you have researched {all} technologies at the Library, you will be able to research powerful {Future Technology}.

ENTRY.name: T64
ENTRY.STRING: To build a Wonder of the World, select a Citizen and click the {Wonders} button. Two additional Wonders become available for each Age your nation advances. Once one nation builds a particular Wonder, such as the Pyramids, no other nations may construct that Wonder. Wonders provide special unique powers to your nation, and in some games, help you achieve a {Wonder Victory}.

ENTRY.name: T65
ENTRY.STRING: To engage in Diplomacy with other nations, click the #ICON43 {Diplomacy} button or press {F9}. In a team game you can give resources to other team members. In a Diplomacy game you can also negotiate peace treaties and alliances with other nations.

ENTRY.name: T66
ENTRY.STRING: When an unfinished building (or a building under repair) is attacked by ground units, any Citizens working on that building are damaged as well and will flee from the building.

ENTRY.name: T67
ENTRY.STRING: Some buildings provide a special bonus when constructed. For instance, building a new Market provides a one-time #ICON3 Wealth bonus. Similarly, when the Mongols build a new Stable they gain free Ranged Cavalry units. However, these bonuses accrue only when the new building increases the nation's all time high total of that building--so if the Market (or Stable, etc) replaces one which was destroyed or voluntarily razed, no bonus applies.

ENTRY.name: T68
ENTRY.STRING: You cannot see #ICON6 {Oil} fields until your nation reaches the #ICON32 Industrial Age. Once you find Oil fields, you can build {Oil Wells} either on land or at sea. Build {Refineries} to increase Oil Well output throughout your nation.

ENTRY.name: T69
ENTRY.STRING: To select all units (or buildings) anywhere on the map that match the type currently selected, press HOME. For example, if you have selected a Citizen, pressing HOME will select all Citizens anywhere in the game.

ENTRY.name: T70
ENTRY.STRING: To cycle through all of your buildings that have new research available, press {TAB}.

ENTRY.name: T71
ENTRY.STRING: To have your selected unit {Follow} another friendly unit, you simply right-click on the target unit.

ENTRY.name: T72
ENTRY.STRING: If you have a group of siege units and non-siege unit selected, you can ALT + right-click on a target to order a {Siege Attack}: your siege units will attack the target, and your other units will guard the siege units.

ENTRY.name: T73
ENTRY.STRING: [Thrones and Patriots] Senates are new buildings in Rise of Nations: Thrones and Patriots. They can be constructed after you reach the Classical Age. Senates perform three major functions: The City where you build your Senate becomes your Capital. At the Senate you can research Governments that provide additional benefits to your nation. When you research a Government, you receive a Patriot unit to assist you in battle.

ENTRY.name: T74
ENTRY.STRING: [Thrones and Patriots] Patriots are special Generals that are automatically created at the Senate when you have researched a Government. Each new Government you research will generate a different Patriot, each with specific powers. Some of the powers include healing nearby units, decreasing the time needed to assimilate an enemy City, decreasing the build time of your buildings nearby, and other effects.

ENTRY.name: T75
ENTRY.STRING: [Thrones and Patriots] If your Patriot is killed in battle, another Patriot will come to your aid from the Senate. If your Capital is captured when your Patriot dies, he will reappear in a nearby city.

ENTRY.name: T76
ENTRY.STRING: [Thrones and Patriots] Can't find your Patriot? There's a new "Find Patriot" button below the "Find Military" button to the left of the minimap. Pressing it once will find your Patriot; each subsequent press will find other Generals under your control.

ENTRY.name: tutorial
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the Tutorial campaign, which will teach you how to play Rise of Nations.

ENTRY.name: solo_menu
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a Solo game. You can choose from a Quick Battle, Conquer the World Campaigns, and Scenarios. You can also load and delete saved games and play back recorded games.

ENTRY.name: mp_menu
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a Multiplayer game.

ENTRY.name: options
ENTRY.STRING: Click for player profiles and options.

ENTRY.name: options_menu
ENTRY.STRING: Click for the Scenario Editor and other extras.

ENTRY.name: updates
ENTRY.STRING: Click to check for an updated version of the game and install if present (requires an internet connection).

ENTRY.name: motd_online
ENTRY.STRING: Click to update and view the online Message of the Day (requires an Internet connection to receive updates).

ENTRY.name: quit
ENTRY.STRING: Click to quit the game.

ENTRY.name: ctw
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a Conquer the World Campaign. A strategic map lets you control your nation's development over the course of many games.

ENTRY.name: solo
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a Quick Battle, the standard solo game. You play against the computer in a randomly generated encounter with settings you choose.

ENTRY.name: scenario
ENTRY.STRING: Click to relive one of the Great Battles of history.

ENTRY.name: skilltest
ENTRY.STRING: Click to test your Rise of Nations skills in a series of special challenges.

ENTRY.name: main_load_scenario
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a Scenario.

ENTRY.name: load_solo
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load a saved game.

ENTRY.name: solo_recorded
ENTRY.STRING: Click to play back a recorded game.

ENTRY.name: back
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the main menu.

ENTRY.name: steamqm
ENTRY.STRING: Click to find a quickmatch opponent.

ENTRY.name: steamlobby
ENTRY.STRING: Click to search available match lobbies.

ENTRY.name: steamleaderboards
ENTRY.STRING: Click to access Leaderboards.

ENTRY.name: gamespy
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start an Internet Game via the GameSpy matchmaking service.

ENTRY.name: lan
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a game on your Local Area Network (LAN) or to start a Direct Internet Game (TCP/IP).

ENTRY.name: mp_recorded
ENTRY.STRING: Click to play back a recorded game.

ENTRY.name: back
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the main menu.

ENTRY.name: options
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set preferences, remap hotkeys, and manage your player profile.

ENTRY.name: editor
ENTRY.STRING: Click to enter the Scenario Editor, which lets you create and edit custom scenarios.

ENTRY.name: script_ide
ENTRY.STRING: Click to run the Script Editor, which lets you create, edit and debug custom and existing scripts.

ENTRY.name: unit_balance
ENTRY.STRING: Click to run the Unit Balance Tool, which performs matchups between different unit types.

ENTRY.name: map_balance
ENTRY.STRING: Click to run the Map Balance Tool, which will generate random maps in mass quantities.

ENTRY.name: credits
ENTRY.STRING: Click to learn all about the cool people who created Rise of Nations.

ENTRY.name: about
ENTRY.STRING: Click to display legal information and view the product ID number.

ENTRY.name: motd
ENTRY.STRING: Click to display the current "message of the day" showing recent changes during development.

ENTRY.name: back
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the main menu.

ENTRY.name: CANCELBUILD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel construction of this building.

ENTRY.name: CANCELSPELL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel this action.

ENTRY.name: CANCELACTION
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel the current action.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel.

ENTRY.name: EJECTALL
ENTRY.STRING: Ejects all units garrisoned inside.

ENTRY.name: EJECT
ENTRY.STRING: Causes the unit to appear outside the building (or unit) it has been ejected from.

ENTRY.name: EJECTV
ENTRY.STRING: Units ejected from a building will attempt to move to the building's rally point, if any.

ENTRY.name: CHATONOFF
ENTRY.STRING:
If chat is on for a nation, your chat messages and signals will be seen by the player(s) controlling it.
If chat is off, no chat messages or signals will go to that nation.

ENTRY.name: NOTASSIMILATED
ENTRY.STRING:
You have captured this town but have not yet {assimilated} it into your nation.
Held cities are assimilated after a period of time.
Until a city is assimilated you cannot construct new buildings in it nor produce units there.

ENTRY.name: NEUTRALIZED
ENTRY.STRING:
This building is in contested or enemy territory.
You cannot create units or conduct research at a building that is not in your territory.
Forts and Towers in contested or enemy territory still function normally.

ENTRY.name: NEUTRALIZED2
ENTRY.STRING:
This building (which belongs to another nation) is in contested territory or unfriendly territory.
The owner of a neutralized building cannot use it to build units or conduct research.
Forts and towers in contested or enemy territory still function normally.

ENTRY.name: INACTIVE
ENTRY.STRING:
This building can only function if it is within the economic radius of a City.
If you build a City nearby, this building will reactivate.

ENTRY.name: PREV
ENTRY.STRING: Click to see the previous screen of buildings.

ENTRY.name: BACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to see the previous screen of building types.

ENTRY.name: NEXT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to see the next screen of building types.

ENTRY.name: CLEARGATHER
ENTRY.STRING: Click to remove the Rally Point for this building. Units created here will appear near the building.

ENTRY.name: RENAME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to rename this City. This is a great way to taunt your human opponents in a multiplayer game.

ENTRY.name: GROUPV
ENTRY.STRING:
Grouping can be a useful way to help you keep track of units (or buildings) at different places on the map.
Set a group with nothing selected to save a {camera view}--the camera will return to the saved position and zoom level. Use this to keep track of key points on the map.
Groups can be activated with the number keys (0-9).
You can also set F1-F8 to create groups.
Hold down CTRL + SHIFT to erase a group.

ENTRY.name: GROUPNV
ENTRY.STRING:
Grouping can be used to keep track of units on the map.
Or use an "empty" group to store a camera position.
You can use 0-9 and F1-F8 to create groups.

ENTRY.name: GARRISON
ENTRY.STRING:
Units can always garrison inside the type of building that created them.
You can also garrison foot and horse units inside Cities, Towers, Keeps, Stockades, Bunkers, Forts, Castles, Fortresses, and Redoubts.
Units in a garrison receive protection from damage and (in the case of Towers, Forts, etc.) increase the attack strength of the building.
Units in a garrison are slowly healed or repaired.

ENTRY.name: HALT
ENTRY.STRING: Causes the selected unit to stop whatever it is doing.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTAIR
ENTRY.STRING: If you set a rally point on the map, all ready aircraft will immediately launch to the rally point. New aircraft created at this airbase will go to the rally point. Aircraft in flight from this base will divert immediately to the rally point.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTFARM
ENTRY.STRING:
Sets a rally point on each Farm in this City's economic radius.
New Citizens produced here will go to work on one of the Farms if it is empty.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTWOODCUTTER
ENTRY.STRING:
Sets a rally point on each Woodcutter's Camp in this City's economic radius.
New Citizens produced here will go to work gathering Timber if a space is available.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTMINE
ENTRY.STRING:
Sets a rally point on each Mine in this City's economic radius.
New Citizens produced here will go to work mining Metal if a space is available.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTALL
ENTRY.STRING:
Sets a rally point on each Farm, Woodcutter's Camp, or Mine in this City's economic radius.
New Citizens produced here will go to work gathering resources if a space is available.
Citizens look for work at buildings in the order that the buildings were originally constructed.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINT
ENTRY.STRING:
If you set a rally point on the map, new units created at this building will automatically go to that spot.
Ejected garrisoned units will also go to the rally point.

ENTRY.name: GATHERPOINTV
ENTRY.STRING:
If you set the rally point "inside" the building, new units (up to the building's maximum, usually 10) will remain garrisoned inside until you eject them.
If you set the rally point on a unit, new units will go to the location of that unit, and will join any control groups of which the target unit is a member.

ENTRY.name: AIRFUELING
ENTRY.STRING: This air unit is refueling.

ENTRY.name: AIRREADY
ENTRY.STRING: This unit is ready to launch. To launch an attack, right-click a target.

ENTRY.name: AIRREADY2
ENTRY.STRING: This unit is ready to launch.

ENTRY.name: DISEMBARK
ENTRY.STRING: The disembark command tells units to get off transport ships at a particular land location.

ENTRY.name: ATTACKGROUND
ENTRY.STRING: You can choose to continuously bombard a map location (as opposed to a specific unit or building).

ENTRY.name: INFINITEQUEUE
ENTRY.STRING: When Infinite Queue is on, a building will continue to produce units in the order currently specified in its queue for as long as there are resources available to produce them. When a unit is created, nothing is removed from the queue: the queue simply cycles to the next item.

ENTRY.name: BORDERMV
ENTRY.STRING:
You can normally only construct new buildings inside your National Borders.
To expand your borders, build new Cities and Forts near the edges, add Temples to existing Cities, and research #ICON25 Civic technologies at the Library.
The more territory control, the higher the taxes (in Wealth) you will collect.

ENTRY.name: BORDERV
ENTRY.STRING:
You can add captured and assimilated enemy cities to your nation.
If you are colonizing a brand new continent/island, you can build {one} city near the coast to establish your borders.

ENTRY.name: DRAGMV
ENTRY.STRING: Press SHIFT while dragging a box around units to add them to your current selection. Press CTRL while dragging a box around units to select Civilian as well as Military units. Press ALT while dragging to select only non-siege military units. Press CTRL + ALT to select only siege units. Press ALT + SHIFT to select only aircraft. Press CTRL + SHIFT to select only Citizens and Scholars. Press CTRL + ALT + SHIFT to select only Transports.

ENTRY.name: DRAGEDITOR1
ENTRY.STRING: Click to place unit; drag to set facing.

ENTRY.name: DRAGEDITOR2
ENTRY.STRING: Right-click to set initial orders. SHIFT + right-click to add additional waypoints. CTRL + right-click to move unit(s) to new location. While holding down right mouse button, left-click to set formation and roll mouse wheel to set width of formation.

ENTRY.name: ALARMPEASANT
ENTRY.STRING: Tells all selected Citizens to hide in the nearest City, Tower, or Fort.

ENTRY.name: ALARM
ENTRY.STRING: Tells Citizens in this City to take refuge in the City (or a nearby Tower, Fort, etc.).

ENTRY.name: ALLCLEAR
ENTRY.STRING: Tells any Citizens hiding in this City to return to work.

ENTRY.name: STRATMAP
ENTRY.STRING: Displays a large strategic map of the whole world.

ENTRY.name: SELECTALLCURRENT
ENTRY.STRING: Selects all of your units (or buildings) on any type of map you currently have selected. If you have a Citizen selected this will select all of your Citizens.

ENTRY.name: IFACEMINIMAP_MOUSEOVER
ENTRY.STRING: Shows a diagram of the entire world map. You can use the minimap instead of the main map to select destinations for your units. The type of information provided by the minimap can be controlled by changing the Data display$HOTKEYSTRING0.

ENTRY.name: IFACESELECTEDSTAR
ENTRY.STRING: Indicates this City is its nation's capital. In standard games a nation is eliminated if its capital is captured and held. Capitals have a greater effect on national borders than other cities. Capturing a capital can bring the conqueror large amounts of plunder.

ENTRY.name: IFACESELECTEDFLAG
ENTRY.STRING: Shows which nation owns this unit or building.

ENTRY.name: PING
ENTRY.STRING:
Causes a flashing signal to appear on other players' maps.
Any players you've set to receive "chat" will receive the signal (e.g., in a team game this is by default your starting allies).
You can also use a mouse drag to draw an arrow.

ENTRY.name: CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: {Chat} allows you to type a message to the other players. By default your chat messages will be sent to your allies in a team game or to everyone in a free-for-all or head-to-head match. On the Diplomacy screen you can change your default chat settings to control which players you are chatting to. You can also chat directly to a specific player with whom you are negotiating. You can also use SHIFT+ENTER to send a message to everyone, CTRL+ENTER to send to your allies, and CTRL+SHIFT+ENTER to send to your enemies.

ENTRY.name: OK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to accept the current settings.

ENTRY.name: RESET
ENTRY.STRING: Click to reset all options on this tab to the default settings.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel settings changes.

ENTRY.name: ASSIGN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to assign the shortcut listed in the box above to the function selected in the list at left.

ENTRY.name: REMOVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete this keyboard shortcut.

ENTRY.name: IMPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Imports a list of keyboard shortcuts.

ENTRY.name: EXPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Exports your keyboard shortcuts (for instance so that you can transfer them to another machine).

ENTRY.name: PLUS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to move this graphic component higher in the list.

ENTRY.name: MINUS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to move this graphic component lower in the list.

ENTRY.name: ALL_AUTO
ENTRY.STRING: Click to have all graphics components determine performance levels automatically.

ENTRY.name: ALL_HIGH
ENTRY.STRING: Click to have all graphics components use high performance features.

ENTRY.name: ALL_LOW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to have all graphics components use low performance features.

ENTRY.name: ALL_ON
ENTRY.STRING: Click to turn on all graphics components.

ENTRY.name: ALL_OFF
ENTRY.STRING: Click to turn off all graphics components.

ENTRY.name: DELETE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete the selected player profile.

ENTRY.name: NEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create a new player profile.

ENTRY.name: RENAME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to rename the selected player profile.

ENTRY.name: ADD_NAME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to add a new player nickname. You can play under more than one nickname while still keeping the same player profile.

ENTRY.name: DELETE_NAME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete the selected player nickname.

ENTRY.name: ACCEPT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to accept the resolution change.

ENTRY.name: RENDER_OFF
ENTRY.STRING: Click to turn off all graphics components.

ENTRY.name: RENDER_ON
ENTRY.STRING: Click to turn on all graphics components.

ENTRY.name: SHOW_UNITS_BEHIND
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to highlight units hidden behind buildings and trees. Hidden units will show through in their team color.

ENTRY.name: FRIEND_FOE_COLOR
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to turn on Friend/Foe colors, a simplified color scheme in which your forces appear in Blue, your allies in Light Blue, your enemies in Red, and neutral nations in White.

ENTRY.name: ADVANCED_MENU
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to turn on Advanced Options, which enable additional features such as Stance to customize the behavior of your units.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_TRANSPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to allow your Scout unit to auto transport across water. Uncheck the box if you want to restrict your scouts to exploring a single continent.

ENTRY.name: RESEARCH_PROGRESS_BARS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to see progress bars tracking your technology research in the upper right portion of the screen.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_PEASANTS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to control the kinds of work your idle Citizens will look for, or if they will simply stay idle.

ENTRY.name: CRAFT_AS_GROUP
ENTRY.STRING: If this box is checked, groups of craft users (such as Spies) will use their special abilities (such as Bribe) as a group when given a target. Though all will attempt the mission, only one unit will use its craft upon success. If this box is not checked, only one craft user from a group will attempt to use a special ability each time the group is given a target.

ENTRY.name: SHOW_GAME_CLOCK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to cause the game clock to be shown on the interface.

ENTRY.name: NAMES_ON_ZOOM_OUT
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to show city names on the main map. If this box is not checked, city names will not be displayed.

ENTRY.name: START_PAUSED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like solo games to begin paused. (Multiplayer games never begin paused).

ENTRY.name: AUTO_DEPLOY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like your siege and artillery units to deploy themselves automatically when they are left idle for more than five seconds (machine guns will deploy after one second). If this box is not checked, packed siege and artillery units will always remain packed until you tell them to deploy.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_CAST
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like idle craft users (such as Spies and Scouts) to use certain of their craft abilities automatically when idle.

ENTRY.name: DRAG_MILITARY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like drag select to prefer military units: if any military units are within a drag box, all non-military units (such as Citizens, Scholars, Caravans) will be filtered out of the selection. If this box is not checked, all units within the drag box are selected.

ENTRY.name: ONE_CLICK_GARRISON
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to turn on One Click Garrison. Units will attempt to garrison inside of any building you click on while giving them a move order.

ENTRY.name: LEFT_CLICK_INTERFACE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you prefer a "Left-Click" interface (in which you left-click on a unit to select it and then left-click where you want it to move). If this box is not checked, the default "Right-Click" interface is used (in which you left-click on a unit to select it and then right-click where you want it to move).

ENTRY.name: RIGHT_CLICK_EXECUTE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you prefer that right-click always specify the target of an order. For instance you can select a unit, click "Attack", and then right-click on the unit or location you wish to attack (normally you would need to left-click in this situation).

ENTRY.name: AUTO
ENTRY.STRING: Graphics components set to Auto will adjust to the performance level of your system automatically.

ENTRY.name: SECTION
ENTRY.STRING: The boxes in this section determine which graphics components are used, and whether they use high performance or low performance features. High performance features require faster computer systems and video cards.

ENTRY.name: LOW
ENTRY.STRING: Graphics components set to Low will use low performance features to speed up graphics rendering.

ENTRY.name: HIGH
ENTRY.STRING: Graphics components set to High will use high performance features for greatest graphic detail.

ENTRY.name: ALLOW_TAUNTS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to allow players to use audio "taunts", prerecorded voiceovers that can be called up by typing numbers in the chat box.

ENTRY.name: ALLOW_IN_GAME_MUSIC
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to hear music during the game. Players with slower systems can achieve faster game performance by unchecking this box.

ENTRY.name: BUILDING_STANCES
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the default stance of units produced by all of your buildings. Note that you can also change an individual building to use a different default stance.

ENTRY.name: ALWAYS_COMBINE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you want unit icons for identical units to be combined into a single icon (with a number indicating how many units) when there are more units than can be displayed at once at full size. If this box is not checked, unit icons will be shrunk to fit in the available space.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_ALL_CLEAR
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like your Citizens whom you've hidden with the Alarm button to return to work automatically if the coast appears to be clear. If this box is not checked, they will remain inside their buildings until you explicitly give the All Clear signal.

ENTRY.name: RECORD_GAMES
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you would like your games to be recorded for later playback. To play back your games later, select "Recorded Games" from either the Solo or Multiplayer menu.

ENTRY.name: DISABLE_CROSS_NETWORK_PLAY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to disable Cross-Network Play.

ENTRY.name: SHOW_FRAMERATE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to display the graphics frame rate on the game screen.

ENTRY.name: FULLSCREEN
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to display the game in fullscreen.

ENTRY.name: VSYNC
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to enable vsync.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_ALLCLEAR
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to have your citizens automatically ungarrison.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ENEMY_NEW_AGE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when an enemy reaches a new age.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ALLY_NEW_AGE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when an ally reaches a new age.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_I_NEW_AGE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when you reach a new age.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_FUTURE_TECH_RESEARCHED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a future tech research begins and ends.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_NUKES_RESEARCHED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when nuclear missile research begins and ends.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_NUKE_LAUNCHED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a nuclear missile is launched.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_CITY_UNDER_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when one of your cities is under attack.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ALLY_CIY_UNDER_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when an ally's city is under attack.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_VICTORY_TIMER
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a victory timer starts or stops.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_WE_LOSE_CITY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when you lose a city.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_WE_CAPTURE_CITY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when you capture a city.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_PLAYER_STARTS_WONDER
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a player starts a wonder.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_PLAYER_FINISHES_WONDER
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a player completes a wonder.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ARMAGEDDON
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when the armageddon count changes.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_PLAYER_LEAVES
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a player leaves the game.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_REINFORCEMENTS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when reinforcements arrive.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_CAPITAL_UNDER_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when your capital is under attack.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ALLY_CAPITAL_UNDER_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when an allied capital is under attack.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_ALLY_CITY_UNDER_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when an allied city is under attack.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_CANNON_TIME_BEGIN
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when cannon time has begun.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_CANNON_TIME_END
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when cannon time has ended.

ENTRY.name: NOTICE_CAPITAL_MOVED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to be notified when a player's Capital has been moved.

ENTRY.name: ALLOW_MAIN_MENU_MUSIC
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to hear music in the main menu.

ENTRY.name: DISABLE_AUDIO_DUCKING
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to disable windows audio ducking.

ENTRY.name: SECTION_ONOFF
ENTRY.STRING: The boxes in this section determine which graphics components are used, and whether they use high performance or low performance features. High performance features require faster computer systems and video cards.

ENTRY.name: LOW_ONOFF
ENTRY.STRING: Graphics components set to Off will be disabled. This will speed up graphics rendering.

ENTRY.name: HIGH_ONOFF
ENTRY.STRING: Graphics components set to On will be enabled. This will increase graphic detail.

ENTRY.name: ANTI-ALIASING
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to enable Anti-Aliasing.

ENTRY.name: GAME
ENTRY.STRING: The Game tab controls the way your units and buildings behave, and allows you to configure some interface preferences.

ENTRY.name: HOTKEYS
ENTRY.STRING: The Hotkeys tab allows you to specify keyboard shortcuts for most game commands.

ENTRY.name: MOUSE
ENTRY.STRING: The Mouse tab lets you control the behavior of the mouse.

ENTRY.name: GRAPHICS
ENTRY.STRING: The Graphics tab lets you choose a display resolution and optimize your video performance.

ENTRY.name: SOUND
ENTRY.STRING: The Sound tab lets you control the volume level of the sound effects and music.

ENTRY.name: PLAYER
ENTRY.STRING: The Player tab lets you manage player profiles and nicknames.

ENTRY.name: PLAYER_DISABLED
ENTRY.STRING: The Player tab lets you manage player profiles and nicknames. This tab is UNAVAILABLE during a game or scenario.

ENTRY.name: GAME_SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the game speed.

ENTRY.name: DIFF_LEVEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to adjust the difficulty level.

ENTRY.name: AUTO_PEASANTS_TIMING
ENTRY.STRING: Click to control how long your Citizens, Caravans, and Scholars stay idle before looking for something to do on their own.

ENTRY.name: MESSAGE_DURATION
ENTRY.STRING: Click to control how long text messages and notifications remain on the screen.

ENTRY.name: SCROLL_SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: Click to control how fast the map scrolls.

ENTRY.name: FX_VOLUME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the volume level for sound effects.

ENTRY.name: MUSIC_VOLUME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the volume level for music.

ENTRY.name: MESSAGE_DETAIL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to choose the level of rollover text shown on-screen. The higher levels provide additional tips and details.

ENTRY.name: TAUNT_VOLUME
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the volume level for taunts.

ENTRY.name: PROFILE
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists the available player profiles. You can click on one and then use the buttons displayed to the right.

ENTRY.name: NICKNAME
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists the available player nicknames for this profile. You can use nicknames to play under different "handles" while still using your preferences.

ENTRY.name: HOTKEYS
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists the available hotkey assignments. You can click on a hotkey assignment to change or examine it.

ENTRY.name: KEY
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists the available hotkey assignments. You can click on a hotkey assignment to change or examine it.

ENTRY.name: ASSIGNED
ENTRY.STRING: This shows commands currently assigned to the hotkey sequence.

ENTRY.name: NAME
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists the available player nicknames for this profile. You can use nicknames to play under different "handles" while still using your preferences.

ENTRY.name: GROUPS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to filter which keyboard shortcuts are displayed in the list.

ENTRY.name: RESOLUTION
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the display resolution.

ENTRY.name: LANGUAGE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the display language.

ENTRY.name: FULLSCREEN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to set the display fullscreen status.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click in this box and then press the keyboard shortcut you wish to use for the selected function. You can also press the middle mouse button, roll the mouse wheel, or press any "extended" mouse button. The main left and right mouse buttons cannot be remapped here.

ENTRY.name: MP_PORT
ENTRY.STRING: Click in this box and enter the port number to be used when hosting a game or connecting to remote hosts if you are a client.

ENTRY.name: MP_PORT2
ENTRY.STRING: Click in this box and enter the client port number. It is recommended to leave this at the default setting.

ENTRY.name: MP_IP
ENTRY.STRING: Click in this box and type an alternative IP address for hosting using GameSpy matchmaking.

ENTRY.name: CREDITS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to learn about the cool people who made Rise of Nations.

ENTRY.name: CLOSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to close this box.

ENTRY.name: CLOSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to close this box.

ENTRY.name: FAST_FORWARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to fast forward the replay.

ENTRY.name: PAUSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to pause the replay.

ENTRY.name: SEEK_BACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to seek to the previous index point.

ENTRY.name: SEEK_FORWARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to seek to the next index point.

ENTRY.name: PLAY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the replay at normal speed.

ENTRY.name: LOCK_VIEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to toggle "camera follow", in which the camera follows the view the current player had during the game.

ENTRY.name: FOW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to toggle the "fog of war" shroud.

ENTRY.name: PLAYER_VIEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to change player viewpoint.

ENTRY.name: MINIMIZE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to minimize the playback controls.

ENTRY.name: MAXIMIZE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to maximize the playback controls.

ENTRY.name: FAST_FORWARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to fast forward. (Disabled in Observer mode.)

ENTRY.name: PAUSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to pause. (Disabled in Observer mode.)

ENTRY.name: SEEK_BACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to seek to the previous index point. (Disabled in Observer mode.)

ENTRY.name: SEEK_FORWARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to seek to the next index point. (Disabled in Observer mode.)

ENTRY.name: PLAY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the replay at normal speed. (Disabled in Observer mode.)

ENTRY.name: LOCK_VIEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to toggle "camera follow", in which the camera follows the view the current player.

ENTRY.name: FOW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to toggle the "fog of war" shroud.

ENTRY.name: PLAYER_VIEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to change player viewpoint.

ENTRY.name: MINIMIZE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to minimize the observer controls.

ENTRY.name: MAXIMIZE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to maximize the observer controls.

ENTRY.name: CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: You can type a message here. If you click "Send" or press ENTER, it will be sent to all of the nations whose names you have checked in the Chat column. You can use the "Send All" and "Send Ally" buttons below to send the message to different groups.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go to all players whose names you have checked in the Chat column.

ENTRY.name: SEND_ALL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go to all players in the game, regardless of your Chat settings.

ENTRY.name: SEND_ALLY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go to your allies, regardless of your Chat settings.

ENTRY.name: CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to determine your chat status with a particular player. Options include "Send Chat" (#ICON50), "Receive Only" (#ICON51), and "Neither Send nor Receive" (#ICON52).

ENTRY.name: CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: You can type a message here (and press ENTER) to send it to the nation you are negotiating with. Messages entered here will {only} go to the player controlling the nation you are talking to.

ENTRY.name: PROPOSE_PEACE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to propose {peace} with this nation.Nations at peace cannot attack one another without declaring war first.Military units which violate a peace treaty by entering a nation's territory suffer severe {attrition} damage.

ENTRY.name: PROPOSE_ALLY
ENTRY.STRING: Select this option to propose an {alliance} with this nation.When two nations are allied, their territories are combined, and they jointly win the game if they fulfill the victory conditions.With a sufficient number of #ICON27 Science technologies, allies can also see each other's Lines of Sight.Except in a {Team} game, you may not have more than one ally at a time.

ENTRY.name: MUTUAL_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to propose a mutual attack on this target. If the deal is accepted, both you and your negotiating partner automatically declare war on the target.

ENTRY.name: PROPOSE_WAR
ENTRY.STRING: Select this option to declare {war} on this nation.Nations at war with each other can engage in combat, destroy buildings, and capture cities.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go only to the nation with whom you are negotiating, regardless of your Chat settings.

ENTRY.name: SEND_ALL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go to all players in the game, regardless of your Chat settings.

ENTRY.name: SEND_ALLY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message you have typed above. The message will go to your allies, regardless of your Chat settings.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Enter the name of the saved game here.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous screen.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load the selected saved game.

ENTRY.name: SAVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to save to the selected file name.

ENTRY.name: DELETE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete the selected entry.

ENTRY.name: FILES
ENTRY.STRING: Click on a saved game file to select it.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Enter the name of the recorded game here.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous screen.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load the selected recorded game.

ENTRY.name: SAVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to save to the selected file name.

ENTRY.name: DELETE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete the selected entry.

ENTRY.name: SELECT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to choose this file.

ENTRY.name: FILES
ENTRY.STRING: Click on a recorded game file to select it.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Enter the name of the scenario game here.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous screen.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load the selected scenario game.

ENTRY.name: SAVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to save to the selected file name.

ENTRY.name: DELETE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to delete the selected entry.

ENTRY.name: SELECT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to choose this file.

ENTRY.name: FILES
ENTRY.STRING: Click on a scenario game file to select it.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Enter the script file name here.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the script editor.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load the script in the editor.

ENTRY.name: SAVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to save script to the selected file name.

ENTRY.name: FILES
ENTRY.STRING: Click on a script file to select it.

ENTRY.name: FILES
ENTRY.STRING: Click on an image to select it.

ENTRY.name: EDIT
ENTRY.STRING: Enter the name of the image to import here.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to import the selected image as the map.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous screen.

ENTRY.name: SELECT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the file or folder.

ENTRY.name: SESSIONS
ENTRY.STRING: This box lists games that are currently active on your Local Area Network. To join a game, click on the name, then click the Join button below. You can also create your own game or join a game through a Direct Internet connection.

ENTRY.name: CREATE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create and host a new multiplayer game. Other players will be able to join via your Local Area Network or through a Direct Internet connection.

ENTRY.name: JOIN_IP
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join a game through a Direct Internet connection. You will need to know the host computer's IP address, which you can obtain by asking the player who is creating and hosting the game.

ENTRY.name: JOIN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join a game on your Local Area Network. If several games are listed in the window above, click on the one you wish to join before clicking here.

ENTRY.name: OBSERVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to observe a game on your Local Area Network. You will be able to see the game from any player's point of view, but you will not control a nation yourself.

ENTRY.name: OBSERVE_IP
ENTRY.STRING: Click to observe a game through a Direct Internet connection. You will need to know the host computer's IP address. You will be able to see the game from any player's point of view, but you will not control a nation yourself.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: CREATE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create a new GameSpy profile.

ENTRY.name: LOGIN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to log in once you have entered your nickname and password in the boxes above.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: CREATE_NEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create a new GameSpy profile. You'll need to enter your e-mail address, and choose a nickname and password in the boxes above.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL_NEW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: SAVE_PASSWORD
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to have Rise of Nations remember your password. It will be filled in automatically the next time you log in.

ENTRY.name: PASSWORD
ENTRY.STRING: Enter your GameSpy password here.

ENTRY.name: NICK
ENTRY.STRING: Enter your GameSpy nickname here.

ENTRY.name: EMAIL
ENTRY.STRING: Enter your e-mail address.

ENTRY.name: JOIN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join the game you have selected in the window above.

ENTRY.name: JOINGROUP
ENTRY.STRING: Click to join the lobby you have selected in the window above.

ENTRY.STRING: Description of game rooms open.

ENTRY.name: PING
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the internet latency or "ping" time to each game room, in milliseconds. Lower ping times will result in better performance and less "lag".

ENTRY.name: NO_UNIQUE_UNITS
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the No Unique Units settings for each game room.

ENTRY.name: SHARE_CONTROL

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: CREATE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create a new game room. You become the host of the new game room and have control of the game's settings.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message to the other players in this room.

ENTRY.name: REFRESH
ENTRY.STRING: Click to refresh the list of game rooms.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to add someone to your Buddy List.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to remove the currently selected buddy from your list.

ENTRY.name: FILTER
ENTRY.STRING: Click to choose which settings you want to be displayed in the games listing.

ENTRY.name: IGNORE_LIST
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the list of players that you are currently ignoring.

ENTRY.name: UNRATED
ENTRY.STRING: The Unrated Beginner tab displays unrated games for players who describe themselves as beginners.

ENTRY.name: RATED
ENTRY.STRING: The Rated tab displays games that will count towards each player's rating.

ENTRY.name: UNRATED EXPERT
ENTRY.STRING: The Unrated Expert tab displays unrated games for players who describe themselves as expert.

ENTRY.name: UNRATED INTERMEDIATE
ENTRY.STRING: The Unrated Intermediate tab displays unrated games for players who describe themselves as intermediate.

ENTRY.name: TAB_ROOM_CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: The Lobby Chat tab displays the conversation in progress in the lobby.

ENTRY.name: TAB
ENTRY.STRING: Room tabs show the different rooms available for this category. The bar under the number shows how full the room is.

ENTRY.name: ROOM_CAT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to choose the category of game you'd like to play.

ENTRY.name: REGION
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select region.

ENTRY.name: TOURNAMENT_PLAY
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box if you wish to use the Tournament Play setting. In Tournament Play, you cannot see other players' scores during the game, you cannot see the nation choices of other players on the setup screen, and players who resign or are defeated are not shown the map until the end of the game.

ENTRY.name: LOCK_GAME_SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to lock the game speed. If the game speed is locked, players cannot change it during the game.

ENTRY.name: TURBO_RESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to play with Turbo Resources, in which case nations accrue resources much more quickly than normal.

ENTRY.name: NO_NATION_POWERS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to disable Nation Powers.

ENTRY.name: NO_UNIQUE_UNITS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to disable Unique Units.

ENTRY.name: FREE_PAUSE
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to use Free Pause, which allows players to pause the game as often as they wish and to give orders while the game is paused.

ENTRY.name: REQUIRE_RANDOM_NATIONS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to require that all players pick a random nation.

ENTRY.name: SHARE_CONTROL
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to allow players on the same team to control each others' units and buildings.

ENTRY.name: REQUIRE_RANDOM_TEAMS
ENTRY.STRING: Check this box to prevent players from picking their teammates: teams will be selected randomly.

ENTRY.name: GAME_RULES
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the general game rules. If Standard is selected, the standard rules only are used; otherwise additional rules can be chosen once the game is launched.

ENTRY.name: MAP_SIZE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the size of map that will be used.

ENTRY.name: TEAM_STYLE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the rules for Teams and Allies.

ENTRY.name: MAP_STYLE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the style of map that will be used.

ENTRY.name: CANNON_TIMES
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the Cannon Time rules for the game. Cannon Time allows each player to switch to Very Slow game speed for brief periods.

ENTRY.name: MAX_PLAYERS
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the maximum players that will be allowed to join this game.

ENTRY.name: GAME_SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select the Game Speed.

ENTRY.name: DESCRIPTION
ENTRY.STRING: Enter a description for your game that will identify it and attract other players to join.

ENTRY.name: PASSWORD
ENTRY.STRING: If you only wish to admit players who know a specific password, enter the password here. Leave this field blank to allow any players who wish to join.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel and return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: CREATE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to create a new game room for this game.

ENTRY.name: RESET
ENTRY.STRING: Click to reset all settings to the defaults.

ENTRY.name: LAUNCH
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to launch the game.

ENTRY.name: EXIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to cancel this game and return to the main lobby.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: Click to send a chat message to the other players in this room.

ENTRY.name: REMOVE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to remove the currently selected buddy from your list.

ENTRY.name: ADD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to add someone to your Buddy List.

ENTRY.name: BOOT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to boot the currently selected player from the staging room. (Host only)

ENTRY.name: EXIT_CLIENT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to leave this game and return to the main lobby.

ENTRY.name: QUIT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to leave this game and return to the main lobby.

ENTRY.name: TAB_ROOM_CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: The "Lobby Chat" tab displays the coversation in progress in the lobby.

ENTRY.name: TAB_STAGING_CHAT
ENTRY.STRING: The "Game Chat" tab displays the conversation in progress in this game room.

ENTRY.name: IGNORE_LIST
ENTRY.STRING: Shows the list of players that you are currently ignoring.

ENTRY.name: SEND
ENTRY.STRING: To chat to other players in this room, type a message here and click the Send button.

ENTRY.name: PLAYERS
ENTRY.STRING: Lists the players in this room.

ENTRY.name: BUDDYLIST
ENTRY.STRING: Lists your current buddies.

ENTRY.name: REQUIRE_RANDOM_TEAMS
ENTRY.STRING: {REQUIRE RANDOM TEAMS}: Requiring Random Teams prevents players from choosing their teammates--they must pick {"?"}.

ENTRY.name: FREE_PAUSE
ENTRY.STRING: {FREE PAUSE}: Check this box to allow players to scroll their screens and give orders when game is paused. This option is always active during solo games, but must be selected here to be used in multiplayer games.

ENTRY.name: TURBO_RESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: {TURBO RESOURCES}: Check this box to increase the resource gather rate. This option is for players who want to de-emphasize resource collection and spend more time with other parts of the game.

ENTRY.name: TOURNAMENT_PLAY
ENTRY.STRING: {TOURNAMENT PLAY}: Check this box to hide players' nation picks before the game and hide players' scores during the game. Players who resign early are not shown the entire map until the game is over.

ENTRY.name: REQUIRE_RANDOM_NATIONS
ENTRY.STRING: {REQUIRE RANDOM NATIONS}: Requiring Random Nations prevents players from choosing a specific nation to play--they must pick {Random}.

ENTRY.name: TEAM_STYLE
ENTRY.STRING: {TEAMS AND ALLIES}: Click here to select whether and how nations will be organized into teams--and whether alliances can shift during the game.

ENTRY.name: MAP_STYLE
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP STYLE}: Click here to select a style of map to play on. Each map style makes for a different kind of game and a differing strategic situation.

ENTRY.name: MAP_SIZE
ENTRY.STRING: {MAP SIZE}: Click here to select the size of the map. Smaller maps make for quicker games; larger maps delay the initial contact between nations.

ENTRY.name: MAX_PLAYERS
ENTRY.STRING: {MAX PLAYERS}: Click here to set the maximum number of players allowed to join this game.

ENTRY.name: CANNON_TIMES
ENTRY.STRING: {CANNON TIME}: Click to allow each player to use "super slow motion" for brief periods during a multiplayer game.

ENTRY.name: GAME_RULES
ENTRY.STRING: {RULES}: Click here to select whether the game will be played with standard or customized rules.

ENTRY.name: GAME_SPEED
ENTRY.STRING: {GAME SPEED}: Click here to set the game speed. More experienced players may want time to pass more quickly in the game.

ENTRY.name: BOUDICEA
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 1, which covers basic concepts for the beginner player such as movement and combat. We recommend that players who haven't played a real time strategy game before start here.

ENTRY.name: ALFRED THE GREAT
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 2, which covers additional concepts for beginners such as creating units and constructing buildings.

ENTRY.name: THE 100 YEARS WAR
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 3, an intermediate tutorial covering important differences between Rise of Nations and other real time strategy games. We recommend that players who are experienced at other real time strategy games start here.

ENTRY.name: BRITTANIA
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 4, an intermediate tutorial covering further important differences between Rise of Nations and other real time strategy games.

ENTRY.name: BATTLE OF BRITAIN
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 5, in which you practice your one-on-one play with the computer providing suggestions and hints.

ENTRY.name: HENRY VIII
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select Tutorial 6, an advanced tutorial in which diplomacy and modern weapons are covered.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the main menu.

ENTRY.name: START
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the tutorial you have selected.

ENTRY.name: SELECTION_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select this skill test.

ENTRY.name: DOWN_ARROW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to scroll down.

ENTRY.name: NUMBER_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select this skill test.

ENTRY.name: UP_ARROW
ENTRY.STRING: Click to scroll up.

ENTRY.name: BACK
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the Tools and Extras menu.

ENTRY.name: START
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start the selected skill test.

ENTRY.name: RANKINGS
ENTRY.STRING: This is a list of the best players and their high scores in the current scenario.

ENTRY.name: CTWRULES
ENTRY.STRING: In Conquer the World, you begin with one Capital territory and one army. Use your army to conquer more territories. To win, conquer the entire world! You may attack an unclaimed or enemy territory once per turn. Captured territories give you bonuses, including Tribute, rare resources, and bonus cards. Certain territories contain supply centers. In addition to your starting army, you get one extra army for every supply center you control. An army can attack or support an attack during a turn. A supporting army automatically sends reinforcements during a battle. Armies can also offer defensive support. If you use enough supporting armies, you can win a non-Capital territory without having to fight. You must have two more armies than your opponent to "overrun" the territory. An overrun attack does not count as your attack in a turn. An army can attack or support an attack during a turn. A supporting army automatically sends reinforcements during the scenario. Vassals are nations that have joined your empire but retain control of their strategic resources. They no longer field armies, and your armies are allowed to move through their territory. Their territories count as yours for victory purposes.

ENTRY.name: CLOSE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to close this window.

ENTRY.name: MORE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to view.

ENTRY.name: MAP
ENTRY.STRING: Click here to select a map.

ENTRY.name: DIFFICULTY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select a difficulty level. This determines the computer players' efficiency and level of aggressiveness.

ENTRY.name: LOAD
ENTRY.STRING: Click to load a saved Conquer the World game.

ENTRY.name: CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the previous menu.

ENTRY.name: START
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a new Conquer the World game.

ENTRY.name: CONTINUE
ENTRY.STRING: Click to continue the previous Conquer the World game.

ENTRY.name: NATION_START
ENTRY.STRING: Click to start a new Conquer the World game.

ENTRY.name: NATION_CANCEL
ENTRY.STRING: Click to return to the campaign selection screen.

ENTRY.name: RANDOM_BONUSES
ENTRY.STRING: Check to randomize bonus cards, tributes, and rare resources on the map.

ENTRY.name: RANDOM_NATIONS
ENTRY.STRING: Check to randomize the starting locations of the nations.

ENTRY.name: HELP_ARMY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to select this army. Then click an adjacent enemy territory to attack.

ENTRY.name: HELP_START
ENTRY.STRING: Now that you've chosen a territory, click the "Start" button to begin the attack.

ENTRY.name: HELP_SUPPLY
ENTRY.STRING: You may acquire additional armies by capturing territories containing supply centers #ICON15.

ENTRY.name: HELP_CAPITAL
ENTRY.STRING: This flag shows a capital territory. Capitals can be very difficult to capture. If you capture a capital, that nation is defeated, and you automatically take over that nation's other territories.

ENTRY.name: HELP_DIPLO
ENTRY.STRING: Click the "Diplomacy" button to enter negotiations with other nations. You can buy territories, make peace or alliances, and declare war on other nations.

ENTRY.name: HELP_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click the "buy bonus card" button to purchase a bonus card. You will be shown three cards from which to choose.

ENTRY.name: HELP_INFRA
ENTRY.STRING: Each territory controlled by a nation has a number that shows that territory's strength. The higher the number, the stronger the territory and the more difficult it will be to capture. In general, the longer a territory has been held, the higher the number.

ENTRY.name: HELP_BUY_INFRA
ENTRY.STRING: Click the "buy territory strength" button to increase the strength of one of your territories.

ENTRY.name: HELP_TACTIC_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: You have acquired a blue Tactical Bonus Card. Play these cards at the beginning of a Conquest battle, such as a capital attack, to gain a bonus. Move your mouse over the card to see the bonus. Bonus cards may only be played once.

ENTRY.name: HELP_STRATEGIC_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: You have acquired a red Strategic Bonus Card. Click a card, then click the territory you wish to use it on. Move your mouse over the card to see the bonus. Bonus cards may only be played once.

ENTRY.name: HELP_BARBARIAN
ENTRY.STRING: You have selected a barbarian nation to attack. To win a barbarian scenario, you may need to achieve special victory conditions. Click the "Start" button to begin the battle.

ENTRY.name: HELP_TRIBUTE
ENTRY.STRING: The Tribute number shows the amount of Tribute in your treasury. Tribute may be used to purchase bonus cards, increase territory strength, and negotiate treaties. Increase your Tribute by conquering territories with the tribute icon #ICON19 or by ending your turn without fighting a battle.

ENTRY.name: HELP_SUPPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Black arrows show where a friendly army can help support an attack. Supporting armies contribute citizens or reinforcements to a battle. If you have at least two more armies involved in an attack than the defenders, you automatically win the territory without having to fight. Capital territories cannot be overrun.

ENTRY.name: HELP_MOVE
ENTRY.STRING: Blue arrows show where your army can move within your own nation.

ENTRY.name: HELP_RARE_RES
ENTRY.STRING: Certain territories contain rare resources. During a Conquest battle, you receive the benefits and income provided by all the rare resources in the territories under your control.

ENTRY.name: HELP_WONDER_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: You have acquired a bonus card that allows you to construct a Wonder of the World! To construct the Wonder, drag the bonus card to the territory where you wish to build the Wonder. When fighting a battle, you receive the benefits provided by all the Wonders under your control after you have researched $NUM0 Civics technologies. Bonus cards may only be played once.

ENTRY.name: HELP_STARTING_ALLY
ENTRY.STRING: The $TRIBE0 are your allies. Working together towards your common goals can prove rewarding in the long run.

ENTRY.name: HELP_STARTING_VASSAL
ENTRY.STRING: The $TRIBE0 are a client state. You can move through their territory and will gain income from controlling them.

ENTRY.name: HELP_STARTING_VASSAL_PLURAL
ENTRY.STRING: The $TRIBE0 is a client state. You can move through their territory and will gain income from controlling them.

ENTRY.name: HELP_ESPIONAGE
ENTRY.STRING: Click on the Espionage button to begin an Espionage attempt. You may do this once per turn. Performing Espionage allows you to launch surreptitious attacks against your enemy. Performing Espionage does not count as your one attack for this turn.

ENTRY.name: ENEMY
ENTRY.STRING: Enemy ArmyEnemy armies can attack your territories and defend their own. Nearby enemy armies can contribute troops to a battle.

ENTRY.name: ICON_STRENGTH
ENTRY.STRING: Territory Strength: $NUM0Territories gain strength for every Age that they are held by the same nation. Territories with higher strength are more difficult to attack. Increased territory strength can also be purchased with Tribute.

ENTRY.name: ICON_WONDER
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON16 Wonder$WONDERNAME0$WONDERDESC1

ENTRY.name: ICON_BONUS
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON17 Bonus Card#BONUSCARDNAME0Conquer territories to gain bonus cards. Bonus cards can help develop your nation or improve your odds for the next battle.

ENTRY.name: ICON_RARE
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON18 Rare Resource$RARERESNAME0$RARERESINCOME1$RAREDESC2During a Conquest battle, you receive the benefits and income of all the rare resources in the territories under your control.

ENTRY.name: ICON_TRIBUTE
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON19 Tribute: $NUMBER0Tribute is gained when a territory is conquered. Tribute is used to purchase bonus cards, increase territory strength, and make diplomatic deals.

ENTRY.name: ICON_SUPPLY
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON15 Supply CenterSupply centers enable a nation to support extra armies. A nation may have one army for each supply center it controls.

ENTRY.name: ICON_NUKE
ENTRY.STRING: #ICON109 Nuclear MissileTurn the key and click the big red button to launch your missiles. Beware that your enemy will likely launch its missiles in retaliation. More nuclear missiles may be purchased in the top left interface.

ENTRY.name: ARMY
ENTRY.STRING: ArmyClick to select.Armies are used to attack adjacent territories or defend your own. Only one army may attack per turn. Nearby armies can contribute troops to a battle.

ENTRY.name: ARMY_UNAVAIL
ENTRY.STRING: ArmyA new army may not be moved.Armies are used to attack adjacent territories or defend your own. Only one army may attack per turn. Nearby armies can contribute troops to a battle.

ENTRY.name: ARROW_ATTACK
ENTRY.STRING: Attack ArrowGreen arrows show territories that may be attacked.

ENTRY.name: ARROW_MOVE
ENTRY.STRING: Move ArrowBlue arrows show where your army can move within your own nation.

ENTRY.name: ARROW_SUPPORT
ENTRY.STRING: Support ArrowBlack arrows show when a friendly army can help support an attack.

ENTRY.name: BATTLE_INFO
ENTRY.STRING:
{Your Advisor's Briefing}
[Attacking from $COUNTRY0 to $COUNTRY1]

ENTRY.name: START_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Start BattleClick to begin a scenario with your currently attacking army.

ENTRY.name: BONUS_CARDS
ENTRY.STRING: Bonus CardsConquer new territories to gain bonus cards.

ENTRY.name: RESOURCES
ENTRY.STRING: Rare ResourcesConquer new territories to gain new rare resources.

ENTRY.name: DIPLO_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: DiplomacyClick to enter diplomacy with other nations.

ENTRY.name: CENTER_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Center MapClick to center the view over your nation's capital.

ENTRY.name: WONDERS
ENTRY.STRING: WondersPlay Build Wonder bonus cards or conquer territories containing Wonders to add them to your empire.

ENTRY.name: TRIBUTE_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Tribute: $NUM0Tribute is gained when a territory is conquered. Use Tribute to purchase bonus cards, increase territory strength, and make diplomatic deals.

ENTRY.name: BUILD_BUTTON
ENTRY.STRING: Upgrade territory strengthClick to upgrade a territory's strength, which results in extra cities and buildings during defensive battles.More Tribute needed.

ENTRY.name: RANDOM_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: Buy Bonus CardTribute: {$NUM0}Click to purchase one bonus card. You will be given three cards from which to choose.More tribute needed.

ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in territories you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.name: TIMBER
ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in the territories that you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in the territories that you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in the territories that you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in the territories that you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.name: OIL
ENTRY.STRING: Rare Resource Income$RARERESNAME0: +$NUM0This income comes from rare resources in the territories that you control. These resources will give you bonus powers and income during Conquest battles.

ENTRY.name: NEWS_BUTTON

ENTRY.name: RULES_BUTTON

ENTRY.STRING: OptionsClick to display Conquer the World game options.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to display additional Wonders of the World.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to display additional Wonders of the World.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to display additional rare resources.

ENTRY.name: NUKE_KEY
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Launch KeyClick to turn the key and enable the launch button. The key must be activated before a launch can be performed.

ENTRY.name: NUKE_LAUNCH
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Launch ButtonAfter the Nuclear Launch Key has been turned, click to launch your nuclear missiles. Keep in mind that your enemy will also launch their missiles in retaliation.

ENTRY.name: BUY_NUKE
ENTRY.STRING: Buy Nuclear MissileClick to purchase a nuclear missile for $NUM0 tribute.

ENTRY.name: ESPIONAGE
ENTRY.STRING: Espionage MissionsClick to perform espionage against your enemy. This option is only available while you are not at war with the enemy.

ENTRY.name: INCOME
ENTRY.STRING: Industry IncomeIncome is generated from the combined territory strength of all territories in a nation.

ENTRY.name: OPP_INCOME
ENTRY.STRING: Enemy Industry IncomeIncome is generated from the combined territory strength of all territories in a nation.

ENTRY.name: STRATEGIC_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: Click this Strategic Card and drop it onto a territory to use its bonus.Available locations are highlighted.

ENTRY.name: STRATEGIC_CARD_DISABLED
ENTRY.STRING: You are currently unable to play strategic cards.

ENTRY.name: TACTICAL_CARD
ENTRY.STRING: Tactical Card can be played to gain bonuses during battles.You will be prompted to play tactical bonus cards when you start a scenario.

ENTRY.name: DEFCON
ENTRY.STRING: DEFCON meterThe current DEFCON level is $NUM0. The lower the level, the greater the danger of global thermonuclear war.

ENTRY.name: NUM_NUKES
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Missile StockpileYou currently possess $NUM0 Nuclear Missile(s).

ENTRY.name: NUM_NUKES_SINGULAR
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Missile StockpileYou currently possess $NUM0 Nuclear Missile(s).

ENTRY.name: OPP_NUM_NUKES
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Missile StockpileYour enemy currently possesses $NUM0 Nuclear Missile(s).

ENTRY.name: OPP_NUM_NUKES_SINGULAR
ENTRY.STRING: Nuclear Missile StockpileYour enemy currently possesses $NUM0 Nuclear Missile(s).

ENTRY.name: NUKE_TURNS
ENTRY.STRING: $NUM0 turns before missile launchYour missiles as well as your enemy's will be launched in $NUM1 turns.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to see a graph of resources over time.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to see research data.

ENTRY.STRING: Click to see economic data.

ENTRY.name: TAB_CHART_MILITARY
ENTRY.STRING: Click to see military data.
